Global criteria pass (healthy adults; whole-group univariate whole-brain contrast), but the task/contrast is joint bimanual movement role contrast ("Leading versus Following") and does not itself measure a social-processing construct (missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1 and SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2). Construct evidence span: "contrast 'Leading versus Following'".

Global criteria pass, but no affiliation/attachment manipulation or bonding/cooperation/relationship contrast is present (missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2). Construct evidence span: "Leading versus Following".

Global criteria pass, but contrast is not about receiving/producing communicative signals (e.g., faces/gaze/gesture communication); it is motor-role control (missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2). Construct evidence span: "Leading versus Following".

Global criteria pass, but there is no explicit self-referential/agency judgment contrast (e.g., Self>Other); it's leading vs following motor role (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "Leading versus Following".

Global criteria pass, but although following may involve sensitivity to partner cues, this analysis does not operationalize perception/mentalizing about others with an explicit social-cognitive contrast; it's a motor-role contrast (missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2). Construct evidence span: "Leading versus Following".

Global criteria pass (healthy adults; whole-brain regression on task contrast), but the regressor is "leader skill" and the construct is motor-role expertise rather than a social-processing task/contrast (missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2). Construct evidence span: "correlated with the covariate 'leader skill'".

Global criteria pass, but no affiliation/attachment manipulation or relationship/bonding contrast is present; this is expertise regression (missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2). Construct evidence span: "covariate 'leader skill'".

Global criteria pass, but analysis does not index social communication (faces/gaze/gesture comprehension/production); it indexes leading expertise effects on motor role contrast (missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2). Construct evidence span: "correlated with the covariate 'leader skill'".

Global criteria pass, but no explicit self-referential/agency judgment contrast is tested; it's a regression on motor leading expertise (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "covariate 'leader skill'".

Global criteria pass, but no explicit perception/mental-state reasoning about others contrast; regression is on leader expertise within leading vs following (missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2). Construct evidence span: "covariate 'leader skill'".

Global criteria pass (healthy adults; whole-brain regression), but this is the deactivation map for the leader-skill regression and does not operationalize social processing (missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2). Construct evidence span: "Deactivation".

Global criteria pass, but no affiliation/attachment construct is measured; it's a deactivation map tied to leader-skill regression (missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2). Construct evidence span: "Deactivation".

Global criteria pass, but no social-communication task/contrast; deactivation map from leader-skill regression (missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2). Construct evidence span: "Deactivation".

Global criteria pass, but no self-referential/agency judgment contrast; deactivation map from leader-skill regression (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "Deactivation".

Global criteria pass, but no explicit perception/mentalizing-about-others contrast; deactivation map from leader-skill regression (missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2). Construct evidence span: "Deactivation".

Global criteria pass (healthy adults; whole-brain regression), but the regressor is "follower skill" within a motor role contrast and does not itself constitute a social-processing task/contrast (missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2). Construct evidence span: "correlated with the covariate 'follower skill'".

Global criteria pass, but no affiliation/attachment manipulation (e.g., bonding/cooperation/relationship) is present; it's an expertise regression (missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2). Construct evidence span: "covariate 'follower skill'".

Global criteria pass, but no social communication paradigm/contrast; it's an expertise regression on leading vs following (missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2). Construct evidence span: "covariate 'follower skill'".

Global criteria pass, but no explicit self-perception/agency/self-knowledge contrast; regression on follower skill (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "covariate 'follower skill'".

Global criteria pass, but despite mentions of TPJ/pSTS/mPFC, the analysis does not directly manipulate perception/understanding of others as a task construct; it is motor-role contrast modulated by follower expertise (missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2). Construct evidence span: "correlated with the covariate 'follower skill'".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults and group-level whole-brain task-evoked emotional vs neutral contrast during an empathy/emotion rating task. Construct evidence span: "emotional>neutral video clips".

Global criteria pass, but the task/contrast is not about bonding, attachment, kinship, cooperation/competition, or relationship formation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: participants view a person communicating autobiographical emotional events (audiovisual social signal) and judge the target's emotional intensity; this is receptive social-communicative/emotion recognition processing. Construct evidence span: "emotional>neutral video clips".

Global criteria pass, but the analysis does not involve self-referential judgments/agency/self-knowledge contrasts (no Self>Other or self-evaluation). Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: empathic accuracy task requires tracking and inferring others' emotional states (mental state understanding) while watching targets. Construct evidence span: "emotional>neutral video clips".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task-evoked contrast (neutral vs emotional) within a social/empathy task, capturing social processing differences. Construct evidence span: "neutral>emotional video clips".

Global criteria pass, but neutral vs emotional clips do not operationalize affiliation/attachment or social bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: both conditions involve receptive processing of an interacting/communicating target (speech, facial cues); this contrast still indexes social-communicative processing differences (emotional content removed). Construct evidence span: "neutral>emotional video clips".
Global criteria pass, but no self-focused judgment or self-referential manipulation is tested at the task/contrast level. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: participants still perceive/interpret the target person and their (neutral) state/content; contrast examines other-perception differences between neutral and emotional narratives. Construct evidence span: "neutral>emotional video clips".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain parametric modulation of task-evoked activity by empathic accuracy during social/empathy processing. Construct evidence span: "Positively related to Z-EA scores".

Global criteria pass, but Z-EA modulation reflects empathic accuracy/mentalising rather than affiliation/attachment or bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: empathic accuracy depends on decoding communicated emotional information from targets (receptive social communication), and this analysis tests brain activity tracking that decoding success. Construct evidence span: "Positively related to Z-EA scores".

Global criteria pass, but the regressor/modulator is empathic accuracy about the target, not self-judgment or self-referential processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: parametric modulation by empathic accuracy directly indexes understanding others' emotional states over time. Construct evidence span: "Positively related to Z-EA scores".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain parametric modulation by empathic accuracy during a social/empathy task, here capturing regions negatively tracking empathic accuracy. Construct evidence span: "Negatively related to Z-EA scores".

Global criteria pass, but negative modulation by empathic accuracy is not an affiliation/attachment manipulation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: empathic accuracy during decoding of a target's communicated emotion is a receptive social-communication process; negative modulation still reflects variation in that decoding success. Construct evidence span: "Negatively related to Z-EA scores".
Global criteria pass, but no explicit self-perception/self-knowledge contrast; modulation is based on accuracy about others. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: negative modulation by Z-EA scores still indexes processes tied to inferring others' emotional states (inverse relationship). Construct evidence span: "Negatively related to Z-EA scores".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain voxelwise correlation between BOLD and participants' continuous ratings of the target's emotional intensity during social stimulus viewing. Construct evidence span: "Positive correlation with participants' emotional intensity ratings".

Global criteria pass, but continuous ratings of another's emotional intensity are not an affiliation/attachment or bonding measure. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: participants decode socially communicated emotional cues from video narratives; correlation with perceived intensity reflects receptive social-communication processing of emotional signals. Construct evidence span: "Positive correlation with participants' emotional intensity ratings".

Global criteria pass, but the regressor is perceived intensity of the target's emotion, not self-focused affect/trait judgments. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: analysis correlates brain activity with perceived emotional intensity of another person, directly measuring perception/understanding of others' affective states. Construct evidence span: "Positive correlation with participants' emotional intensity ratings".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain voxelwise negative correlation between BOLD and participants' continuous ratings of the target's emotional intensity during the social task. Construct evidence span: "Negative correlation with participants' emotional intensity ratings".

Global criteria pass, but this analysis concerns inverse coupling with perceived emotional intensity, not affiliation/attachment constructs. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: negative coupling with decoded emotional intensity from communicated video narratives still reflects receptive social-communication processing differences. Construct evidence span: "Negative correlation with participants' emotional intensity ratings".
Global criteria pass, but no self-referential task manipulation; ratings concern the target. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: negative correlation with perceived emotional intensity of the target directly indexes other-perception/mental state representation (inverse relationship). Construct evidence span: "Negative correlation with participants' emotional intensity ratings".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate main effect). Task involves perceiving/labeling emotional expressions in face/voice modalities, i.e., social cue processing. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of modality".

Global criteria met, but task/contrast is emotion perception across modalities and does not assess bonding/affiliation/attachment. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate main effect). Modality main effect in an emotion-perception task using facial expressions and vocal prosody falls under receptive social communication (facial/non-facial affect recognition). Construct evidence span: "Main effect of modality".

Global criteria met, but no self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge manipulation; participants judge stimulus valence, not self. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate main effect). Participants perceive and categorize others' emotional expressions (anger/happy/neutral) from face/voice—understanding others’ affective states. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of modality".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate main effect). Main effect of emotion in an emotion-perception task is a core social processing component (processing social affect cues). Construct evidence span: "Main effect of emotion".

Global criteria met, but emotion-category main effect is not about attachment/affiliation bonds or relationship-related evaluation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Meets global criteria. Main effect of emotion during recognition of facial/vocal emotional expressions reflects receptive social communication (affect recognition). Construct evidence span: "Main effect of emotion".
Global criteria met, but analysis does not involve self-evaluation or self-related processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Meets global criteria. Emotion main effect reflects perceiving others’ emotional states from expressive cues. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of emotion".

Excluded because this is an interaction effect (Emotion×Modality), which is a type of interaction contrast. Exclusion criterion applies: GLOBAL_E3. Construct evidence span: "3 (Emotion)×3 (Modality) interaction effects".
Excluded because this is an interaction effect (Emotion×Modality). Exclusion criterion applies: GLOBAL_E3.

Healthy adult sample and whole-brain task-evoked univariate contrast. Contrast is explicitly social role judgment about self relative to another person. Construct evidence span: "Self- vs. Celebrity-judgments".

Does not specifically index bonding/attachment; it is self-referential social attribute judgment rather than loved-one/attachment/cooperation. Construct evidence span: "Self- vs. Celebrity-judgments". Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

No communicative exchange or face/voice/gaze signaling; task is trait/role self-judgment. Construct evidence span: "Self- vs. Celebrity-judgments". Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Whole-brain self-referential judgment contrast (self attributes vs other person), which directly measures self-knowledge/self-evaluation. Construct evidence span: "Self- vs. Celebrity-judgments".

Although the comparison target is another person (celebrity), the intended psychological process is self-judgment rather than understanding others. Construct evidence span: "Self- vs. Celebrity-judgments". Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adult sample and whole-brain task-evoked univariate contrast. Reflecting on a friend's social roles is a social-cognitive (other-referential) task. Construct evidence span: "Friend- vs. Celebrity-judgments".

Friend vs celebrity involves a close other but the task is role/trait judgment, not attachment/affiliation bonding manipulation (e.g., loved one vs stranger, cooperation/competition). Construct evidence span: "Friend- vs. Celebrity-judgments". Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

No explicit communication signals (faces/voices/gaze) or interaction; it is semantic trait/role judgment about a person. Construct evidence span: "Friend- vs. Celebrity-judgments". Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

This contrast targets other-referential processing (friend) rather than self-referential evaluation. Construct evidence span: "Friend- vs. Celebrity-judgments". Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Whole-brain contrast involves judging whether social roles describe a friend (other mental/trait representation) relative to another person. Construct evidence span: "Friend- vs. Celebrity-judgments".

Fails global social-task requirement: contrast is erotic pictures (sexual stimuli) rather than a social-processing task. Missing GLOBAL_I2 and SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1/SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Female > Male Pictures" (Heterosexual Women).

Not an affiliation/attachment task (no bonding/relationship manipulation). Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Female > Male Pictures" (Heterosexual Women).

Not a social communication contrast (no faces/communication-specific manipulation; erotic picture gender contrast). Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Female > Male Pictures" (Heterosexual Women).
Not self-referential processing; passive viewing/ratings of erotic images. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Female > Male Pictures" (Heterosexual Women).

Although stimuli depict other people, the contrast targets sexual arousal to stimulus sex, not reasoning about others' mental states/actions. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Female > Male Pictures" (Heterosexual Women).

Fails social-task/construct: erotic picture sex contrast, not social processing. Missing GLOBAL_I2 and SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1/SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Male > Female Pictures" (Heterosexual Women).

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain partnered>non-partnered contrast) and captures social interaction/joint action with partner requiring social cue processing/mentalizing. Construct evidence span: "partnered versus non-partnered (Leading + Following + Mutual > Solo + Alone)".

No clear task/contrast measuring affiliation/attachment (bonding, kinship, cooperation/competition as primary construct); this is joint motor interaction with haptic coupling rather than affiliation manipulation. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Partnered haptic joint action involves exchange of movement information via touch (non-facial receptive/interactive communication). Construct evidence span: "partnered versus non-partnered (Leading + Following + Mutual > Solo + Alone)".

Contrast is not designed to isolate self-agency/self-knowledge; it collapses across leading/following/mutual and reflects general partnering/tactile effects. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Partnered (vs solo) condition entails tracking/understanding partner’s actions/intentions during joint action; includes action perception/mentalizing components. Construct evidence span: "partnered versus non-partnered (Leading + Following + Mutual > Solo + Alone)".

Although within a social paradigm, this contrast targets leader-specific motor planning after removing shared partnering effects; does not measure social processing per se. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
No affiliation/attachment manipulation; leader-specific motor planning contrast. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Contrast isolates leading-specific motor production beyond shared partnering; not specifically communication signaling or reception. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Leading emphasizes self-initiated action/agency (internally driven movement generation) within joint action. Construct evidence span: "Leading > Conjunction".
Not focused on perceiving/understanding partner; removes shared partnering and highlights self-initiation/motor planning. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Following-specific effects reflect externally oriented responsiveness to partner cues during joint action (social interaction). Construct evidence span: "Following > Conjunction".

No explicit affiliation/attachment construct manipulation (bonding/relationship/cooperation vs competition) in this contrast. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Following involves receptive decoding of partner’s haptic movement cues (non-facial communication reception). Construct evidence span: "Following > Conjunction".
Contrast emphasizes externally-driven following rather than self-agency/self-knowledge. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Following requires action perception/understanding of other’s ongoing movement intentions to synchronize. Construct evidence span: "Following > Conjunction".
Mutual interaction recruits mentalizing/reward networks in a symmetric joint action context, reflecting social interaction processing. Construct evidence span: "Mutual > Conjunction".

Despite reward/positive interaction framing, the task/contrast is not designed to measure affiliation/attachment (no bonding/kinship/close-other manipulation). Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Mutual condition entails bidirectional coordination via haptic exchange (interactive nonverbal communication). Construct evidence span: "Mutual > Conjunction".
Not a self-vs-other judgment or agency isolation; emphasizes mutual/mentalizing rather than self-focused processing. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Mutual coordination with ambiguous agency increases reasoning about partner intentions (mentalizing). Construct evidence span: "Mutual > Conjunction".
Improvisation contrast (Leading+Solo > Mutual+Alone) targets motor improvisation/working memory, not social processing. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
No affiliation/attachment construct; purely motor improvisation contrast. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not a communication contrast; includes solo condition and focuses on improvisation. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

While improvisation is self-generated, the contrast is not framed as self-perception/agency judgment but as improvisation vs prelearned. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Does not involve perceiving/understanding others; includes solo actions. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Self-initiation contrast is about internally vs externally triggered movement, not social processing. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
No affiliation/attachment construct. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not measuring communication; focuses on self-initiated motor control. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Contrast directly targets self-initiated (agency-related) action vs externally-triggered following. Construct evidence span: "self-initiated versus externally-triggered ([Leading + Solo + Mutual + Alone] > Following)".
Not focused on representing others; rather contrasts self-initiated actions against following. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Joint improvisation analysis (Leading > Solo; and higher-order contrasts) primarily indexes additive effects of partnering/touch with improvisation; not a clear social-processing contrast beyond basic partnering. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
No affiliation/attachment manipulation. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Leading > Solo mainly captures physical contact/partner presence rather than explicit communicative exchange; and other joint-improvisation contrasts yielded no effects. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Leading > Solo is not an agency/self-perception isolation; it is partnering/touch difference. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Leading > Solo does not specifically assess understanding others; it reflects presence of partner/tactile interaction and no interaction effects were found. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Meets global criteria; cooperativity is a core affiliative/prosocial interaction dimension. Construct evidence span: "Cooperativity>affectivity".

Global criteria met, but no self-focused judgments or self stimuli. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Affectivity>cooperativity".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast of retrieval for collaboratively learned labels vs arbitrary. Task is communicative/collaborative and invokes mentalizing/social context. Construct evidence span: "Collaborative > Arbitrary".

Contrast is not designed to measure affiliation/attachment, bonding, or close relationship/cooperation constructs; it manipulates collaborative vs arbitrary learning context without attachment/affiliation outcome. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Although the overall paradigm is a communication game, this contrast (collaboratively learned label retrieval vs arbitrary) is about memory retrieval/learning context and not a social-communication-specific manipulation (e.g., faces, gaze, prosody, communicative cues). Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-referential judgment/agency manipulation; contrast is label retrieval across learning contexts. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Collaborative learning/retrieval is hypothesized to engage theory-of-mind/understanding others vs arbitrary learning; includes mentalizing-related regions (mPFC/TPJ/precuneus) at group whole-brain level. Construct evidence span: "Collaborative > Arbitrary".

Although global criteria are met (healthy adults; whole-brain contrast), this contrast targets self-generated semantic associations (individual > arbitrary) rather than social processing. Missing: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
Not an affiliation/attachment task or contrast. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not a social-communication-specific manipulation; contrast is individual vs arbitrary learning context during retrieval. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No explicit self-referential/agency/self-knowledge manipulation; individual condition is self-generated labels but not self-perception. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
No explicit reasoning about others/mental states in this contrast (individual encoding lacks partner). Missing: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adults; group whole-brain contrast isolates effect of collaborative (social) vs individual learning context on later retrieval, engaging social-cognition network. Construct evidence span: "Collaborative > Individual".

Collaboration here is for task communication, not measuring bonding/attachment/cooperation vs competition with affective/relationship content. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Contrast is about retrieval differences due to collaborative vs individual encoding, not a manipulation of communicative signals or receptive/expressive social-communication processes (faces/gaze/emotion etc.). Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-referential judgment/agency contrast. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Directly tests collaborative vs individual context; authors interpret activation as theory of mind/mentalizing (understanding others) with rTPJ/mPFC/precuneus involvement. Construct evidence span: "Collaborative > Individual".

Whole-brain contrast is about arbitrary associative retrieval vs individual self-generated retrieval (hippocampal/effort), not social processing. Missing: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
Not affiliation/attachment. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not a social-communication manipulation. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-perception manipulation. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
No others/mentalizing manipulation. Missing: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Reverse of collaborative>individual; still a whole-brain contrast indexing effect of collaborative (social) vs individual learning context on retrieval (regions more active for individual than collaborative). Construct evidence span: "Individual > Collaborative".
Not an affiliation/attachment construct contrast. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not measuring social communication signals; contrast reflects learning-context effects on retrieval. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-judgment/agency manipulation. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Contrast differs by presence/absence of collaboration with another person at encoding; even though direction is individual>collaborative, it still pertains to mentalizing/others-related processing differences tied to collaboration. Construct evidence span: "Individual > Collaborative".

Whole-brain contrast compares arbitrary vs collaborative learning context; collaborative is social and contrast indexes differences attributable to social/collaborative encoding on retrieval. Construct evidence span: "Arbitrary > Collaborative".
Not an attachment/affiliation manipulation. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not a social-communication construct contrast (no specific communicative cue processing); focuses on associative retrieval differences. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-referential manipulation. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Compares conditions that differ in collaborative partner involvement; even in arbitrary>collaborative direction, contrast pertains to differential recruitment of mentalizing/others-related processes attributable to collaboration. Construct evidence span: "Arbitrary > Collaborative".

Laughter vs silence indexes social feedback/reward salience but not affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship/kinship/cooperation constructs. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Group minus No Laughter".

Laughter is a non-facial auditory communicative signal; this contrast captures reception of socially communicative vocalizations (laughter vs silence). Construct evidence span: "Group minus No Laughter".

Contrast varies listener response (laughter vs none) collapsed across speaker; it does not isolate self-referential judgments/agency. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Group minus No Laughter".

Does not involve inferring others’ mental states; primarily sensory/social signal processing of laughter. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Group minus No Laughter".

Meets global criteria and examines response to social auditory feedback (single-person laughter) in a social interaction setting. Construct evidence span: "Single minus No Laughter [(SELF_ Single - SELF_No) + (OTHER_Single - OTHER_No)]".

Single laughter vs silence is social feedback but not an affiliation/attachment or bonding manipulation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Single minus No Laughter".
Captures reception of non-facial social communication signal (auditory laughter) relative to silence. Construct evidence span: "Single minus No Laughter".
Collapsed across SELF/OTHER; does not isolate self-related processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Single minus No Laughter".

No explicit mental-state/trait reasoning about others; primarily auditory social signal processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Single minus No Laughter".

Meets global criteria and compares degrees of social auditory feedback (group vs single laughter) during social interaction. Construct evidence span: "Group minus Single Laughter [(SELF_ Group - SELF_Single) + (OTHER_ Group - OTHER_Single)]".

Group vs single laughter varies intensity/number of laughers rather than affiliation/attachment or relationship-bonding constructs. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Group minus Single Laughter".

Both conditions are communicative auditory signals; contrast indexes processing of socially communicative cues (group laughter vs single laughter). Construct evidence span: "Group minus Single Laughter".

Does not isolate self-referential judgment/agency; it varies listener response type collapsed across speaker. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Group minus Single Laughter".

No explicit inference about others’ mental states; primarily graded processing of laughter. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Group minus Single Laughter".

Meets global criteria and tests social action–outcome contingency (self action paired with others’ laughter) reflecting social reward/interaction processing. Construct evidence span: "[(SELF_ Group - SELF_No) - (OTHER_Group - OTHER_No)]".

This is social reward/contingency rather than attachment/bond formation or cooperation/competition per se. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "action-outcome contingency".

Interaction contrast primarily targets contingency/reward (self vs other × laughter vs none) rather than isolating communicative signal perception per se. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "[(SELF_ Group - SELF_No) - (OTHER_Group - OTHER_No)]".

Interaction explicitly depends on self as agent (SELF vs OTHER) modulating response to feedback, indexing self-agency/outcome monitoring. Construct evidence span: "(SELF_Group - SELF_No) - (OTHER_Group - OTHER_No)".

Does not require representing others’ traits/intentions; other is only a non-self agent baseline. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "SELF vs OTHER".

Meets global criteria and evaluates social action–outcome contingency using single laughter vs no laughter (even though null). Construct evidence span: "[(SELF_ Single - SELF_No) - (OTHER_Single - OTHER_No)]".

Contingency/reward manipulation is not an affiliation/attachment construct. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "action-outcome contingency".

Targets interaction/contingency (SELF vs OTHER × laughter vs none) rather than isolating communicative signal reception. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "[(SELF_ Single - SELF_No) - (OTHER_Single - OTHER_No)]".

Interaction depends on self as agent vs other, indexing self-related agency/outcome monitoring regardless of significance. Construct evidence span: "(SELF_Single - SELF_No) - (OTHER_Single - OTHER_No)".

Other serves as comparison agent but does not involve mental-state reasoning about others. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "SELF vs OTHER".

Contrast compares mentalizing about others (dissimilar vs similar) and does not involve self-referential judgment (no Self > Other). Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Mentalizing with Dissimilar > Similar person".

Mentalizing/theory-of-mind about other persons’ mental states directly matches understanding others. Whole-brain group contrast compares mental state inference demands for dissimilar vs similar targets. Construct evidence span: "Mentalizing with Dissimilar > Similar person".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast). Response-period ToM localizer-style contrast engages social inference with contextual adjustment. Construct evidence span: "Response period" (SocAdj > PhyConf).

No bonding/attachment/cooperation manipulation; contrast reflects ToM/context update demands. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "SocAdj > PhyConf".

Does not involve communicative signals (faces/voices/gaze/gestures); written scenarios with inference selection. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Response period".

No explicit self-referential processing; participants choose causes/intentions, not judging self traits/states. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Response period".
Response period requires selecting the cause/intentions in social scenarios—mental state understanding of others (ToM). Construct evidence span: "Social scenarios requiring contextual adjustment".
Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast). Contrast isolates social vs physical scenario processing. Construct evidence span: "manipulation (Soc > Phy)".

Social vs physical scenarios do not specifically probe attachment/affiliation or social bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Soc > Phy".
No communicative cue perception/production; stimuli are written scenarios. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Soc > Phy".
Contrast is about social vs physical scenarios, not self-referential judgments. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Time-point 1".
Social (intentional action) scenarios imply reasoning about others' intentions/mental states vs non-social physical causality. Construct evidence span: "Soc > Phy".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast). Social vs physical scenario processing at Time-point 2 reflects social inference/mentalizing. Construct evidence span: "manipulation (Soc > Phy)".

No affiliation/attachment construct targeted; contrast is generic social vs physical inference. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Soc > Phy".
Not about decoding communicative signals; written scenario comprehension. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Time-point 2".
No self-related judgments. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Soc > Phy".
Social scenarios involve a character's intentional action; contrast is designed to capture social representations/mental state inferences. Construct evidence span: "Type of scenario (Soc > Phy)".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast). Response-period social vs physical inference captures broad social processing. Construct evidence span: "Response period" (Soc > Phy).

No attachment/affiliation manipulation; contrast is ToM-like social vs physical inference. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Soc > Phy".

No facial/vocal/gaze/gesture communication processing; written scenarios. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Response period".
No self-perception component. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Soc > Phy".
During response, participants decide causes/intentions for social scenarios (mental state understanding) vs physical causality. Construct evidence span: "Type of scenario (Soc > Phy)".

Although global criteria appear met, this contrast isolates contextual adjustment (Adj > Conf) across both social and physical scenarios and is not specifically measuring social processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "manipulation (Adj > Conf)".

No affiliation/attachment manipulation; adjustment vs confirmation is not social bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Adj > Conf".
No communicative signal processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Time-point 2".
No self-related judgments. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Adj > Conf".

Adj > Conf collapses across social and physical trials and at T2 shows no significant whole-brain activation; not a specific others-mental-state inference contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Time-point 2".

Contrast targets contextual adjustment demands (Adj > Conf) across social+physical scenarios; not specifically social processing despite ToM-network overlap. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "manipulation (Adj > Conf)".
No attachment/affiliation content; it is inference updating. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Adj > Conf".
Not communication-related. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Response period".
No self-referential processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Adj > Conf".

Adj > Conf includes physical inferences and does not isolate understanding others' mental states; primary manipulation is contextual updating. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Adj > Conf".

Analysis appears to be a condition label/ROI-style post-hoc (beta extraction for SocAdj) rather than a group-level whole-brain univariate contrast map. Fails GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "SocAdj".
Not a whole-brain group contrast and does not assess attachment/affiliation. Fails GLOBAL_I2; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "SocAdj".
Not a whole-brain group contrast and not a communication task. Fails GLOBAL_I2; missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "SocAdj".
Not a whole-brain group contrast and not self-referential. Fails GLOBAL_I2; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "SocAdj".
Although SocAdj involves ToM, this entry is not specified as a whole-brain group-level contrast/stat map. Fails GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "SocAdj".

Fails GLOBAL_I2 and not communication. Construct evidence span: "SocConf". Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and not self-referential. Construct evidence span: "SocConf". Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Physical adjustment condition label/post-hoc rather than group-level whole-brain univariate contrast map; fails GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "PhyAdj".

Fails GLOBAL_I2 because this contrast (SA vs. SC) has no reported significant whole-brain activations ("No significant activation was found in the contrasts of SA vs. SC"). Missing required inclusion: GLOBAL_I2; local: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Fails GLOBAL_I2 because this contrast (SA vs. SC) has no reported significant whole-brain activations ("No significant activation was found in the contrasts of SA vs. SC"). Missing required inclusion: GLOBAL_I2; local: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Fails GLOBAL_I2 because this contrast (SA vs. SC) has no reported significant whole-brain activations ("No significant activation was found in the contrasts of SA vs. SC"). Missing required inclusion: GLOBAL_I2; local: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Fails GLOBAL_I2 because this contrast (SA vs. SC) has no reported significant whole-brain activations ("No significant activation was found in the contrasts of SA vs. SC"). Missing required inclusion: GLOBAL_I2; local: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults and group-level whole-brain task contrast on social trait evaluation. Construct evidence span: "SC vs. SA".

Although whole-brain contrast is present, the task is self trait evaluation (communion vs agency) rather than affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship formation. Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "SC vs. SA".

Not a social communication (faces/gaze/voice) paradigm; it is trait-word self-evaluation. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "SC vs. SA".

Self-referential trait judgment (communion vs agency) constitutes self-knowledge/agency-related self-processing. Construct evidence span: "Differences in neural response... during the self-evaluation (SC vs. SA)".
Contrast is within self-evaluation (no reasoning about others’ states/traits). Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "SC vs. SA".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast involving endorsement of others’ evaluation of oneself (social feedback context). Construct evidence span: "OA vs. OC".

Endorsing others’ trait evaluations is social feedback/self-reflection but not specifically affiliation/attachment bonding (e.g., loved-one/relationship, cooperation/competition). Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "OA vs. OC".

No communicative cue processing (faces/voices/gaze); uses trait words and agreement ratings. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "OA vs. OC".
Task requires evaluating self in light of others’ feedback (reflected self-knowledge). Construct evidence span: "endorsement of others’ agentic evaluation of oneself (OA)".

Although framed as others’ evaluations, participants are not inferring others’ mental states/traits; they are agreeing/disagreeing about the self. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "endorsement of others’ ... evaluation of oneself".

Fails GLOBAL_I2 because this contrast (OC vs. OA) has no reported significant whole-brain activations ("No significant activation was found in the contrasts of ... OC vs. OA"). Missing required inclusion: GLOBAL_I2; local: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.

Fails GLOBAL_I2 because this contrast (OC vs. OA) has no reported significant whole-brain activations ("No significant activation was found in the contrasts of ... OC vs. OA"). Missing required inclusion: GLOBAL_I2; local: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Fails GLOBAL_I2 because this contrast (OC vs. OA) has no reported significant whole-brain activations ("No significant activation was found in the contrasts of ... OC vs. OA"). Missing required inclusion: GLOBAL_I2; local: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Fails GLOBAL_I2 because this contrast (OC vs. OA) has no reported significant whole-brain activations ("No significant activation was found in the contrasts of ... OC vs. OA"). Missing required inclusion: GLOBAL_I2; local: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Fails GLOBAL_I2 because this contrast (OC vs. OA) has no reported significant whole-brain activations ("No significant activation was found in the contrasts of ... OC vs. OA"). Missing required inclusion: GLOBAL_I2; local: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast) and indexes social interaction/retaliation under provocation. Construct evidence span: "Aggressive reaction to provoking opponent > non aggressive reaction to non-provoking opponent".

Task is competitive/retaliatory aggression rather than bonding/attachment; missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Aggressive reaction to provoking opponent > non aggressive reaction to non-provoking opponent".

Does not primarily measure communicative signal reception/production (faces, gaze, prosody); missing local inclusion criteria SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Aggressive reaction to provoking opponent > non aggressive reaction to non-provoking opponent".

Contrast targets retaliatory choice toward opponents, not explicit self-referential judgment/agency manipulation; missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Aggressive reaction to provoking opponent > non aggressive reaction to non-provoking opponent".

Contrast depends on evaluating opponent type (provoking vs non-provoking) and responding accordingly, implicating representation of others’ intentions/traits. Construct evidence span: "provoking opponent" vs "non-provoking opponent" in "Aggressive reaction to provoking opponent > non aggressive reaction to non-provoking opponent".

Meets global criteria and reflects differential responding within a social interaction (non-aggressive response to non-provoking opponent vs aggressive to provoking opponent). Construct evidence span: "Non aggressive reaction to non-provoking opponent > aggressive reaction to provoking opponent".

Not an affiliation/attachment manipulation; primarily retaliation/aggression. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Non aggressive reaction to non-provoking opponent > aggressive reaction to provoking opponent".

No communicative cue processing contrast (e.g., faces/voices/gaze). Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Non aggressive reaction to non-provoking opponent > aggressive reaction to provoking opponent".

Not self-referential evaluation; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Non aggressive reaction to non-provoking opponent > aggressive reaction to provoking opponent".

Requires distinguishing opponent provocation level and tailoring response, engaging understanding of others’ behavior/traits. Construct evidence span: "non-provoking opponent" vs "provoking opponent".

Although global criteria are met, contrast primarily indexes generic reward/feedback (win vs loss) rather than social processing per se; missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2 (must measure Social Processing). Construct evidence span: "Won > lost".
Win>loss does not measure affiliation/attachment; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Won > lost".

Not explicit self-referential/agency judgment; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Won > lost".
Does not require representing others’ mental/emotional states; missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Won > lost".

Not affiliation/attachment; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Lost > won".

Although attachment is measured as an individual-differences variable, this specific map is not an affiliation/attachment task contrast (no bond/relationship manipulation such as loved one vs stranger). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

This contrast is not a social communication task (no face/voice/gaze/gesture communication processing contrast); it is emotion regulation with therapist-provided reappraisal. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast of a social interaction task (Cyberball inclusion frequency effect). Construct evidence span: "(toss - button press) high-frequency effect".

Global inclusion criteria are met and the contrast indexes increased social inclusion/interaction frequency, a positive social interaction/affiliative reward manipulation. Construct evidence span: "(toss - button press) high-frequency effect".

Global criteria pass, but this contrast is not about communicating signals (faces/voice/gaze/gesture) and instead manipulates quantity of social interaction/reward. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Although precuneus is discussed as self-related, the analyzed contrast is primarily social interaction frequency/reward vs motor control and does not explicitly require self-judgment/agency/self-referential evaluation. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

The contrast does not require inferring others' mental states/emotions (ToM/empathy) and mainly reflects social interaction frequency/reward. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast. Contrast targets socially constituted meaning appraisal engaging social-cognition processes. Construct evidence span: "A Meaning > Physical".

Task is meaning vs physical appraisal of symbolic artifacts, not an affiliation/attachment manipulation (e.g., bonding/cooperation/close others). Missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "A Meaning > Physical".

No communicative signal processing (faces/voices/gestures) or production/reception contrast; it is meaning vs physical property rating of objects. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "A Meaning > Physical".
No self-referential judgment contrast; focuses on meaning vs physical properties across stimuli. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "A Meaning > Physical".

No explicit reasoning about others’ mental states/actions/animacy; contrast is meaning vs physical. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "A Meaning > Physical".

Although global criteria are met (healthy adults; whole-brain), this contrast targets physical property appraisal rather than social processing. Missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1 and SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "B Physical > Meaning".
Physical-property contrast does not assess affiliation/attachment constructs. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "B Physical > Meaning".

No social communication stimuli/contrast (e.g., faces, gaze, gestures); it is physical property evaluation. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "B Physical > Meaning".
Not a self vs other or self-judgment contrast; physical evaluation only. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "B Physical > Meaning".
Does not involve perceiving/inferring others’ states/actions; physical evaluation only. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "B Physical > Meaning".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast within meaning-related task comparing own vs other models, engaging self/other social-cognitive processing. Construct evidence span: "C Own > Other (meaning related task)".

Own vs other here indexes familiarity/ownership/episodic memory more than bonding/attachment; no close-other/affiliation manipulation. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "C Own > Other (meaning related task)".

No communicative cue reception/production contrast; participants rate representational meaning of objects. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "C Own > Other (meaning related task)".

Own vs other contrast in meaning task directly maps to self-related processing (self-referential ownership/agency/autobiographical access). Construct evidence span: "Own > Other (meaning related task)".

Contrast is driven by self-relatedness (own) rather than inference about others’ mental states; 'other' is a baseline category without mental-state judgment. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "C Own > Other (meaning related task)".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain interaction within meaning-related task comparing own-vs-other as a function of collective vs individual, capturing socially contextualized meaning/interaction history. Construct evidence span: "D Own > Other × Collective > Individual (meaning related task)".

Interaction isolates effects specific to participants’ own collectively built models vs other/individual, closely tied to group interaction and reported relatedness (social bonding/affiliation). Construct evidence span: "Own > Other × Collective > Individual".

Although collaboration history is relevant, the in-scanner task is not communicative cue processing/production; it's object meaning rating with an ownership/collective interaction. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "D Own > Other × Collective > Individual (meaning related task)".

Interaction includes an explicit self (own) vs other component within meaning judgments, indexing self-related processing modulated by collective construction experience. Construct evidence span: "Own > Other × Collective > Individual".

Even though 'other' is part of the interaction, the contrast is not about inferring others’ mental states/actions; it's self/ownership and collective-history effects. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "D Own > Other × Collective > Individual (meaning related task)".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain covariate analysis on task-evoked Black>White responses during face viewing in a social cognition/race perception context. Construct evidence span: "Covariate analyses showing individual differences in racial trust disparity predicted by neural response to Black > White faces".

Task involves perceiving faces by race and later trustworthiness ratings; does not directly measure bonding/attachment/cooperation. Missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Face perception task (Black vs White faces) is a receptive social cue processing paradigm; whole-brain activation covaries with social evaluative outcome (racial trust disparity). Construct evidence span: "neural response to Black > White faces".
No self-referential judgments or self-related stimuli/contrasts. Missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Processing others' facial information by social group and relating it to trait evaluation (trustworthiness) concerns perception/understanding of others' traits. Construct evidence span: "racial trust disparity".
Excluded because this is a seed-based connectivity (PPI/gPPI) analysis: "Increased connectivity with orbitofrontal cortex (18, 66, 0)".
Excluded because this is a seed-based connectivity (PPI/gPPI) analysis (GLOBAL_E2).

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain covariate analysis on task-evoked Black>White responses during face viewing; outcome is social cognition construct (racial differentiation disparity in trustworthiness judgments). Construct evidence span: "racial differentiation disparity predicted by neural response to Black > White faces".
No affiliation/attachment manipulation (e.g., bonding, cooperation/competition, close others). Missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Receptive processing of facial cues (faces by race) used to predict later differentiation in trustworthiness ratings; fits face-based social communication reception. Construct evidence span: "neural response to Black > White faces".
No self-referential processing. Missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Concerns perceiving/evaluating others' trait (trustworthiness) and differentiating outgroup vs ingroup faces—an others-focused social perception construct. Construct evidence span: "racial differentiation disparity".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain covariate analysis on task-evoked Black>White responses during face viewing; links neural response to social cognition outcome (less racial differentiation disparity). Construct evidence span: "Increased activation with less differentiation disparity".

No attachment/affiliation task content; primarily race-based face perception and later trustworthiness differentiation. Missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Face-viewing paradigm (receptive facial communication); neural activity predicts differentiation in trustworthiness judgments. Construct evidence span: "Increased activation with less differentiation disparity".
No self-related judgments or self stimuli. Missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Others-focused evaluation/differentiation of trustworthiness from faces; pertains to representing others' traits. Construct evidence span: "less racial differentiation disparity".
Excluded because this is a seed-based connectivity (PPI/gPPI) analysis: "Increased connectivity with dorsolateral prefrontal seed (24, 42, 21)".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast). Task involves processing social-evaluative videos (approval/disapproval) vs neutral, i.e., social information interpretation. Construct evidence span: "whole brain analysis negative > neutral".

Although socially valenced, this contrast tests responses to criticism vs neutral rather than affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship formation; missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "negative > neutral".

Meets global criteria. Participants view dynamic audiovisual communicative stimuli (a person directly speaking evaluative sentences), engaging receptive social communication processes (non-facial + facial cues). Construct evidence span: "watching videos of posers expressing ... statements" / "negative > neutral".

Meets global criteria. Social evaluation is explicitly self-referential (participants imagine the poser addressing them; criticism/praise about 'you'), engaging self-relevant appraisal. Construct evidence span: "social-evaluative" / "negative > neutral".

Meets global criteria. Viewing others delivering evaluative emotional statements requires perceiving others' affective/intentional states (understanding the poser’s evaluation/hostility). Construct evidence span: "disapproving sentences" / "negative > neutral".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast). Contrast isolates processing of positive social evaluation (praise) vs neutral, a social-processing manipulation. Construct evidence span: "whole brain analysis positive > neutral".

Positive evaluation/praise is social but does not operationalize affiliation/attachment (no bonding/relationship/close-other manipulation); missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "positive > neutral".

Meets global criteria. Dynamic audiovisual videos of a person speaking compliments are a receptive social-communication task. Construct evidence span: "positive > neutral" / "videos of posers ... sentences".
Meets global criteria. Praise directed at the participant (imagined interaction) engages self-relevant evaluation/self-appraisal. Construct evidence span: "approving" / "positive > neutral".
Meets global criteria. Requires perceiving the poser’s positive affect and communicative intent (other-related emotional state inference). Construct evidence span: "approving" / "positive > neutral".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task-evoked map). Conjunction identifies common network for positive and negative social evaluation vs neutral—core social processing. Construct evidence span: "conjunction analysis positive > neutral & negative > neutral".

Conjunction reflects general processing of social evaluation (praise/criticism), not affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship formation; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "conjunction ... positive > neutral & negative > neutral".

Meets global criteria. Conjunction driven by processing communicative social-evaluative speech videos (receptive social communication). Construct evidence span: "positive > neutral & negative > neutral".

Meets global criteria. Both contrasts involve self-relevant appraisal of being praised/criticized (imagined direct evaluation), so shared network reflects self-referential social evaluation. Construct evidence span: "positive > neutral & negative > neutral".

Meets global criteria. Shared network also reflects perceiving and interpreting others' emotional/intentional evaluative states across valence. Construct evidence span: "positive > neutral & negative > neutral".

Fails global inclusion for social processing map: contrast is performance feedback valence, not a social-processing task/contrast; also does not measure social processing. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
Contrast is negative vs positive performance feedback and does not assess affiliation/attachment. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Contrast is negative vs positive performance feedback and does not assess social communication. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Contrast concerns processing of performance feedback valence rather than self-knowledge/agency judgments. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Contrast is feedback valence and does not involve reasoning about others’ mental/emotional states. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults examining neural response to receiving empathic vs unempathic social comments (social interaction content). Construct evidence span: "Emotional empathic (EE) > emotional unempathic (EN)".

Although empathy may relate to social support, the contrast is not a direct measure of bonding/attachment or affiliation formation (e.g., loved one vs stranger, cooperation/competition). Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Task involves processing verbally delivered empathic vs unempathic statements (receptive non-facial social communication). Construct evidence span: "Emotional empathic (EE) > emotional unempathic (EN)".
Contrast focuses on processing others' comments rather than explicit self-judgment/self-knowledge or agency manipulations. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Understanding empathic intent/content requires inferring the speaker’s stance toward the participant (others’ mental/emotional states). Construct evidence span: "Emotional empathic (EE) > emotional unempathic (EN)".

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults comparing processing of socially relevant (un)empathic comments; this is a social-processing manipulation. Construct evidence span: "Unempathic (EN + CN) > empathic (EE + CE)".

Contrast does not operationalize affiliation/attachment (no bonding/relationship manipulation such as loved-one, cooperation/competition, or social comparison). Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Stimuli are verbal comments; contrast indexes receptive processing of socially communicative content (empathic vs unempathic). Construct evidence span: "Unempathic (EN + CN) > empathic (EE + CE)".
No explicit self-reflection/self-knowledge contrast; it is primarily about processing incoming comments. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Evaluating (un)empathy entails interpreting another person’s intentions/attitudes and mental state toward the participant. Construct evidence span: "Unempathic (EN + CN) > empathic (EE + CE)".

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults comparing cognitively unempathic vs cognitively empathic social responses; social processing of others’ statements. Construct evidence span: "Cognitive unempathic (CN) > cognitive empathic (CE)".
Not a task/contrast about forming or maintaining social bonds/attachment; it manipulates empathy content in comments. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Verbal comments are the manipulation; contrast reflects receptive processing of communicative statements. Construct evidence span: "Cognitive unempathic (CN) > cognitive empathic (CE)".
Does not require explicit self-evaluation; focuses on other's comment type. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Cognitive (un)empathy statements explicitly reference understanding the recipient’s feelings, requiring appraisal of the other’s understanding/mental state. Construct evidence span: "Cognitive unempathic (CN) > cognitive empathic (CE)".

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults comparing empathic comments to baseline; engages social processing of supportive statements. Construct evidence span: "Empathic (EE + CE) > high level baseline".

Although empathic support can relate to affiliation, this contrast is not designed to measure attachment/affiliation bonding or relationship-specific cues. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Contrast isolates processing of empathic verbal interventions vs baseline; receptive non-facial communication. Construct evidence span: "Empathic (EE + CE) > high level baseline".
No explicit self-related judgment condition; mainly processing others’ comments. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Empathic comments require interpreting the commenter’s empathic stance/intent (other’s mental state). Construct evidence span: "Empathic (EE + CE) > high level baseline".

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults for processing emotionally empathic comments vs baseline; social processing of empathy content. Construct evidence span: "Emotional empathic (EE) > high level baseline".

Not a direct affiliation/attachment measure (no relationship bonding/manipulation like loved one vs stranger or cooperation). Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Stimulus is verbal emotional empathy; contrast reflects receptive processing of social communication. Construct evidence span: "Emotional empathic (EE) > high level baseline".
Does not contrast self-focused appraisal/agency; it contrasts types of received comments. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Emotionally empathic statements convey the speaker’s feelings/concern, requiring inference about other’s affect/intent. Construct evidence span: "Emotional empathic (EE) > high level baseline".

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults for processing cognitively empathic comments vs baseline; social processing of understanding/mentalizing statements. Construct evidence span: "Cognitive empathic (CE) > high level baseline".
Not designed to measure attachment/affiliation formation; focuses on cognitive empathy statements. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Verbal cognitive empathy comments are social communicative stimuli; contrast indexes receptive communication processing. Construct evidence span: "Cognitive empathic (CE) > high level baseline".
No explicit self-knowledge/self-judgment manipulation; rather reception of others’ comments. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Cognitive empathy statements explicitly claim understanding of the participant’s feelings, invoking appraisal of the other’s understanding/mental state. Construct evidence span: "Cognitive empathic (CE) > high level baseline".

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults comparing emotional vs cognitive social comments; both are socially relevant interpersonal statements. Construct evidence span: "Emotional (EE + EN) > cognitive (CE + CN)".
Emotional vs cognitive phrasing is not a direct affiliation/attachment manipulation (no bond/relationship target). Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Contrast is between types of verbally communicated social statements (receptive non-facial communication). Construct evidence span: "Emotional (EE + EN) > cognitive (CE + CN)".
Not an explicit self-perception task; focuses on comment type. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Comments communicate the speaker’s affect/concern vs understanding, requiring inference about the other’s emotional/mental stance. Construct evidence span: "Emotional (EE + EN) > cognitive (CE + CN)".

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults comparing emotionally vs cognitively empathic comments—core social processing of empathy type. Construct evidence span: "Emotional empathic (EE) > cognitive empathic (CE)".
Contrast is about empathy type, not affiliation/attachment or relationship bonding per se. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Both conditions are verbal empathic statements; contrast isolates receptive processing differences in communicative content. Construct evidence span: "Emotional empathic (EE) > cognitive empathic (CE)".
No explicit self-judgment manipulation; primarily processing others’ empathic messages. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Differentiating emotional vs cognitive empathy requires interpreting the speaker’s affective vs mentalizing stance (others’ mental/emotional states). Construct evidence span: "Emotional empathic (EE) > cognitive empathic (CE)".

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults comparing emotionally vs cognitively unempathic comments; social processing of negative interpersonal statements. Construct evidence span: "Emotional unempathic (EN) > cognitive unempathic (CN)".
Not a direct attachment/affiliation construct; it contrasts types of unempathic comments. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Verbal social statements are the manipulation; contrast reflects receptive social communication processing. Construct evidence span: "Emotional unempathic (EN) > cognitive unempathic (CN)".
Does not require self-knowledge/agency judgments; it is about processing received comments. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Unempathic comments convey the other person’s lack of concern/understanding, requiring inference about their attitudes/mental state. Construct evidence span: "Emotional unempathic (EN) > cognitive unempathic (CN)".

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults comparing cognitively vs emotionally unempathic comments; social processing of interpersonal (un)understanding statements. Construct evidence span: "Cognitive unempathic (CN) > emotional unempathic (EN)".
No direct affiliation/attachment manipulation; focuses on type of unempathic comment. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Contrast is between two kinds of verbal social messages; receptive social communication. Construct evidence span: "Cognitive unempathic (CN) > emotional unempathic (EN)".
Not an explicit self-perception contrast; primarily processing others’ statements. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Cognitively unempathic statements directly concern (not) understanding the recipient’s feelings—appraisal of other’s mental state/intent. Construct evidence span: "Cognitive unempathic (CN) > emotional unempathic (EN)".
