Excluded: analysis lacks required global inclusion evidence (not specified as a group-level univariate whole-brain task contrast); missing GLOBAL_I2 and local construct evidence. Construct evidence span: "= 10."
Excluded: analysis lacks required global inclusion evidence (missing GLOBAL_I2) and no affiliation/attachment construct evidence. Construct evidence span: "= 10."
Excluded: analysis lacks required global inclusion evidence (missing GLOBAL_I2) and no social communication construct evidence. Construct evidence span: "= 10."
Excluded: analysis lacks required global inclusion evidence (missing GLOBAL_I2) and no self-perception construct evidence. Construct evidence span: "= 10."
Excluded: analysis lacks required global inclusion evidence (missing GLOBAL_I2) and no ToM/other-perception construct evidence. Construct evidence span: "= 10."
Healthy adults performing affective ToM judgments with/without emotional facial expressions; group-level whole-brain contrast. Construct evidence span: "EFE > no EFE".

Excluded: contrast targets emotional facial expressions during affective ToM, not bonding/affiliation processes (missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2). Construct evidence span: "EFE > no EFE".
Presence of emotional facial expressions (receptive facial communication) modulating activity during ToM judgments; whole-brain univariate contrast. Construct evidence span: "EFE > no EFE".
Excluded: no self-referential judgment or self-focused contrast (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "EFE > no EFE".
Affective ToM requires inferring protagonist’s emotional state; EFE availability modulates neural responses during these other-focused mental state judgments. Construct evidence span: "EFE > no EFE".

Healthy adults performing affective ToM; group-level whole-brain contrast of condition without facial expressions vs with facial expressions during social-cognitive judgments. Construct evidence span: "No EFE > EFE".

Excluded: contrast reflects absence vs presence of facial expressions during ToM, not affiliation/attachment or bonding (missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2). Construct evidence span: "No EFE > EFE".
Manipulates availability of facial expression cues (receptive facial communication) during task; whole-brain univariate contrast. Construct evidence span: "No EFE > EFE".
Excluded: no self-focused processing or self vs other comparison (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "No EFE > EFE".

Even without EFE, task demands inferring protagonist’s affective state (affective ToM); contrast reflects differential recruitment during other-focused mentalizing. Construct evidence span: "No EFE > EFE".

Excluded: correlation analysis with behavior (RT reduction) is not a simple task-evoked group contrast suitable for whole-brain generalizable map per GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Correlation of differential activation EFE > no EFE with RT reduction by EFE". Missing inclusion criteria not met: GLOBAL_I1.

Excluded: no affiliation/attachment construct and also fails GLOBAL_I2 (correlation with RT reduction). Construct evidence span: "Correlation of differential activation EFE > no EFE with RT reduction by EFE".

Excluded: although based on EFE manipulation, this is a brain–behavior correlation map rather than task-evoked contrast meeting GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Correlation of differential activation EFE > no EFE with RT reduction by EFE". Missing inclusion criteria not met: GLOBAL_I1.
Excluded: no self-perception construct; also fails GLOBAL_I2 (correlation). Construct evidence span: "Correlation of differential activation EFE > no EFE with RT reduction by EFE".

Excluded: ToM task present but this is a correlation map with RT reduction, not a primary whole-brain task contrast per GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Correlation of differential activation EFE > no EFE with RT reduction by EFE". Missing inclusion criteria not met: GLOBAL_I1.

Excluded: brain–behavior correlation map (RT reduction) does not meet GLOBAL_I2 for inclusion as whole-brain task-evoked contrast. Construct evidence span: "Correlation of differential activation no EFE > EFE with RT reduction by EFE". Missing inclusion criteria not met: GLOBAL_I1.
Excluded: no affiliation/attachment construct and fails GLOBAL_I2 (correlation). Construct evidence span: "Correlation of differential activation no EFE > EFE with RT reduction by EFE".
Excluded: correlation map rather than primary task contrast; fails GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Correlation of differential activation no EFE > EFE with RT reduction by EFE".
Excluded: no self-perception construct and fails GLOBAL_I2 (correlation). Construct evidence span: "Correlation of differential activation no EFE > EFE with RT reduction by EFE".

Excluded: ToM-related but correlation with RT reduction does not meet GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Correlation of differential activation no EFE > EFE with RT reduction by EFE". Missing inclusion criteria not met: GLOBAL_I1.
Excluded: interaction contrast (EFE × ToM demand) violates global exclusion for interactions. Construct evidence span: "Greater EFE effects with explicit demand of affective ToM judgments".
Excluded: interaction (GLOBAL_E3) and not an affiliation/attachment task. Construct evidence span: "Greater EFE effects with explicit demand of affective ToM judgments".

Excluded: interaction contrast (GLOBAL_E3) despite ToM relevance. Construct evidence span: "Greater EFE effects with explicit demand of affective ToM judgments".

Excluded: interaction (GLOBAL_E3) and not affiliation/attachment. Construct evidence span: "(EFE no ToM - no EFE no ToM) - (EFE ToM - no EFE ToM)".
Excluded: interaction effect (GLOBAL_E3) even though related to EFE. Construct evidence span: "(EFE no ToM - no EFE no ToM) - (EFE ToM - no EFE ToM)".
Excluded: interaction (GLOBAL_E3) and no self-referential task. Construct evidence span: "(EFE no ToM - no EFE no ToM) - (EFE ToM - no EFE ToM)".
Excluded: interaction (GLOBAL_E3) despite ToM relevance. Construct evidence span: "(EFE no ToM - no EFE no ToM) - (EFE ToM - no EFE ToM)".

Healthy adults performing an explicit interpersonal trust/distrust attitude-control task; this is a group-level univariate whole-brain contrast (Attitude > Age). Construct evidence span: "Attitude > Age".

Although the task is social, this contrast does not specifically isolate affiliation/attachment processes (bonding, loved-one/close-relationship, cooperation/competition as primary construct). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Uses face stimuli but the contrast targets instructed attitude control (trust/distrust) rather than facial/social signal communication per se (e.g., faces>nonfaces, affect recognition). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

No explicit self-referential judgment or self vs other manipulation; focuses on evaluating/imagining trust/distrust toward others. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Participants imagine trusting/distrusting each depicted person, engaging mentalizing/trait evaluation about others; group-level univariate whole-brain contrast. Construct evidence span: "Attitude > Age".

Healthy adults instructed to imagine trusting a person (social attitude control) vs non-social age evaluation; group-level univariate whole-brain contrast (Trust > Age). Construct evidence span: "Trust > Age".

Trust attitude toward strangers is not a direct measure of affiliation/attachment bonds or relationship-focused affiliation contrasts (e.g., loved one vs stranger). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Although faces are viewed, the contrast is not about decoding/communicating facial signals but instructed trust attitude formation/regulation. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-judgment/agency/self-knowledge contrast is specified. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Instructed to imagine trusting the target person, implying evaluation of others’ traits/intentions (mental state/trait inference) vs age judgment; whole-brain univariate contrast. Construct evidence span: "Trust > Age".

Healthy adults instructed to imagine distrusting a person vs age evaluation; measures social attitude control; group-level univariate whole-brain contrast (Distrust > Age). Construct evidence span: "Distrust > Age".

Distrust toward strangers is not an affiliation/attachment bonding measure and does not match typical affiliation contrasts (close relationship/cooperation). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Faces are present but the contrast is about instructed distrust (attitude/mentalizing) rather than facial communication decoding. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No explicit self-referential processing manipulation. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Imagining a person is motivated to take advantage of you requires inferring others’ intentions/traits; contrasted with age evaluation; whole-brain univariate contrast. Construct evidence span: "Distrust > Age".

This is an individual-differences regression (change-of-trust predicting activity) rather than a group-level task-evoked whole-brain contrast map generalizable as a main effect; does not meet GLOBAL_I2. Missing inclusion criteria not met: GLOBAL_I1.

Excluded because required global inclusion criteria are not met (GLOBAL_I2). Additionally, construct is not specifically affiliation/attachment. Missing: GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Excluded because required global inclusion criteria are not met (GLOBAL_I2). Missing: GLOBAL_I2, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Excluded because required global inclusion criteria are not met (GLOBAL_I2) and no self-referential manipulation. Missing: GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Although the regressor relates to social trust change, this is not a group-level task-evoked whole-brain contrast map (fails GLOBAL_I2).

This is an individual-differences regression (change-of-distrust predicting activity) rather than a group-level task-evoked whole-brain contrast map; does not meet GLOBAL_I2. Missing inclusion criteria not met: GLOBAL_I1.
Excluded because required global inclusion criteria are not met (GLOBAL_I2). Missing: GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Excluded because required global inclusion criteria are not met (GLOBAL_I2). Missing: GLOBAL_I2, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Excluded because required global inclusion criteria are not met (GLOBAL_I2) and no self-referential manipulation. Missing: GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Although about distrust toward others, this is not a group-level univariate whole-brain contrast (fails GLOBAL_I2).
Seed-based functional connectivity (PPI; "Precuneus Connectivity-Positive"), which is excluded by GLOBAL_E2.

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast). Task involves observing/understanding communicative interaction (speech acts) between two people. Construct evidence span: "contrast \"Words > Faces\"".

Global criteria met, but this contrast does not target affiliation/attachment or bonding; it is primarily speech/word perception relative to faces. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Words > Faces".

Although the paradigm is communicative, this specific contrast (Words > Faces) mainly isolates auditory/linguistic stimulation vs silent faces, not a social-communication construct contrast (e.g., faces vs non-faces, gaze, affect). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Words > Faces".
No self-referential or self-judgment component in this contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Words > Faces".

This contrast does not specifically probe understanding others' mental states/actions; it contrasts spoken word blocks with face-only blocks. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Words > Faces".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast). Contrast directly compares communicative action types (Request vs Naming), indexing social-pragmatic processing in interaction context. Construct evidence span: "contrast \"Request > Naming\"".

Requesting vs naming does not measure affiliative bonding, attachment, kinship, or relationship formation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Request > Naming".

Meets global criteria. Contrast manipulates communicative intent/function (speech acts: requesting vs naming) within social interaction videos; this is a social-communication comprehension manipulation. Construct evidence span: "the contrast \"Request > Naming\"".
No self-referential judgments or agency/self-knowledge contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Request > Naming".

Meets global criteria. Understanding requests in a dyadic interaction requires interpreting the speaker's intention/desire toward the partner (mental-state/action-intention understanding of others), which is manipulated by Request vs Naming. Construct evidence span: "contrast \"Request > Naming\"".

Insufficient analysis-level metadata (no description/table context beyond name) to verify it is a group-level whole-brain univariate contrast and to determine construct content. Missing required global inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Request > Naming".

Insufficient metadata to apply global criteria (GLOBAL_I2) and no evidence it targets affiliation/attachment. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Request > Naming".
Insufficient metadata to apply global criteria (GLOBAL_I2). Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Request > Naming".

Insufficient metadata to apply global criteria (GLOBAL_I2) and no self-related manipulation indicated. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Request > Naming".

Insufficient metadata to apply global criteria (GLOBAL_I2) and cannot confirm it measures mental-state/action understanding. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Request > Naming".

Insufficient analysis-level metadata (no description/table context beyond name) to verify it is a group-level whole-brain univariate contrast and to determine construct content. Missing required global inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Naming > Request".

Insufficient metadata to apply global criteria (GLOBAL_I2) and no evidence it targets affiliation/attachment. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Naming > Request".
Insufficient metadata to apply global criteria (GLOBAL_I2). Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Naming > Request".

Insufficient metadata to apply global criteria (GLOBAL_I2) and no self-related manipulation indicated. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Naming > Request".

Insufficient metadata to apply global criteria (GLOBAL_I2) and cannot confirm it measures mental-state/action understanding. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Naming > Request".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults and a group-level whole-brain task-evoked contrast (agentic/communal word processing) compared to implicit baseline. Task involves processing social trait words (agency/communion), i.e., social cognition content. Construct evidence span: "Compared to implicit baseline".

Global criteria pass, but this contrast is not a task specifically measuring affiliation/attachment (no bonding, loved-one, cooperation/competition, or attachment manipulation). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Compared to implicit baseline".

Global criteria pass, but the task is word valence classification of trait adjectives, not social-communicative cue processing (faces/voices/gaze/gesture) or communication production/reception. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Compared to implicit baseline".

Global criteria pass, but the analysis is not self-referential (no self-judgment or self>other comparison); it is generic trait-word processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Compared to implicit baseline".

Global criteria pass, but the contrast does not require reasoning about others’ mental states/traits (no other-target judgments or ToM/empathy); it is valence classification of trait words without other-referential framing. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Compared to implicit baseline".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task-evoked contrasts for agentic/communal trait-word processing compared to fixation. Stimuli are social trait adjectives (agency/communion), indexing social-cognitive content processing. Construct evidence span: "Compared to fixation task".

Global criteria pass, but this is not an affiliation/attachment paradigm (no relationship bonding, attachment cues, or cooperation/competition manipulation). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Compared to fixation task".

Global criteria pass, but task lacks social-communication cues (faces, gaze, prosody, gesture) and is not communication reception/production. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Compared to fixation task".

Global criteria pass, but no explicit self-referential processing or self vs other contrast is present; participants judge word valence. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Compared to fixation task".

Global criteria pass, but no explicit other-referential target or mental-state inference about others is required; it is generic trait-word valence classification. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Compared to fixation task".

Global criteria are met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast). The contrast directly compares two social-trait content dimensions (agency vs communion) during valence classification, i.e., social-cognitive content processing. Construct evidence span: "Agency vs. Communion".

Global criteria pass, but agency>communion is not an affiliation/attachment manipulation (no bonding/attachment/cooperation context). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Agency vs. Communion".

Global criteria pass, but this contrast does not involve social-communicative signal processing (faces/voices/gaze/gesture). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Agency vs. Communion".

Global criteria pass, but although 'agency' is a subconstruct label under self, this task is not self-focused (no self-judgment/self-reference); it contrasts word categories. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Agency vs. Communion".

Global criteria pass, but the contrast does not require evaluating others’ traits/mental states (no other-target instructions); it is content-category comparison. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Agency vs. Communion".

Global criteria are met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast). Contrast compares processing of communal vs agentic social trait words, capturing social-cognitive content effects. Construct evidence span: "Communion vs. Agency".

Global criteria pass, but communion>agency here reflects trait-word category effects, not an affiliation/attachment task (no loved-one, bonding, cooperation/competition). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communion vs. Agency".

Global criteria pass, but this is not social communication (no communicative cue perception/production like faces, voices, gaze, gesture). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communion vs. Agency".

Global criteria pass, but the analysis is not self-referential; it contrasts communal vs agentic trait words without self-focus. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communion vs. Agency".

Global criteria pass, but there is no explicit other-referential judgment or mental-state inference about others—just trait-word valence classification with content comparison. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communion vs. Agency".

Fails global inclusion because this is an interaction contrast "(disgusted out-group–neutral out-group)–(disgusted in-group–neutral in-group)", which is not a simple within-group main effect/contrast generalizable to the healthy adult population as required by GLOBAL_I2; also qualifies as an interaction per GLOBAL_E3. Construct evidence span: "Brain activation of the racial prejudice in disgust perception... ((disgusted out-group-neutral out-group)-(disgusted in-group-neutral in-group))".

Excluded because analysis is an interaction contrast (out-group vs in-group modulated by emotion), violating GLOBAL_E3; additionally task is passive viewing of faces and does not measure affiliation/attachment. Construct evidence span: "((disgusted out-group-neutral out-group)-(disgusted in-group-neutral in-group))".

Excluded because analysis is an interaction contrast, violating GLOBAL_E3 (even though it involves facial emotion perception). Construct evidence span: "((disgusted out-group-neutral out-group)-(disgusted in-group-neutral in-group))".

Excluded because analysis is an interaction contrast (GLOBAL_E3) and does not involve self-referential processing. Construct evidence span: "((disgusted out-group-neutral out-group)-(disgusted in-group-neutral in-group))".

Excluded because analysis is an interaction contrast, violating GLOBAL_E3 (despite being about perception of others' facial disgust). Construct evidence span: "((disgusted out-group-neutral out-group)-(disgusted in-group-neutral in-group))".

Excluded because this is seed-based task-dependent functional connectivity (gPPI) from an insula seed, which violates GLOBAL_E2; also not a univariate whole-brain activation map per GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Functional connectivity of insula underlying the racial prejudice in disgust perception" / "Positive connectivity".

Excluded because this is seed-based connectivity (gPPI), violating GLOBAL_E2; not an affiliation/attachment task. Construct evidence span: "Functional connectivity of insula... Positive connectivity".

Excluded because this is seed-based connectivity (gPPI), violating GLOBAL_E2 (even though context is facial emotion viewing). Construct evidence span: "Positive connectivity" (insula seed connectivity).
Excluded because this is seed-based connectivity (gPPI), violating GLOBAL_E2; not self-processing. Construct evidence span: "Functional connectivity of insula...".
Excluded because this is seed-based connectivity (gPPI), violating GLOBAL_E2. Construct evidence span: "Functional connectivity of insula... Positive connectivity".

Excluded because this is seed-based task-dependent functional connectivity (gPPI) reporting negative coupling, violating GLOBAL_E2; not a univariate whole-brain activation map per GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Negative connectivity" / "Functional connectivity of insula underlying the racial prejudice in disgust perception".
Excluded because this is seed-based connectivity (gPPI), violating GLOBAL_E2; not an affiliation/attachment task. Construct evidence span: "Negative connectivity".
Excluded because this is seed-based connectivity (gPPI), violating GLOBAL_E2. Construct evidence span: "Negative connectivity".
Excluded because this is seed-based connectivity (gPPI), violating GLOBAL_E2; not self-processing. Construct evidence span: "Negative connectivity".
Excluded because this is seed-based connectivity (gPPI), violating GLOBAL_E2. Construct evidence span: "Negative connectivity".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast), but this contrast targets self-referential processing rather than social processing; missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self-referential > social".

Global criteria met, but the contrast does not test affiliation/attachment (no bonding/relationship/cooperation manipulation); missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self-referential > social".

Global criteria met, but the contrast is not a social-communication manipulation (not faces/emotion/gaze etc. as primary contrast); missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self-referential > social".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast). This contrast isolates self-referential processing relative to social stimulus processing. Construct evidence span: "Self-referential > social".

Global criteria met, but the contrast is not designed to measure perception/understanding of others (it subtracts social/other-related content rather than isolating it); missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self-referential > social".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast). This contrast isolates implicit social stimulus processing (social images with humans) relative to self-referential/non-social. Construct evidence span: "Social > self-referential".

Global criteria met, but social stimuli are humans vs non-social scenes without manipulation of bonding/attachment/affiliation; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Social > self-referential".

Global criteria met, but this is not a dedicated social-communication contrast (e.g., faces>nonfaces, emotion recognition, gaze), rather broad social vs nonsocial content; missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Social > self-referential".

Global criteria met, but the contrast does not target self-perception; it instead emphasizes social/other-related stimulus processing; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Social > self-referential".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast). Social stimuli depict humans, engaging perception/understanding of others (implicit animacy/person perception). Construct evidence span: "Social > self-referential".

Healthy adults and this is a group-level whole-brain univariate effect within the task (ingroup vs outgroup categorization while viewing pain/neutral pictures). Construct evidence span: "Main effect of condition (ingroup/outgroup)".

Not an affiliation/attachment manipulation (no bonding/relationship, cooperation, loved-one contrasts). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of condition (ingroup/outgroup)".

Task is not primarily social communication (no faces/expressions/communicative signals); stimuli are hands/feet in painful/neutral situations with group labels. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of condition".

No self-referential judgment or self>other contrast; focuses on others (ingroup/outgroup). Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of condition (ingroup/outgroup)".

Ingroup/outgroup categorization during observation of others in pain/neutral contexts engages representations of others (social categorization/assessment). Construct evidence span: "Main effect of condition (ingroup/outgroup)".
Healthy adults; whole-brain group-level univariate interaction within social empathy task (ingroup/outgroup x pain). Construct evidence span: "Pain×condition".

Although group membership is manipulated, the contrast tests empathy-for-pain modulation by ingroup/outgroup rather than bonding/attachment or affiliation behaviors. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Pain×condition".

Meets global criteria: healthy adults and a group-level univariate whole-brain task contrast. The contrast targets theory of mind/social interaction processing. Construct evidence span: "Activations during the “ToM > R” analysis (whole-brain analysis".

Global criteria are met (healthy adults; whole-brain ToM>R), but the task/contrast probes theory of mind/mentalizing rather than affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship-specific evaluation; missing local criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "ToM > R".

Global criteria are met, but the contrast is about mental state attribution from animated shapes, not social communication (e.g., faces/voices/gaze/gestures); missing local criteria SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "ToM > R".

Global criteria are met, but the task does not require self-referential judgment or self vs other comparisons; missing local criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "ToM > R".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast) and local criteria because ToM/mentalizing is directly reasoning about others' mental states from animations. Construct evidence span: "ToM > R".

Excluded because this is a Bayesian volume-of-interest (VOI) analysis rather than a whole-brain voxelwise map; violates GLOBAL_I2 and meets exclusion GLOBAL_E1 (ROI/VOI). Construct evidence span: "Bayesian analysis... (volume of interest (VOI)".
Excluded because this is a Bayesian volume-of-interest (VOI) analysis (ROI/VOI), not a whole-brain map; violates GLOBAL_I2 and meets GLOBAL_E1. Construct evidence span: "volume of interest (VOI)".

Healthy adults performing a gaze-following (social cue) task with whole-brain univariate task-evoked contrast (main effect of interference: incongruent > congruent). Construct evidence span: "Main effect of interference: incongruent minus congruent contrast (IE)".

Fails local construct requirements: task is gaze-following/attention orienting to faces (social communication cueing) rather than measuring affiliation/attachment bonding, cooperation/competition, or close-relationship processes. Construct evidence span: "gaze-following (GF) paradigm". Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Whole-brain univariate task contrast during processing of others' averted gaze (gaze-following cueing), which is a core social-communication (receptive) process. Construct evidence span: "gaze-following (GF) behaviour" / "directional gazes".

No self-referential judgment or self-perception contrast in this whole-brain analysis; task contrasts are incongruent vs congruent gaze-cue interference. Construct evidence span: "incongruent minus congruent". Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Although stimuli are faces, the analyzed contrast targets attentional/saccadic conflict (incongruent > congruent) rather than reasoning about others’ mental states/traits or empathy/ToM. Construct evidence span: "directional cue and the distractor's gaze provided conflicting directional information". Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adults and group-level whole-brain task contrast. Social/empathy processing is directly manipulated. Construct evidence span: "EmpaToM: emotionally negative > neutral video".

Contrast indexes empathic affect to negative vs neutral narratives, not bonding/affiliation or relationship formation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Stimuli are videos of autobiographic narratives but contrast is emotionality (negative>neutral) rather than communicative cue processing (e.g., faces/gaze/prosody) per se. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Although participants rate their own affect, the fMRI contrast is video emotionality (negative>neutral) focused on responses to others' stories, not self-referential judgment (Self>Other). Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Healthy adults, whole-brain task contrast. Emotional narratives about others elicit empathic processing of others' states (empathy for suffering). Construct evidence span: "emotionally negative > neutral video".

Healthy adults and group-level whole-brain task contrast. Contrast isolates Theory of Mind inference vs factual reasoning, a core social-cognitive process. Construct evidence span: "EmpaToM: ToM > nonToM question".

Mentalizing (ToM>nonToM) does not specifically measure affiliation/attachment, cooperation/competition, or bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Contrast targets inference about mental states (ToM) vs factual reasoning, not reception/production of communicative signals like faces/gaze/prosody. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No explicit self-referential or agency/self-knowledge manipulation (e.g., Self>Other). Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Healthy adults, whole-brain task contrast. ToM questions require reasoning about others' thoughts/intentions (understanding mental states). Construct evidence span: "ToM > nonToM question".

Healthy adult sample and whole-brain group univariate task contrast. Empathy manipulation via negative vs neutral social narratives. Construct evidence span: "EmpaToM: emotionally negative > neutral video".
This empathy contrast does not probe bonding/attachment or affiliative interactions. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not a communication-specific contrast (e.g., faces>nonfaces, gaze), but emotionality of narratives. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Not a self-referential comparison despite self-affect ratings; contrast is driven by others' emotional narratives. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Empathy for others' negative experiences requires representing others' affective states. Construct evidence span: "emotionally negative > neutral video".
Healthy adults, whole-brain group-level task contrast. ToM inference contrasted with factual reasoning during questions. Construct evidence span: "EmpaToM: ToM > nonToM questions".
ToM vs nonToM questions do not measure affiliative bonding/attachment constructs. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not focused on communicative signal processing/production; focuses on mental state inference. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-knowledge/agency manipulation; task concerns others' mental states. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Theory of Mind is a direct instance of understanding others' mental states. Construct evidence span: "ToM > nonToM questions".

Although social, this is a differential contrast directly comparing two effects (Empathy vs ToM), not a simple within-condition task-evoked contrast intended for generalization; treated here as an interaction/difference-of-differences style comparison, which is excluded. Construct evidence span: "(emotional > neutral video) > (ToM > nonToM questions)".
Excluded due to between-contrast differential comparison (Empathy vs ToM). Construct evidence span: "(emotional > neutral video) > (ToM > nonToM questions)".
Excluded due to between-contrast differential comparison (Empathy vs ToM). Construct evidence span: "(emotional > neutral video) > (ToM > nonToM questions)".
Excluded due to between-contrast differential comparison (Empathy vs ToM). Construct evidence span: "(emotional > neutral video) > (ToM > nonToM questions)".
Excluded due to between-contrast differential comparison (Empathy vs ToM) rather than a single construct map. Construct evidence span: "(emotional > neutral video) > (ToM > nonToM questions)".

Excluded because this is a differential contrast comparing two effects (ToM vs Empathy), considered an interaction/difference-of-contrasts. Construct evidence span: "(ToM > nonToM questions) > (emotionally negative > neutral video)".
Excluded due to between-contrast differential comparison. Construct evidence span: "(ToM > nonToM questions) > (emotionally negative > neutral video)".
Excluded due to between-contrast differential comparison. Construct evidence span: "(ToM > nonToM questions) > (emotionally negative > neutral video)".
Excluded due to between-contrast differential comparison. Construct evidence span: "(ToM > nonToM questions) > (emotionally negative > neutral video)".

Excluded due to between-contrast differential comparison (ToM vs Empathy) rather than a single ToM or empathy map. Construct evidence span: "(ToM > nonToM questions) > (emotionally negative > neutral video)".

Healthy adult sample and group-level whole-brain task contrast. Task involves receiving and evaluating social reputation feedback from others (faces + trait sentences). Construct evidence span: "Positive vs. Neutral".

Contrast is about valence of reputation feedback (positive vs neutral) rather than affiliation/attachment or relationship bonding processes. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Although stimuli include faces and verbal comments, the contrast targets evaluative processing of reputation valence (positive vs neutral), not social-communication-specific perception (e.g., faces>nonfaces, gaze, emotion recognition). Missing local criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Participants imagine being told comments about themselves and rate pleasantness (self-referential social evaluation). Construct evidence span: "Positive vs. Neutral".

Task is primarily about receiving self-relevant reputation feedback rather than inferring others’ mental states/traits; no ToM/empathy/other-understanding contrast. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Healthy adult sample and group-level whole-brain task contrast. Task involves receiving and evaluating social reputation feedback from others. Construct evidence span: "Negative vs. Neutral".
Contrast examines negative reputation feedback vs neutral, not affiliative bonding/attachment. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Not a social-communication construct contrast (no face perception or communicative cue manipulation); focuses on valenced reputation evaluation. Missing local criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Receiving negative comments about oneself and rating pleasantness is self-referential evaluation. Construct evidence span: "Negative vs. Neutral".
No requirement to infer others’ mental states/intentions; primarily self-evaluation of feedback. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Conjunction of positive and negative reputation (both > neutral) reflects general social reputation processing in a whole-brain group map in healthy adults. Construct evidence span: "(Positive > Neutral) inclusively masked with (Negative > Neutral)".

Common activation for reputation feedback (positive/negative) does not specifically index affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship formation. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Conjunction reflects general processing of being evaluated, not specific social communication perception/production contrasts. Missing local criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Both positive and negative reputations are self-directed ("You are kind/unreliable") and require self-referential pleasantness rating. Construct evidence span: "(Positive > Neutral) inclusively masked with (Negative > Neutral)".
No explicit other-mentalizing demands; giver identity is present but not requiring inference about their minds/traits. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults isolating regions more engaged by positive (vs neutral) than negative (vs neutral) reputation feedback—social evaluative processing. Construct evidence span: "(Positive vs. Neutral) vs. (Negative vs. Neutral)".

This is a valence-difference contrast in reputation feedback, not an affiliation/attachment manipulation (e.g., loved one/cooperation/bonding). Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Not a social communication-specific contrast; it does not isolate communicative cue processing (e.g., face vs nonface, gaze, emotion recognition), but rather positive-vs-negative social evaluation. Missing local criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Contrast targets differential self-relevant valuation of positive vs negative comments about the participant. Construct evidence span: "(Positive vs. Neutral) vs. (Negative vs. Neutral)".
Does not require understanding others’ mental states; focuses on participant’s experienced pleasantness/value of feedback. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults testing greater responses to negative (vs neutral) than positive (vs neutral) reputation feedback—social evaluative processing. Construct evidence span: "(Negative vs. Neutral) vs. (Positive vs. Neutral)".
Negative>positive reputation difference does not operationalize affiliation/attachment bonding. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not a communication-perception/production contrast; rather valenced feedback evaluation. Missing local criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Contrast reflects differential self-evaluative processing of negative vs positive comments about self. Construct evidence span: "(Negative vs. Neutral) vs. (Positive vs. Neutral)".
No explicit mentalizing/empathy about the commenter; primary process is self-referential evaluation. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adults and group-level whole-brain task contrast; task involves observed eye contact and gaze cues (social cue processing). Construct evidence span: "Eye Contact > Averted Gaze".

Contrast probes perceptual processing of eye contact vs averted gaze rather than bonding/attachment, kinship, or affiliation outcomes; missing local criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Eye Contact > Averted Gaze".
Whole-brain task contrast during viewing faces establishing eye contact vs averted gaze, a core communicative/ostensive signal. Construct evidence span: "Eye Contact > Averted Gaze".
No self-referential judgment or self-related stimuli; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Eye Contact > Averted Gaze".
Requires interpreting others' attentional relation (eye contact vs averted), i.e., understanding others' social/attentional states. Construct evidence span: "Eye Contact > Averted Gaze".
Healthy adults, group-level whole-brain task contrast; compares observed averted vs mutual gaze context, part of social cue processing. Construct evidence span: "Averted Gaze > Eye Contact".
Does not measure attachment/affiliation or social bonding; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Averted Gaze > Eye Contact".
Uses gaze direction between faces (averted vs eye contact), a nonverbal social-communicative cue. Construct evidence span: "Averted Gaze > Eye Contact".
No self-focused processing; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Averted Gaze > Eye Contact".
Concerns perception of others' gaze/attentional relation (looking away vs at each other), relevant to understanding others. Construct evidence span: "Averted Gaze > Eye Contact".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast in a gaze-following paradigm (social attention). Construct evidence span: "Congruent Gaze Cues > Incongruent Gaze Cues".

Gaze congruency contrast indexes attention orienting, not affiliation/bonding; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Congruent Gaze Cues > Incongruent Gaze Cues".
Gaze cueing (congruent vs incongruent) is receptive nonverbal communication (gaze following). Construct evidence span: "Congruent Gaze Cues > Incongruent Gaze Cues".
No self-referential component; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Congruent Gaze Cues > Incongruent Gaze Cues".
Infers/uses others' attentional direction toward target (gaze), supporting understanding of others' attention. Construct evidence span: "Congruent Gaze Cues > Incongruent Gaze Cues".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast within gaze-following paradigm; processes social cue/attention mismatch. Construct evidence span: "Incongruent Gaze Cues > Congruent Gaze Cues".

Contrast reflects attentional reorienting to gaze incongruency, not attachment/affiliation; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Incongruent Gaze Cues > Congruent Gaze Cues".
Gaze following and processing of gaze cue incongruency are aspects of receptive social communication. Construct evidence span: "Incongruent Gaze Cues > Congruent Gaze Cues".
No self-knowledge/agency manipulation; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Incongruent Gaze Cues > Congruent Gaze Cues".

Requires interpreting others' gaze direction relative to target (others' attention/intent signals), fitting perception/understanding of others. Construct evidence span: "Incongruent Gaze Cues > Congruent Gaze Cues".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast examining effect of observed eye contact context on subsequent congruent gaze following—social attention processing. Construct evidence span: "Eye Contact congruent > Averted Gaze congruent".

Modulation of gaze-following by eye-contact context does not operationalize affiliation/attachment; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Eye Contact congruent > Averted Gaze congruent".
Assesses communicative/ostensive influence of observed eye contact on processing gaze cues (gaze following). Construct evidence span: "Eye Contact congruent > Averted Gaze congruent".
No self-processing; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Eye Contact congruent > Averted Gaze congruent".

Involves understanding others' attentional relation (eye contact) and subsequent joint attention to object (congruent gaze), fitting perception/understanding of others. Construct evidence span: "Eye Contact congruent > Averted Gaze congruent".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast within averted-gaze context comparing incongruent vs congruent gaze cues (gaze following). Construct evidence span: "Averted Gaze incongruent > Averted Gaze congruent".

Contrast is about gaze-cue congruency under averted-gaze context, not attachment/affiliation; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Averted Gaze incongruent > Averted Gaze congruent".
Gaze cue incongruency in a social gaze paradigm is receptive social communication processing. Construct evidence span: "Averted Gaze incongruent > Averted Gaze congruent".
No self-referential manipulation; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Averted Gaze incongruent > Averted Gaze congruent".
Requires processing others' gaze direction vs target (others' attention cues) under averted-gaze context. Construct evidence span: "Averted Gaze incongruent > Averted Gaze congruent".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast), but this contrast indexes generic cognitive/motor conflict (Incongruent > Congruent) without isolating social processing; it is not explicitly measuring social processing content. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Incongruent > Congruent".

Although study context concerns affiliation/mimicry, this analysis is the generic incongruency (imitative conflict) contrast and does not operationalize affiliation/attachment. Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Incongruent > Congruent".

Task includes faces/hands, but this analysis collapses across social cue conditions and tests general imitation control rather than communication (e.g., faces vs nonfaces, emotion/gaze). Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Incongruent > Congruent".

No self-referential/agency/self-judgment manipulation in this contrast; it reflects imitation inhibition. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Incongruent > Congruent".

While observing others' actions is part of the task, this contrast is not designed to measure understanding others (mental states/empathy) and is primarily response conflict/imitative control. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Incongruent > Congruent".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate). Contrast targets mimicry regulation specifically modulated by a social cue (happy facial expressions), i.e., processing/responding to social-affective information. Construct evidence span: "Happy (Incongruent > Congruent) masked inclusively with Happy (Incongruent > Congruent) > Angry (Incongruent > Congruent)".

Meets global criteria. Happy faces are affiliative signals and the analysis isolates neural correlates of enhanced mimicry regulation in response to that affiliative cue (reciprocation/affiliative intent). Construct evidence span: "Happy (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > Angry".

Meets global criteria. Uses facial emotional expressions (receptive facial communication) and tests differential processing of happy vs angry context during action control. Construct evidence span: "Happy (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > Angry (Incongruent > Congruent)".

Although authors discuss self-other distinction (TPJ), the contrast is not a self-referential/agency manipulation (no Self > Other judgment); it is emotion-modulated imitation control. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Happy (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > Angry".

The contrast is driven by affective facial cue modulation rather than explicit reasoning about others' mental states/traits; no ToM/empathy contrast. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Happy (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > Angry".

Global criteria met, but this reverse contrast targets angry-specific mimicry regulation effects and (per results) yields only occipital activation; it does not clearly measure social processing beyond low-level visual effects. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Angry (Incongruent > Congruent) masked inclusively with Angry ... > Happy".

Angry faces are non-affiliative/dominance cues; this contrast does not assess affiliation/attachment bonding. Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Angry (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > Happy".

Meets global criteria. Involves receptive facial communication (processing angry vs happy expressions) as contextual modulation of task responses. Construct evidence span: "Angry (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > Happy (Incongruent > Congruent)".
No self-focused/agency/self-knowledge manipulation. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Angry (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > Happy".

No explicit inference about others' mental states/traits; primarily emotion-context modulation. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Angry (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > Happy".

Meets global criteria. Contrast isolates mimicry regulation effects that are stronger for out-group vs in-group faces (social group membership cue), directly indexing social information processing guiding behavior. Construct evidence span: "Out-group (Incongruent > Congruent) masked inclusively with Out-group ... > In-group".

Meets global criteria. Out-group modulation is interpreted as affiliative/appeasement-related mimicry; analysis targets neural systems supporting affiliation-relevant regulation toward social partners. Construct evidence span: "Out-group (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > In-group".

Meets global criteria. Task involves viewing faces and biologically relevant hand actions; contrast captures how group membership (communicative/social cue) modulates imitation control. Construct evidence span: "Out-group (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > In-group (Incongruent > Congruent)".

No explicit self-perception/agency/self-knowledge comparison; group-membership modulation pertains to processing others. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Out-group (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > In-group".

Meets global criteria. Out-group vs in-group faces require representing/assessing others as social agents (social categorization of others) and modulates action processing; this fits perception/understanding of others (action perception/social agent properties). Construct evidence span: "Out-group (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > In-group".

Global criteria met, but in-group-specific mimicry regulation (and reported cerebellar vermis effect) does not clearly operationalize social processing beyond generic condition difference; lacks clear social-processing measurement content. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "In-group (Incongruent > Congruent) masked inclusively with In-group ... > Out-group".

Although in-group relates to social bonding, this analysis does not explicitly test affiliative bonding (e.g., loved one/attachment) and effects are not clearly affiliation-specific. Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "In-group (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > Out-group".

Meets global criteria. Uses face/hand social stimuli and tests in-group vs out-group contextual modulation of imitation control (a social cue in communication context). Construct evidence span: "In-group (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > Out-group (Incongruent > Congruent)".
No self-referential or agency/self-knowledge contrast. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "In-group (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > Out-group".

While social categorization is present, this reverse contrast (in-group > out-group in mimicry regulation) does not clearly measure understanding others; reported effect is cerebellar/vermis. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "In-group (Incongruent > Congruent) ... > Out-group".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level univariate whole-brain conjunction of task contrast). Task involves making attributions about social stimuli (emotional expressions, intentional actions) using why>how. Construct evidence span: "Response to why > how questions for all stimulus categories".

Global criteria are met, but this analysis does not specifically target affiliation/attachment bonding (no loved-one/relationship, cooperation/competition, or attachment manipulation). Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "why > how questions for all stimulus categories".

Although social stimuli include faces/actions, the contrast indexes attributional reasoning (why>how) rather than communicative signal reception/production per se; nonsocial scenes are also included in the conjunction. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2 (communication-specific contrast). Construct evidence span: "why > how questions for all stimulus categories".

No self-referential judgments or self vs other comparison; task is about explaining stimuli in photos. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "why > how questions for all stimulus categories".

Meets global criteria. Why>how for social stimuli requires inferring others' mental states (theory of mind) from emotional expressions/intentional actions; conjunction includes social attribution component. Construct evidence span: "Response to why > how questions for all stimulus categories".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level univariate whole-brain conjunction of interaction contrasts). This analysis isolates greater attributional processing for social vs nonsocial stimuli. Construct evidence span: "Stronger response to why > how questions for social than for nonsocial stimuli".

Contrast is social-vs-nonsocial attributional reasoning, not bonding/attachment or affiliation behaviors. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Stronger response to why > how questions for social than for nonsocial stimuli".

Does not specifically measure social communication (e.g., faces>nonfaces, gaze, mimicry); it measures attributional processing difference for social vs nonsocial. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Stronger response to why > how questions for social than for nonsocial stimuli".

No self-focused processing manipulation (self-judgment, self vs other). Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Stronger response to why > how questions for social than for nonsocial stimuli".

Meets global criteria. Interaction explicitly captures stronger mental-state attribution for social (others' emotions/actions) than nonsocial scenes, aligning with understanding others/ToM. Construct evidence span: "Stronger response to why > how questions for social than for nonsocial stimuli".

Healthy adults and group-level whole-brain task contrast; deception in a sender–receiver interaction is a social task requiring social-cognitive processing. Construct evidence span: "simple deception and sophisticated deception trials vs. truth trials".

Although the task is interactive, this contrast targets deception vs truth (mentalizing/strategic intent) rather than affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship formation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

The contrast is not about processing communicative cues (e.g., faces/voices/gaze) but about strategic deception intention; thus it does not specifically measure social communication. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Not a self-reflection/self-judgment or self-knowledge/agency contrast; it contrasts deceptive vs truthful messages in interaction. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Deception in strategic interaction requires inferring the receiver’s beliefs/expectations (theory of mind); contrast isolates intention to deceive vs truth. Construct evidence span: "Intention to deceive in strategic interactions".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain deception contrast in an interactive sender–receiver game, a social decision-making task. Construct evidence span: "Simple deception vs. truth".

Contrast isolates lying (false message) vs truth, not cooperation/competition bonding or attachment/affiliation outcomes. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not focused on reception/production of communicative social signals; it is a strategic deception contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Not a self-referential or agency/self-knowledge contrast; it is about choosing to deceive another. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Simple deception entails intention to mislead the receiver and anticipating their belief/choice (mental-state reasoning). Construct evidence span: "simple deception trials vs. truth trials".
Healthy adults; whole-brain group contrast during strategic interaction contrasting sophisticated deception vs truth (social decision). Construct evidence span: "Sophisticated deception vs. truth".

Contrast is about sophisticated deception (truth told with intent to mislead), not affiliation/attachment or relationship bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Does not isolate communicative cue processing; rather isolates strategic intent/deception. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Not about self-referential judgments or agency/self-knowledge; it is about deceiving another person. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Sophisticated deception is defined by telling the truth while expecting the receiver not to believe/follow it, directly invoking belief/expectation inference (ToM). Construct evidence span: "sophisticated deception trials vs. truth trials".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast between two forms of deception in a social interaction task. Construct evidence span: "Sophisticated deception vs. simple deception".

Contrast differentiates deception strategies rather than affiliation/attachment constructs like bonding, cooperation, or social comparison as primary target. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Not about communicative signal perception/production; it contrasts strategic mental operations underlying deception types. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Does not require explicit self-evaluation or self-knowledge; compares two deception behaviors. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Sophisticated vs simple deception is theorized to vary in demands for predicting the receiver’s beliefs/choices (mentalizing), i.e., understanding others’ mental states. Construct evidence span: "Sophisticated deception vs. simple deception".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast of genuine truth vs deception conditions in a sender–receiver interaction (social decision). Construct evidence span: "Truth vs. simple and sophisticated deception".

This is about truth-telling vs deception, not attachment/affiliation bonding, cooperation/competition per se, or relationship measures. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Not about processing communicative cues (faces/voices/gaze) or communicative reception/production; it is about honesty vs deception strategy. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Although truth-telling may involve internal monitoring, the contrast does not operationalize self-referential judgment/agency explicitly. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Truth vs deception here reflects sender’s strategy; it is not a task that explicitly measures inference about others’ emotions/traits/beliefs in the contrast definition (as opposed to the deception contrasts). Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adults; whole-brain group-level parametric modulation within a social deception task (incentive/conflict during deception). Construct evidence span: "Parametric analysis modeling the incentive to deceive for simple deception trials".

Parametric modulator is monetary tension/conflict during lying, not affiliation/attachment or bonding-related constructs. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not about communicative cue processing; parametric effect relates to payoff conflict during deception. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Parametric modulation does not operationalize self-knowledge/agency judgments; it models payoff conflict during deception. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Even though the parametric modulator is payoff tension, it is applied within simple deception trials in a sender–receiver setting requiring prediction/manipulation of the receiver’s beliefs/choices. Construct evidence span: "incentive to deceive for simple deception trials".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate main effect/contrast) and targets social/emotional crowd perception. Construct evidence span: "A. Fear>happy+neutral".

Not an affiliation/attachment or bonding/cooperation manipulation; contrast is emotion perception in crowds. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Fear>happy+neutral".
Task involves perceiving others’ emotional body expressions in a crowd (receptive non-facial social cues). Construct evidence span: "Fear>happy+neutral".

No self-referential judgment, agency, or self-knowledge manipulation; participants perform dot-color task while viewing crowds. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Fear>happy+neutral".
Contrast indexes perception of others’ emotional states from body/crowd cues (action/emotion perception). Construct evidence span: "Fear>happy+neutral".
Meets global criteria and examines social/emotion perception from crowd body language. Construct evidence span: "B. Fear+happy>neutral".

No affiliation/attachment, cooperation/competition, kinship, or bonding content manipulated; emotion vs neutral crowd perception only. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Fear+happy>neutral".
Viewing emotional (fear/happy) vs neutral body-expression crowds engages receptive non-facial social communication/affect recognition. Construct evidence span: "Fear+happy>neutral".
No self-perception/self-knowledge or self vs other comparison. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Fear+happy>neutral".
Targets perception/understanding of others’ emotions from group bodily expressions. Construct evidence span: "Fear+happy>neutral".
Meets global criteria; contrast reflects differential processing of emotional crowd cues (fear/neutral vs happy). Construct evidence span: "C. Fear+neutral>happy".
No attachment/affiliation construct (no bonding, loved one, cooperation) tested. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Fear+neutral>happy".
Involves receptive processing of others’ affective body-language in crowds (social cue decoding). Construct evidence span: "Fear+neutral>happy".
No self-related appraisal/agency; task is dot detection during viewing. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Fear+neutral>happy".
Contrast captures perception of others’ emotional state/action cues (fear/neutral crowds) relative to happy. Construct evidence span: "Fear+neutral>happy".
Meets global criteria; evaluates social/emotional crowd perception (happy crowds vs fear/neutral). Construct evidence span: "D. Happy>fear+neutral".

Happy emotion is not an affiliation/attachment manipulation (no bonding/cooperation/kinship). Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Happy>fear+neutral".
Receptive decoding of non-facial social/emotional cues (happy body expressions in crowds). Construct evidence span: "Happy>fear+neutral".
No self-referential processing or self vs other judgment. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Happy>fear+neutral".
Indexes perception of others’ emotional states/actions (happy crowds) relative to other emotions. Construct evidence span: "Happy>fear+neutral".
Meets global criteria; still within social-crowd perception task contrasting neutral vs emotional crowd displays. Construct evidence span: "E. Neutral>fear+happy".
Neutral vs emotional crowds does not assess affiliation/attachment/bonding. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Neutral>fear+happy".
Perception of social body-language cues in crowds (neutral communicative content vs emotional). Construct evidence span: "Neutral>fear+happy".
No self-focused processing. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Neutral>fear+happy".
Still measures perception/understanding of others’ actions/states in a crowd context (neutral crowds) relative to emotional crowds. Construct evidence span: "Neutral>fear+happy".
Meets global criteria; dynamics manipulation (interactive>individual) directly targets social interaction processing in crowd perception. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of dynamics".

Interactive vs individual behavior is not specifically affiliation/attachment (bonding/cooperation/kinship) and lacks relationship/attachment content. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of dynamics".
Interactive vs individual crowds manipulate reception of nonverbal social interaction cues (social signaling/communication). Construct evidence span: "Main effect of dynamics".
No self-referential judgments/agency manipulation. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of dynamics".
Directly probes perception of others’ interactions/actions (interactive vs individual crowds). Construct evidence span: "Main effect of dynamics".
Meets global criteria; specific effect of social interaction dynamics under fear (panic-like crowd) within social perception task. Construct evidence span: "A. Interactive fear>individual fear".

Fearful interaction vs individual fear does not assess attachment/affiliation bonds. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear".
Contrast reflects reception of socially interactive nonverbal cues (panic interaction) vs noninteractive fear. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear".
No explicit self-focused appraisal or agency task. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear".
Targets understanding/perception of others’ actions and emotional states in an interacting fearful crowd. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear".

Meets global criteria; evaluates interaction dynamics effects for fear and happy (social interaction perception). Construct evidence span: "B. Interactive fear>individual fear+interactive happy>individual happy".

Interactive behavior here is not operationalized as bonding/attachment/cooperation; it is interaction dynamics in crowds. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear+interactive happy>individual happy".

Nonverbal social interaction cues (interactive vs individual) in emotional crowds are central to social communication reception. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear+interactive happy>individual happy".
No self-judgment or self-referential comparison. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear+interactive happy>individual happy".

Contrast involves perceiving others’ interactive actions/emotional states (fearful and happy interactions). Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear+interactive happy>individual happy".

Meets global criteria; tests interaction dynamics effects within fear and neutral crowds (social interaction perception). Construct evidence span: "C. Interactive fear>individual fear+interactive neutral>individual neutral".

No affiliation/attachment/bond manipulation; dynamics contrast not about relationship bonds. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear+interactive neutral>individual neutral".

Perception of interactive vs individual nonverbal crowd cues constitutes receptive social communication processing. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear+interactive neutral>individual neutral".
No self-related processing contrast. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear+interactive neutral>individual neutral".

Engages perception of others’ actions/interactions (interactive vs individual) under fear and neutral contexts. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear+interactive neutral>individual neutral".

Meets global criteria; interaction effect combining interactive fear>individual fear with individual neutral>interactive neutral remains within social crowd perception domain. Construct evidence span: "D. Interactive fear>individual fear+individual neutral>interactive neutral".
No attachment/affiliation construct. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear+individual neutral>interactive neutral".

Manipulates reception of nonverbal social interaction cues across conditions (interactive vs individual). Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear+individual neutral>interactive neutral".
No self-appraisal/self-other judgment. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear+individual neutral>interactive neutral".

Assesses perception/understanding of others’ interactive behaviors and emotional states (particularly fear). Construct evidence span: "Interactive fear>individual fear+individual neutral>interactive neutral".
Meets global criteria; interactive vs individual in neutral crowds still probes social interaction perception in crowd context. Construct evidence span: "E. Interactive neutral>individual neutral".

Neutral interaction dynamics do not operationalize attachment/affiliation bonds. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Interactive neutral>individual neutral".

Contrast is driven by presence of social interaction cues (interactive vs individual), a core receptive social communication component. Construct evidence span: "Interactive neutral>individual neutral".
No self-referential processing. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Interactive neutral>individual neutral".
Directly tests perception of others’ interactions/actions (animacy/action perception) in crowds. Construct evidence span: "Interactive neutral>individual neutral".
