Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast during a social addressment/communication task manipulating co-speech gesture. Construct evidence span: "main effect of Gesture (IC>NG)".

Does not measure affiliation/attachment (bonding, kinship, cooperation/competition); it manipulates gesture and body orientation during addressment judgments. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Global criteria met; task is comprehension of nonverbal communicative cues (co-speech iconic gestures) in spoken interaction. Construct evidence span: "IC>NG".

Contrast is not a self-referential judgment (self traits/agency) but gesture presence vs absence; any self-involvement is indirect. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Primarily indexes gesture/speech integration and action observation; not explicitly reasoning about others’ mental states/emotions/traits. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Global criteria met; contrast isolates social cue of direct addressment via egocentric body orientation, relevant to social interaction/self-involvement. Construct evidence span: "ego>allo".
No bonding/relationship/affiliation manipulation; it is body orientation in communication. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Egocentric vs allocentric orientation is a non-facial communicative cue affecting perceived addressment in conversation videos. Construct evidence span: "main effects of egocentric (ego) versus allocentric (allo) actor orientation" / "ego>allo".

Although egocentricity relates to self-involvement, the contrast does not require explicit self-judgment/agency; it is orientation processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Not an explicit ToM/empathy/mental state inference contrast; focuses on orientation cue. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Global criteria met; allocentric vs egocentric orientation is a social-communicative context manipulation (being addressed vs not) within conversational stimuli. Construct evidence span: "allo>ego".
No affiliation/attachment construct assessed. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Allocentric (non-addressing) vs egocentric (addressing) body orientation is a key social communication cue in the task. Construct evidence span: "allo>ego".
No explicit self-focused appraisal; orientation manipulation only. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Does not explicitly require inferring others’ mental states; mainly social cue/context. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Excluded due to global criterion violation: this is a factorial interaction contrast. Exclusion criterion applied: GLOBAL_E3 (between-condition interaction). Missing required global inclusion (as eligible map): GLOBAL_I2.
Excluded due to global criterion violation (interaction). Also not an affiliation/attachment task. Exclusion criterion applied: GLOBAL_E3.
Excluded due to global criterion violation: interaction contrast (IC-ego>IC-allo)>(NG-ego>NG-allo). Exclusion criterion applied: GLOBAL_E3.
Excluded due to global criterion violation (interaction). Exclusion criterion applied: GLOBAL_E3.
Excluded due to global criterion violation (interaction). Exclusion criterion applied: GLOBAL_E3.

Excluded due to global criterion violation: this is an interaction contrast (IC-allo>NG-allo)>(IC-ego>NG-ego). Exclusion criterion applied: GLOBAL_E3. Missing required global inclusion (as eligible map): GLOBAL_I2.
Excluded due to global criterion violation (interaction) and not an affiliation/attachment construct. Exclusion criterion applied: GLOBAL_E3.
Excluded due to global criterion violation: interaction contrast. Exclusion criterion applied: GLOBAL_E3.
Excluded due to global criterion violation (interaction). Exclusion criterion applied: GLOBAL_E3.
Excluded due to global criterion violation (interaction). Exclusion criterion applied: GLOBAL_E3.

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task-evoked contrast) and assesses social interaction/joint attention responding. Construct evidence span: "Responding to joint attention (RJA - RJAc)".

Global criteria are met, but the contrast targets joint attention responding rather than bonding/attachment/cooperation-vs-competition or close-relationship affiliation measures; thus missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Responding to joint attention (RJA - RJAc)".

Meets global criteria and probes communicative gaze behavior (responding to a partner’s gaze bid; joint attention), a core social-communication process. Construct evidence span: "Responding to joint attention (RJA - RJAc)".

Global criteria are met, but the analysis does not require explicit self-referential judgment/agency attribution about the self (no Self>Other or self-evaluation manipulation); thus missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Responding to joint attention (RJA - RJAc)".

Meets global criteria and requires interpreting another agent’s gaze as an intentional cue (representing the partner’s attentional focus), aligning with perception/understanding of others. Construct evidence span: "Responding to joint attention (RJA - RJAc)".
Meets global criteria and assesses social interaction/joint attention initiation versus matched non-social control. Construct evidence span: "Initiating joint attention (IJA - IJAc)".

Global criteria are met, but the task contrast is about initiating joint attention via gaze, not affiliation/attachment bonding, kinship, or relationship/attachment constructs; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Initiating joint attention (IJA - IJAc)".

Meets global criteria and targets productive communicative gaze (initiating a joint attention bid), squarely within social communication. Construct evidence span: "Initiating joint attention (IJA - IJAc)".

Global criteria are met, but the contrast does not isolate self-reflection/self-judgment/agency explicitly (no self-referential appraisal condition); missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Initiating joint attention (IJA - IJAc)".

Meets global criteria and involves tracking/monitoring the partner’s readiness and response (mutual gaze; partner follows), requiring representation of the other’s attention state. Construct evidence span: "Initiating joint attention (IJA - IJAc)".

Meets global criteria and the conjunction isolates activity common to initiating and responding to joint attention, a social interaction process. Construct evidence span: "Conjunction of initiating and responding to joint attention (IJA - IJAc) with (RJA - RJAc)".

Global criteria are met, but this conjunction concerns joint attention (communicative attention coordination) rather than affiliation/attachment bonding constructs; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Conjunction of initiating and responding to joint attention (IJA - IJAc) with (RJA - RJAc)".

Meets global criteria and captures shared neural substrates for communicative gaze behaviors (both initiating and responding to joint attention bids). Construct evidence span: "Conjunction of initiating and responding to joint attention (IJA - IJAc) with (RJA - RJAc)".

Global criteria are met, but the conjunction does not involve explicit self-judgment/agency/self-referential contrasts; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Conjunction of initiating and responding to joint attention (IJA - IJAc) with (RJA - RJAc)".

Meets global criteria and joint attention necessarily entails representing the partner’s attentional focus/intentional cueing; conjunction reflects common other-oriented representation demands across roles. Construct evidence span: "Conjunction of initiating and responding to joint attention (IJA - IJAc) with (RJA - RJAc)".

Meets global criteria and tests differential social processing for initiating vs responding to joint attention after subtracting matched non-social controls. Construct evidence span: "Initiating Joint attention minus responding to joint attention (IJA - IJAc) - (RJA - RJAc)".

Global criteria are met, but the contrast indexes role differences in joint attention, not affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship-related constructs; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Initiating Joint attention minus responding to joint attention (IJA - IJAc) - (RJA - RJAc)".

Meets global criteria and compares two communicative gaze roles (initiating vs responding), directly within social communication. Construct evidence span: "Initiating Joint attention minus responding to joint attention (IJA - IJAc) - (RJA - RJAc)".

Global criteria are met, but the contrast does not specifically operationalize self-referential processing or agency judgments about self; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Initiating Joint attention minus responding to joint attention (IJA - IJAc) - (RJA - RJAc)".

Meets global criteria and contrasts conditions that both require representing the partner’s attention; difference still reflects varying demands on monitoring/inferring the other’s state across roles. Construct evidence span: "Initiating Joint attention minus responding to joint attention (IJA - IJAc) - (RJA - RJAc)".

Meets healthy adult sample and is a group-level whole-brain univariate task contrast (friend > pc) during perceived interpersonal communication, engaging mentalizing/social context processing. Construct evidence span: "Univariate analysis: friend minus pc".

Whole-brain univariate contrast isolates processing of messages believed from a real-life friend vs computer, tapping relationship/attachment-related salience of a close other. Construct evidence span: "text messages perceived as being sent by the participant's friend minus ... computer".

Task is receiving text messages in a communication context; contrast tests sender effect during ongoing communication (friend vs computer), a non-facial receptive communication signal. Construct evidence span: "received text messages ... sent either by their real-life friend ... or by a computer".

Global inclusion criteria met, but construct is not about self-knowledge/agency; contrast is sender (friend vs computer) rather than self-referential judgment. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Friend>computer engages reasoning about another agent/sender and mentalizing network; analysis targets perception/understanding of others' intentions/mental states in social context. Construct evidence span: "confirm that our task activated the 'mentalizing network'".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain map) and uses social task context (text messages from friend vs computer) while decoding affective word content. Construct evidence span: "Searchlight-based MVPA: happy versus sad".

Although task involves a friend, this analysis tests content decoding (happy vs sad) invariant across senders rather than affiliation/attachment manipulation (friend vs stranger/close vs distant) at whole-brain univariate level. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Decoding the semantic/affective content of received text messages (receptive non-facial communication signal) fits social communication construct. Construct evidence span: "sensory content of text messages ('happy' and 'sad')".
Analysis does not involve self-referential judgments or agency; it classifies word content. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Analysis focuses on sensory/lexical content encoding in VOT independent of sender, not understanding others’ mental states or actions. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Excluded because analysis is a multivoxel connectivity/seed-based functional connectivity (MVCA with VOT seed), which is disallowed by global criteria. Construct evidence span: "Searchlight-based MVCA: friend minus pc".

Excluded due to seed-based/multivoxel connectivity analysis (MVCA), which violates global exclusion for connectivity. Construct evidence span: "functional coupling with the sensory-content encoding clusters".
Excluded due to connectivity analysis (MVCA) with seed regions; not an eligible whole-brain univariate activation map. Construct evidence span: "multivoxel connectivity analysis (MVCA)".
Excluded due to connectivity analysis (MVCA) (GLOBAL_E2); additionally not self-perception. Construct evidence span: "MVCA".
Excluded due to connectivity analysis (MVCA) with seed regions (GLOBAL_E2), despite mentalizing relevance. Construct evidence span: "changed the strength of its functional coupling".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task-evoked contrast) and is a social-emotional face processing task. Construct evidence span: "HA>NE".
Task is facial emotion labeling (happy>neutral), not affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship evaluation; missing local criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Facial emotion labeling contrast (happy>neutral) indexes reception of facial communication, a core social-communication process. Construct evidence span: "HA>NE".
No self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge manipulation; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Contrast is emotion faces vs neutral.
Labeling others' emotional facial expressions requires inferring others’ affective state (emotion recognition). Construct evidence span: "happy>neutral".
Meets global criteria and involves social-emotional processing of facial expressions. Construct evidence span: "AN>NE".
Angry>neutral face processing is not an affiliation/attachment paradigm; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Angry facial expression labeling engages reception/interpretation of facial communicative signals. Construct evidence span: "AN>NE".
No explicit self-processing component; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Recognizing anger in another person is perception/understanding of others’ emotional state. Construct evidence span: "angry versus neutral faces".
Meets global criteria and reflects social-emotional face processing. Construct evidence span: "FE>NE".
Fearful>neutral face labeling is not measuring affiliation/attachment; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Fearful facial expressions are communicative social cues; contrast indexes reception of facial communication. Construct evidence span: "FE>NE".
No self-referential processing; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Inferring fear from another’s face is understanding others’ emotional state. Construct evidence span: "Fearful versus neutral faces".

Fails GLOBAL_I2: this is an individual-differences regression (correlation with TAS-20) rather than an univariate group-level task-evoked activation map generalizable to healthy adults. Construct evidence span: "Correlations between brain response to emotional faces and TAS-20 total score".
Fails GLOBAL_I2 (individual-differences correlation) and not an affiliation/attachment task; missing GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2: correlation/regression on TAS-20 rather than group-level univariate task-evoked map. Construct evidence span: "TAS-20 total score".
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and no self-processing manipulation; missing GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2: analysis is alexithymia-modulated activation rather than generalizable task-evoked group map; missing GLOBAL_I2.

Fails GLOBAL_I2 because this is a second-level regression with TAS-20 total score (individual differences) rather than a generalizable group-level contrast map. Construct evidence span: "Correlations between brain response to emotional faces and TAS-20 total score".
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and does not test affiliation/attachment; missing GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2: correlational model with TAS-20, not a standard group-level task contrast. Construct evidence span: "AN>NE" with TAS-20 regressor.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and no self-processing; missing GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 due to individual-differences regression (TAS-20 modulation) rather than generalizable task-evoked map; missing GLOBAL_I2.

Fails GLOBAL_I2: this is an alexithymia (TAS-20) correlation/regression with FE>NE, not a generalizable group-level task effect map. Construct evidence span: "Correlations between brain response to emotional faces and TAS-20 total score".
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and not affiliation/attachment; missing GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2: individual-differences correlation with TAS-20. Construct evidence span: "FE>NE" with TAS-20 regressor.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and no self-focused task; missing GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 because activation is conditional on TAS-20; missing GLOBAL_I2.

Fails GLOBAL_I2: second-level regression/correlation with TAS-20-DDF rather than a generalizable group-level univariate contrast map. Construct evidence span: "Correlations between brain response to emotional faces and TAS-20-DDF".
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and not affiliation/attachment; missing GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2: individual-differences correlation (TAS-20-DDF) not a task main effect map.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and no self-processing contrast; missing GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2: alexithymia-modulated activation rather than generalizable task-evoked map; missing GLOBAL_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 because this is a correlational/regression analysis with TAS-20-DDF (individual differences) for AN>NE, not a standard group-level contrast map. Construct evidence span: "TAS-20-DDF".
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and not affiliation/attachment; missing GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2: TAS-20-DDF modulation, not group-level task effect.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and no self construct; missing GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 due to individual-differences regression; missing GLOBAL_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2: correlation/regression with TAS-20-DDF for FE>NE rather than generalizable group-level contrast. Construct evidence span: "TAS-20-DDF".
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and not affiliation/attachment; missing GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2: individual-differences modulation analysis rather than group-level task map.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and no self contrast; missing GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 due to TAS-20-DDF correlation; missing GLOBAL_I2.

Fails GLOBAL_I2: second-level regression with TSIA-DDF (individual differences) rather than a generalizable group-level whole-brain contrast map. Construct evidence span: "Correlations between brain response to emotional faces and TSIA-DDF".
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and not affiliation/attachment; missing GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Fails GLOBAL_I2: TSIA-DDF correlational model not group-level task effect.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and no self-processing manipulation; missing GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Fails GLOBAL_I2: AN>NE activation correlated with TSIA-DDF (individual differences), not a generalizable group-level contrast. Construct evidence span: "TSIA-DDF".

Fails GLOBAL_I2: individual-differences correlation with TSIA-DDF rather than task effect map.
Fails GLOBAL_I2 and no self-processing; missing GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Meets global criteria: healthy adult sample and group-level univariate whole-brain CV>NV contrast. Task involves viewing campaign videos designed to induce empathy/social evaluation. Construct evidence span: "campaign vs. neutral videos (CV>NV contrast)".

Global criteria are met, but task/contrast is not about forming/expressing social bonds or attachment (no kinship/cooperation/loved-one manipulations). Construct evidence span: "campaign vs. neutral videos (CV>NV contrast)". Missing required local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global criteria are met, but contrast is not specifically isolating social communication (e.g., faces/gaze/speech vs control); it contrasts dramatic empathy-inducing videos vs neutral driving videos. Construct evidence span: "campaign vs. neutral videos (CV>NV contrast)". Missing required local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Global criteria are met, but passive viewing contrast does not require self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge manipulation. Construct evidence span: "campaign vs. neutral videos (CV>NV contrast)". Missing required local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Meets global criteria and locally reflects understanding others’ states: campaign videos depict victims/actors with tragic consequences and are designed to induce empathy, aligning with reasoning about others’ emotions/mental states. Construct evidence span: "empathy induction".

Excluded due to whole-brain voxelwise interaction (Status Type × Status Level), which is not eligible under global criteria. Construct evidence span: "Status Type by Status Level interaction". Global inclusion GLOBAL_I2 not met (requires within-group whole-brain contrast/main effect), and GLOBAL_E3 applies (interaction).

Excluded due to whole-brain voxelwise interaction (Status Type × Status Level), which is not eligible under global criteria. Construct evidence span: "Status Type by Status Level interaction". Global inclusion GLOBAL_I2 not met, and GLOBAL_E3 applies (interaction).

Excluded due to whole-brain voxelwise interaction (Status Type × Status Level), which is not eligible under global criteria. Construct evidence span: "Status Type by Status Level interaction". Global inclusion GLOBAL_I2 not met, and GLOBAL_E3 applies (interaction).

Excluded due to whole-brain voxelwise interaction (Status Type × Status Level), which is not eligible under global criteria. Construct evidence span: "Status Type by Status Level interaction". Global inclusion GLOBAL_I2 not met, and GLOBAL_E3 applies (interaction).

Excluded due to whole-brain voxelwise interaction (Status Type × Status Level), which is not eligible under global criteria. Construct evidence span: "Status Type by Status Level interaction". Global inclusion GLOBAL_I2 not met, and GLOBAL_E3 applies (interaction).
Healthy adults performing a social impression-formation/person evaluation task; whole-brain univariate main effect map (Status Type) is eligible. Construct evidence span: "Status Type main effect".

Global criteria pass, but this main effect tests status dimension (moral vs financial) during impression formation rather than affiliation/attachment bonding or close-relationship processes; AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2 not met. Construct evidence span: "Status Type main effect".

Although faces are viewed, the analysis is not a face-communication contrast (e.g., Faces > non-faces/emotion/gaze). It is a main effect of social status dimension during impression formation; SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2 not met. Construct evidence span: "Status Type main effect".

No explicit self-referential judgment/contrast (e.g., Self > Other). This is a main effect of targets' status type during impression formation; PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2 not met. Construct evidence span: "Status Type main effect".

Task requires forming impressions/evaluating other people based on moral vs financial status labels—reasoning about others' traits/value. Whole-brain univariate main effect (Status Type) captures differential processing of others by status dimension. Construct evidence span: "Status Type main effect".

Healthy adults in a social impression-formation task; whole-brain univariate main effect map (Status Level) is eligible and reflects processing of socially relevant status information. Construct evidence span: "Status Level main effect".

Status Level (lower/same/higher) during impression formation does not specifically index affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship formation; AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2 not met. Construct evidence span: "Status Level main effect".

Not a social communication contrast (no manipulation of communicative signals like expression/gaze/speech; not Faces > non-faces). It is a main effect of status level labels. SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/I2 not met. Construct evidence span: "Status Level main effect".
No explicit self-processing contrast; this is about evaluating others varying in status level. PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2 not met. Construct evidence span: "Status Level main effect".

Status Level main effect reflects impression formation/evaluation of other individuals varying in social status level (lower/same/higher), i.e., assessing others' traits/value. Construct evidence span: "Status Level main effect".

Meets global criteria: healthy adult males and group-level whole-brain task-evoked contrast. Task involves observing an opponent’s angry vs neutral facial expressions in a social interaction (TAP), i.e., processing social-emotional cues. Construct evidence span: "Angry >neutral".

Although the task is social/interactive, this contrast (angry > neutral facial expressions) does not primarily measure bonding/attachment or affiliative processes; it is emotion-signal perception under provocation/competition. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Angry >neutral".

Meets global criteria. Contrast isolates neural response to an opponent’s angry vs neutral facial expression (facial affect signal), a core receptive social-communication process. Construct evidence span: "Angry >neutral".

Contrast is about perceiving the opponent’s facial expression rather than self-referential evaluation/agency. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Angry >neutral".

Meets global criteria. Processing an opponent’s angry vs neutral expression in an interactive context supports representing others’ affective state (social threat/anger). Construct evidence span: "Angry >neutral".

Meets global criteria: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task-evoked contrast. Although likely a different task stage (e.g., outcome phase), the contrast remains angry vs neutral trials in a social interaction. Construct evidence span: "Angry >neutral".

Angry > neutral (in a competitive provocation task) does not operationalize affiliation/attachment/bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Angry >neutral".

Contrast reflects differential processing of the opponent’s angry vs neutral facial affect signal (receptive facial communication), even if measured at a later stage (context effect). Construct evidence span: "Angry >neutral".

No self-focused judgment/agency manipulation; contrast centers on anger vs neutral social context. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Angry >neutral".
Angry vs neutral trials in a reciprocal interaction index processing of another person’s affective/intentional state and its social meaning. Construct evidence span: "Angry >neutral".

Excluded because this is a parametric modulation contrast (punishment selection modulator) rather than a straightforward group-level whole-brain task-evoked condition contrast suitable for the meta-analytic inclusion gate as specified. Fails required global inclusion criterion GLOBAL_I2 (whole-brain evidence from a standard task-evoked statistical map/contrast). Construct evidence span: "Parametric modulation angry >neutral".
Fails global gate (GLOBAL_I2) and additionally does not operationalize affiliative/attachment processes. Construct evidence span: "Parametric modulation angry >neutral".

Fails global gate (GLOBAL_I2) because this is a parametric modulation (aggression/punishment selection dependence) rather than a primary facial-communication contrast map. Construct evidence span: "Parametric modulation angry >neutral".
Fails global gate (GLOBAL_I2) and does not involve self-referential judgments. Construct evidence span: "Parametric modulation angry >neutral".

Fails global gate (GLOBAL_I2) because it is a parametric modulation contrast rather than a standard task-evoked whole-brain contrast; also primarily indexes aggression-related trial-by-trial behavior modulation rather than explicit other-mental-state reasoning. Construct evidence span: "Parametric modulation angry >neutral".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain conjunction across socially oriented conditions (triad/self/other). Construct evidence span: "Brain areas activated in the conjunction triad ∩ self ∩ other."

Local construct not specifically assessed: conjunction reflects general mentalizing/self/other processing rather than affiliation/attachment bonding or cooperation/competition per se. Missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Task uses object images with imagination/judgment prompts; not reception/production of communicative cues (faces/gaze/gestures) as primary measured construct in this analysis. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Conjunction includes self-referential trials (self relevance judgments), capturing self-related processing within whole-brain map. Construct evidence span: "conjunction triad ∩ self ∩ other" (includes "self" trials).

Conjunction includes other-referential/mentalizing trials (object relevance to another person), capturing reasoning about others within whole-brain map. Construct evidence span: "conjunction triad ∩ self ∩ other" (includes "other" trials).

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast indexing triadic social interaction imagination vs self/other (socially related task). Construct evidence span: "Triad > (Self & Other)".

Triadic interaction imagination is not explicitly about forming bonds/attachment, kinship, or cooperation/competition; it targets action/interaction representation and mentalizing/mirroring. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

No communicative signal processing (faces/gaze/gestures) is the measured contrast; contrast is triadic interaction imagination over self/other relevance judgments. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Contrast isolates triadic interaction relative to self/other; not a self-focused comparison (e.g., Self > Other). Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Triadic trials require representing another intentional agent in an interaction (mentalizing/action understanding about others) more than self/other relevance judgments alone. Construct evidence span: "triad > (self & other)".
Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast isolates self-referential processing. Construct evidence span: "Self > (Other & Triad)".
Self-relevance judgment about objects does not measure affiliation/attachment bonds. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
No communicative cue exchange/recognition is measured; it is self-referential appraisal. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Contrast directly targets self-referential processing (self relevance) over other and triad conditions. Construct evidence span: "Self > (Other & Triad)".
This contrast is self-focused and subtracts other/triad; it does not index understanding of others. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast isolates other-referential processing. Construct evidence span: "Other > (Self & Triad)".
Judging object relevance to a public figure (Angela Merkel) does not operationalize affiliation/attachment bonding. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
No communicative cue processing (faces/gaze/gestures) is assessed; it is other-referential appraisal. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Contrast is other-focused and subtracts self/triad; not self-referential. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Contrast directly targets other-referential processing/mentalizing about another person (Angela Merkel) over self and triad. Construct evidence span: "Other > (Self & Triad)".

Meets global criteria: healthy adult sample (10 right-handed males) and a group-level whole-brain univariate contrast. Task involves viewing dyadic biological motion varying in social congruency. Construct evidence span: "(atypical-upright+atypical-inverted)-(typical-upright+typical-inverted)".

Global criteria are met, but the task/contrast does not assess bonding/attachment/cooperation; it tests atypical vs typical social interaction displays. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global criteria are met, but stimuli are point-light dyadic actions without communicative signals (e.g., faces/gaze/speech) and contrast targets social congruency violations rather than communication. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Global criteria are met, but there is no self-referential judgment/agency manipulation; participants perform same/different task while observing others. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Meets global criteria and involves perception/understanding of others' actions/interaction coherence from third-person view using point-light biological motion; contrast isolates atypical (incongruent) vs typical interaction. Construct evidence span: "dyadic displays" and "(atypical-upright+atypical-inverted)-(typical-upright+typical-inverted)".

Meets global criteria: healthy adult sample and a second-level random-effects whole-brain univariate contrast (ROI-detection using subset but still whole-brain). Task is social (observing dyadic interactions) with manipulation of social congruency. Construct evidence span: "Clusters of activation for (atypical-upright+atypical-inverted)-(typical-upright+typical-inverted)".

Global criteria are met, but the contrast does not measure affiliation/attachment/close bonds; it measures atypical vs typical interaction. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global criteria are met, but point-light dyadic motion is not a social-communication task (no facial/vocal/gaze communication contrast). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Global criteria are met, but no self-focused processing; participants observe others and do same/different judgments. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Meets global criteria and directly targets perception/understanding of others' interaction (action perception/animacy inference) via atypical vs typical dyadic biological motion. Construct evidence span: "(atypical-upright+atypical-inverted)-(typical-upright+typical-inverted)".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate task contrasts). Task involves viewing realistic first-person social interaction videos (reactive–aggressive, social-positive, neutral), i.e., social information processing. Construct evidence span: "fMRI contrasts on the basis of the neutral condition as baseline".

Although social-positive interactions are present, this analysis set is broadly 'affective interactions vs neutral' and not specifically designed to measure bonding/attachment/affiliation constructs (e.g., loved one, close relationships, cooperation). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "neutral condition as baseline".

Stimuli are complex social interaction videos, but contrasts are not specifically about communicative signal processing (e.g., faces/gaze/gestures as communication) versus non-social controls; instead they emphasize affective interaction categories (aggressive/positive) vs neutral baseline. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "neutral condition as baseline".

First-person perspective videos instruct participants to put themselves into the observer role, engaging self-referential agency/embodiment during interactions (e.g., being provoked/handshake). Construct evidence span: "displayed from a first-order perspective".

Participants perceive and interpret another person's actions/emotions (aggressive vs friendly vs neutral) within realistic interactions, aligning with action perception/understanding others’ states. Construct evidence span: "reactive–aggressive, social-positive, and neutral".
Meets global criteria; analysis corresponds to whole-brain contrast results in Table 2 (column A) for social interaction condition comparisons. Construct evidence span: "A".

Column A likely reflects reactive–aggressive vs neutral (a1>n1) rather than affiliation/attachment-specific bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "A".

Not a targeted communication contrast (e.g., faces/gaze/communicative gestures vs controls); it is an affective interaction contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "A".
First-person interaction videos entail self-involvement/agency during provocation/response; whole-brain contrasts capture this engagement. Construct evidence span: "A".
Requires interpreting another agent’s behavior (threat/friendly/neutral) and action dynamics in the scene, consistent with perception/understanding of others. Construct evidence span: "A".
Meets global criteria; Table 2 column B is a whole-brain task-evoked contrast within social interaction viewing. Construct evidence span: "B".

Column B likely corresponds to social-positive vs neutral (p1>n1) or similar, but still not an affiliation/attachment-specific operationalization (no bonding/relationship manipulation). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "B".

Not designed to isolate communicative cue processing (faces/gaze/speech acts) versus non-communicative controls; it's an affective interaction comparison. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "B".
First-person perspective and instruction to empathize with the observer implicate self-related processing during interaction viewing. Construct evidence span: "B".
Participants observe and evaluate others’ social actions (friendly vs neutral, etc.), fitting action perception/understanding others. Construct evidence span: "B".
Meets global criteria; Table 2 column C reflects conjunction/overlap across social interaction contrasts, still social task whole-brain. Construct evidence span: "C".

Conjunction of affective interactions vs neutral does not specifically operationalize affiliation/attachment. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "C".
Conjunction analysis is not a communication-specific contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "C".
First-person involvement across affective social interactions engages self/agency processes; conjunction captures common self-involved interaction processing. Construct evidence span: "C".
Conjunction reflects common processing of others’ actions/emotions across affective interaction types. Construct evidence span: "C".
Meets global criteria; Table 2 column D represents another whole-brain social interaction contrast (final phase results). Construct evidence span: "D".

Final-phase contrasts are about affective interaction category differences, not attachment/relationship bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "D".

Not a dedicated social communication task/contrast; it is affective interaction processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "D".
Final phase includes observer's context-appropriate response shown from first-person perspective, implicating agency/self-involvement. Construct evidence span: "D".
Even in final phase, processing depends on interpreting the other’s actions (provocation/friendliness) in the interaction. Construct evidence span: "D".

Healthy adults performing a social feedback/trait-rating task; group-level whole-brain self>other contrast during receiving social feedback. Construct evidence span: "MAIN EFFECT: FEEDBACK ONSET: SELF > OTHER".

Although task is social, this contrast isolates self vs other feedback receipt rather than bonding/attachment or affiliation-specific manipulation (e.g., loved one/stranger, cooperation). Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK ONSET: SELF > OTHER".

Not a social communication task (no face/voice/gaze/communicative signal reception/production contrast); it is evaluative feedback about traits. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK ONSET: SELF > OTHER".
Contrast targets self-referential processing while receiving feedback about one's own traits vs another person's. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK ONSET: SELF > OTHER".

Contrast emphasizes self>other, not processing of others per se; other-related processing is present but not isolated. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2 for an other-focused contrast. Construct evidence span: "SELF > OTHER".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast during social feedback receipt for other>self, which is still social feedback processing. Construct evidence span: "MAIN EFFECT: FEEDBACK ONSET: OTHER > SELF".

Other>self during feedback onset does not specifically operationalize affiliation/attachment (no bonding, relationship/close-other manipulation, or cooperation/competition metric). Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "OTHER > SELF".

Not a communication paradigm; no facial/auditory communicative cue processing contrast. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK ONSET: OTHER > SELF".
Direction is other>self, so not isolating self-related processing. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I2 (Self > Other). Construct evidence span: "OTHER > SELF".
Other>self during receipt of trait feedback isolates processing/evaluation of another person. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK ONSET: OTHER > SELF".
Excluded because this is a between-group interaction/contrast (German > Chinese), not a generalizable within-group healthy adult map. Construct evidence span: "INTERACTION: ... × (GERMAN > CHINESE)".
Excluded due to between-group interaction (German > Chinese). Construct evidence span: "(SELF > OTHER) × (GERMAN > CHINESE)".
Excluded due to between-group interaction (German > Chinese). Construct evidence span: "(SELF > OTHER) × (GERMAN > CHINESE)".
Excluded due to between-group interaction (German > Chinese). Construct evidence span: "(SELF > OTHER) × (GERMAN > CHINESE)".
Excluded due to between-group interaction (German > Chinese). Construct evidence span: "(SELF > OTHER) × (GERMAN > CHINESE)".

Healthy adults; whole-brain group-level covariate analysis linking brain responses in self>other feedback onset contrast to overall updating behavior during a social feedback task. Construct evidence span: "WHOLE-BRAIN CORRELATION WITH OVERALL RELATIVE ABSOLUTE MEAN UPDATES ... FEEDBACK ONSET: SELF > OTHER".

Update/conformity to feedback is social but not specifically affiliation/attachment (no relationship-bond/close-other manipulation). Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "correlation with ... mean updates".

No communicative signal processing; correlation is with evaluative updating to feedback. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "correlation with ... updates".

Covariate analysis is within the self>other feedback-onset contrast, indexing self-referential processing during feedback and its relationship to updating. Construct evidence span: "... in the contrast FEEDBACK ONSET: SELF > OTHER".
Contrast basis is self>other; not isolating others-focused processing. Missing: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2 (other-focused contrast) for inclusion. Construct evidence span: "SELF > OTHER".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain parametric modulation during social feedback (reward-related component) contrasting self vs other modulation by feedback ratings. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK RATING (TRIAL-BY-TRIAL CORRELATION): SELF > OTHER".

Parametric modulation by feedback rating (social reward value) is not an affiliation/attachment construct (no bonding/relationship manipulation). Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK RATING ... SELF > OTHER".
Not a communication signal processing contrast; it is reward-related valuation of feedback. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK RATING ...".
Self-specific parametric effect of feedback ratings (self > other) reflects processing of self-relevant evaluative information. Construct evidence span: "SELF > OTHER".
Contrast is explicitly self > other; not isolating perception/understanding of others. Missing: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "SELF > OTHER".

Healthy adults; whole-brain parametric modulation by feedback discrepancy (social comparison component) for self and other during social feedback. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK DISCREPANCIES (POSITIVE TRIAL-BY-TRIAL CORRELATION): SELF AND OTHER".

Social comparison-like discrepancy signal is present, but task does not measure affiliation/attachment bonds; discrepancy is about evaluative mismatch, not relationships. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK DISCREPANCIES ...".

Not a communication task; discrepancy modulation reflects comparison/mentalizing rather than communicative cue processing. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK DISCREPANCIES ...".
Includes self-related discrepancy modulation during receiving feedback, implicating self-evaluation/update processes. Construct evidence span: "... SELF AND OTHER" (includes self trials).

Includes other-related discrepancy modulation during receiving feedback about another person, implicating evaluation/representation of others. Construct evidence span: "... SELF AND OTHER" (includes other trials).

Healthy adults; whole-brain parametric modulation by feedback discrepancy (negative correlation) during social feedback for self and other, still indexing social comparison-related processing. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK DISCREPANCIES (NEGATIVE TRIAL-BY-TRIAL CORRELATION): SELF AND OTHER".

Negative discrepancy correlation does not specifically measure affiliation/attachment bonding. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK DISCREPANCIES (NEGATIVE ...)".
Not a social communication contrast. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "FEEDBACK DISCREPANCIES (NEGATIVE ...)".
Includes self-related negative modulation by discrepancy during feedback, relevant to self-evaluation/comparison. Construct evidence span: "SELF AND OTHER" (includes self trials).
Includes other-related negative modulation by discrepancy during feedback, relevant to evaluating another person. Construct evidence span: "SELF AND OTHER" (includes other trials).

Excluded due to ROI-based localizer defining an mPFC ROI (not a generalizable whole-brain task-evoked map). Construct evidence span: "Self > other" (localizer) is used to define ROI.
Excluded due to ROI-based localizer analysis (mPFC ROI definition), not eligible whole-brain map for meta-analysis. Construct evidence span: "Self > other".
Excluded due to ROI-based localizer analysis; not a whole-brain task-evoked map eligible for construct coding. Construct evidence span: "Self > other".

Global inclusion met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate localizer contrast). Localizer directly targets self-referential processing via self-trait judgments contrasted with other (Obama). Construct evidence span: "Self > other".

Although the contrast involves 'other', it is not designed to measure understanding of others’ mental states; it is primarily a self-referential localizer. Missing local criteria PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self > other".
Excluded due to ROI-based localizer context and not a social-processing task per se; also contrast is inverse of self-reference localizer. Construct evidence span: "Other > self".
Excluded due to ROI-based localizer analysis. Construct evidence span: "Other > self".
Excluded due to ROI-based localizer analysis. Construct evidence span: "Other > self".

Global inclusion met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate map). The inverse self-reference localizer contrast still indexes self vs other processing (less self-referential activation). Construct evidence span: "Other > self".

The 'other' target is a single familiar public figure; contrast is not a ToM/empathy/other-understanding task. Missing local criteria PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Other > self".

Task is go/no-go detecting remote communication vs moral sentences; not clearly a social-processing construct. Also contrast is content-based (communication>moral) without explicit social interaction/other-processing. Missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1/SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communication > moral (collapsing across self/other)".

Not an affiliation/attachment contrast; no bond/cooperation/competition manipulation isolated in this map. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communication > moral".

Although stimuli mention remote communication, the contrast is semantic category (communication vs moral) and not a communication-cue processing task (faces/voices/gaze etc.) as defined. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communication > moral".

Contrast collapses across self/other; does not isolate self-referential processing. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communication > moral (collapsing across self/other)".

Contrast does not assess understanding of others; collapses across self/other and focuses on sentence domain. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communication > moral".

Contrast is moral>communication semantic category; moral self/other content is not necessarily social-processing per the construct definitions, and it collapses across self/other. Missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1/SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Moral > communication (collapsing across self/other)".

No affiliation/attachment manipulation isolated; contrast is moral content vs communication content. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Moral > communication".
Not a social communication cue processing contrast; it is the opposite category. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Moral > communication".
Collapses across self/other and does not compare self vs other. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Moral > communication (collapsing across self/other)".
Does not measure understanding others; no ToM/empathy/other-judgment contrast. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Moral > communication".

Self>other sentence perspective contrast is primarily self-referential rather than broad social processing; nonetheless, because it is self-related, not categorized as social processing here. Missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2 (must measure social processing). Construct evidence span: "Self > other (collapsing across moral/communication)".

Not an affiliation/attachment contrast; it is self vs other perspective. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self > other (collapsing across moral/communication)".
Not a social communication perceptual task; self/other phrasing of sentences. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self > other".

Global inclusion met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate task-evoked contrast). Contrast explicitly isolates self-referential processing (first-person vs third-person) across content. Construct evidence span: "Self > other (collapsing across moral/communication)".

Although it involves 'other', the contrast is self-referential framing rather than reasoning about others’ mental states. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self > other".

Other>self in sentence perspective is not clearly social processing; primarily inverse self-referential effect. Missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Other > self (collapsing across moral/communication)".
Not an affiliation/attachment manipulation. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Other > self".
Not a social communication task/contrast as defined (faces/voices/gaze etc.). Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Other > self".

Global inclusion met (healthy adults; whole-brain univariate contrast). Inverse of self-reference contrast still pertains to self vs other framing, relevant to self-referential processing. Construct evidence span: "Other > self (collapsing across moral/communication)".
Does not measure understanding of others’ mental states; it's a linguistic perspective contrast. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Other > self".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain parametric map. Task involves witnessing an emergency with varying number of bystanders (social context) affecting action-preparation regions. Construct evidence span: "Decrease in activity with the increase in group size".

Global criteria met, but this contrast indexes modulation by bystander group size during an emergency, not affiliation/attachment bonding, cooperation/competition, or close-relationship processing; missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global criteria met, but no explicit communication/reception-production (faces, gaze, vocal, gesture) contrast; it is a bystander context modulation during emergency. Missing local inclusion criteria SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Decrease in activity with the increase in group size" indicates action-preparation modulation rather than communication.
Global criteria met, but analysis does not involve self-referential judgments/agency/self-knowledge contrasts. Missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain parametric map. Participants witness another person fainting (others' state/action) and social context (bystanders) modulates response, fitting perception/understanding of others (action/empathy-related). Construct evidence span: "Decrease in activity with the increase in group size".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain parametric map during witnessing an emergency with varying bystander group size (social context). Even though effects are in visual/attention regions, the manipulation is social and the analysis measures processing of social scenes. Construct evidence span: "Increase in activity with the increase in group size".

Global criteria met, but contrast reflects visual/attention increase with group size in emergency scenes, not affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship/cooperation constructs; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Global criteria met, but no face/gaze/gesture/communication manipulation; increase attributed to visual complexity/attention. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Global criteria met, but no self-referential/agency/self-knowledge task component. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain parametric map while observing others in an emergency with varying bystander number (others' actions/states). Although authors suggest visual complexity, stimuli depict people and an emergency, supporting action perception of others. Construct evidence span: "Increase in activity with the increase in group size".

Global criteria met, but contrast is primarily a visual-complexity control (inverted/reversed control movies) rather than measuring social processing per se; missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2 (must measure Social Processing). Construct evidence span: "Control > bystander".
Global criteria met, but contrast is visual control and not affiliation/attachment. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Global criteria met, but contrast is not communication-related; it is a control for visual complexity. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Global criteria met, but no self-processing. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Global criteria met, but Control > bystander is not a clean measure of perceiving/understanding others; it is designed to index visual complexity differences (inverted/reversed control). Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2 (contrast must measure perception/understanding of others).

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast comparing emergency event (fainting/falling) vs non-emergency (standing). This measures processing of a social scene involving another person in need. Construct evidence span: "Falling > standing".
Global criteria met, but no bonding/affiliation/attachment manipulation; it's emergency vs standing. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Global criteria met, but contrast is not about communicative signals (faces/gaze/gesture) and facial info was blurred; missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Global criteria met, but no self-referential processing. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast of observing another person faint/fall (others' action/state) vs standing. Fits action perception/empathy-relevant perception of others. Construct evidence span: "Falling > standing".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast comparing standing up vs falling down scenes of another person, within the same social-video paradigm. Still measures processing of socially relevant human action context. Construct evidence span: "Standing > falling".
Global criteria met, but no affiliation/attachment construct; it is standing vs falling. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Global criteria met, but not a communication contrast (faces blurred; no gaze/expressive exchange). Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Global criteria met, but no self-focused judgments/agency. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast of observing another person's action outcome (standing up) vs falling, i.e., perception of others' actions/states. Construct evidence span: "Standing > falling".

Global gate not passed: analysis is a table of "Significant clusters of activation in houses and faces fMRI task" and (per methods) the key reported activation metric is suprathreshold voxel counts extracted from bilateral fusiform gyri (AAL), indicating an ROI-based approach rather than a generalizable whole-brain voxelwise map. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Significant clusters of activation in houses and faces fMRI task".

Global gate not passed due to ROI-based fusiform gyrus (AAL) voxel-count approach (ROI). Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2, plus local affiliation task criteria (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2) not evidenced. Construct evidence span: "faces-houses fMRI task" / "voxel counts in bilateral fusiform gyri".

Although the task involves face perception (faces vs houses), global gate not passed because reported outcomes rely on ROI-defined fusiform gyrus voxel counts (AAL), i.e., ROI analysis. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "voxel counts in the face-sensitive fusiform gyrus" / "faces versus houses".

Global gate not passed due to ROI-based analysis (fusiform gyrus AAL voxel counts). Additionally, the task does not involve self-referential processing (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "faces versus houses fMRI task".

While viewing faces can relate to understanding others, global gate not passed because activation was operationalized via ROI-defined fusiform gyrus voxel counts (AAL), i.e., ROI analysis. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "faces versus houses" / "fusiform gyri as defined by AAL".

Healthy adults and group-level whole-brain task contrast (imitating > rest) in an online reciprocal imitation paradigm indexing social interaction. Construct evidence span: "Imitating".

Task is interactive imitation/observation and does not specifically measure affiliation/attachment (bonding, close relationship, cooperation/competition). Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Imitating".
Imitative exchange of hand gestures is non-facial social communication (production/reception of gestures) in an online interaction context. Construct evidence span: "Imitating".

Contrast targets action execution/interaction rather than explicit self-referential judgment or agency attribution; self-processing not directly measured. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Imitating".
Imitating a partner’s gestures requires action perception/understanding of another’s actions and intentions during interaction. Construct evidence span: "Imitating".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast (being imitated > rest) during live reciprocal imitation, a social-interaction condition. Construct evidence span: "Being imitated".

Being imitated in this paradigm does not operationalize attachment/affiliation bonding or relationship evaluation. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Being imitated".
Condition involves reciprocal gestural interaction (non-facial communicative exchange via hand movements) and thus indexes social communication. Construct evidence span: "Being imitated".
No explicit self-judgment/agency attribution contrast; primarily interactive sensorimotor/social task. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Being imitated".

Being imitated requires monitoring and understanding the other’s contingent responses/intentions in the interaction (understanding mental states/action understanding). Construct evidence span: "Being imitated".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast (observation > rest) of another person’s hand gestures—socially relevant action observation. Construct evidence span: "Observation".

Passive observation of hand gestures does not measure affiliation/attachment bonding or related constructs. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Observation".
Observing another’s hand gestures is reception of non-facial social communication cues. Construct evidence span: "Observation".
Observation condition does not involve explicit self-referential processing or agency judgments. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Observation".
Action perception of another’s hand movements fits perception/understanding of others (action perception). Construct evidence span: "Observation".

Healthy adult sample and group-level whole-brain task-evoked contrast. Contrast probes emotional context during social-cognitive perspective-taking (empathy/ToM/self-reference). Construct evidence span: "Main effect (ES + TS + SRS) - (EN + TN + SRN)".

Contrast is about sad vs neutral emotional context during empathy/ToM/self-reference; it does not measure social bonding/attachment, kinship, cooperation/competition, or relationship formation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Stimuli are complex scenes and the contrast is sad vs neutral across conditions; not designed to isolate reception/production of communicative signals (e.g., faces/gaze/voices) or communication-specific contrasts. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Includes self-referential perspective-taking as part of the contrasted set (self-reference condition) and indexes self-related internal state simulation under emotional context. Construct evidence span: "(ES + TS + SRS) - (EN + TN + SRN)".

Includes other-focused mental state inference as part of the contrasted set (empathy and ToM conditions) and indexes understanding others' internal states under emotional context. Construct evidence span: "(ES + TS + SRS) - (EN + TN + SRN)".
Healthy adult sample; group-level whole-brain task contrast within empathy condition. Construct evidence span: "Empathy ES-EN".
Empathy sad>neutral does not assess affiliation/attachment bonds or relationship-oriented behavior. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Empathy ES-EN is not a communication task/contrast (no face/gaze/voice communication manipulation). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Empathy condition focuses on what another person feels (other-oriented) rather than self-judgment/self-knowledge. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Empathy requires inferring the main character’s feelings; sad vs neutral modulates other-state understanding. Construct evidence span: "Empathy ES-EN".
Healthy adult sample; group-level whole-brain task contrast within ToM condition. Construct evidence span: "ToM TS-TN".
ToM sad>neutral does not measure bonding/attachment or affiliation behaviors. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
ToM TS-TN is not a communication-specific paradigm/contrast (no manipulation of communicative signals). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
ToM condition is other-focused (what would make the main character feel better), not self-referential judgment. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
ToM requires reasoning about another person's internal state/needs; sad vs neutral modulates that inference. Construct evidence span: "ToM TS-TN".
Healthy adult sample; group-level whole-brain task contrast within self-reference condition. Construct evidence span: "Self-reference SRS-SRN".

Self-reference sad>neutral is about own feelings in a depicted situation, not affiliation/attachment or social bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
No communicative signal processing manipulation; contrast is self-reference sad vs neutral. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Self-reference condition explicitly asks participants to imagine how they would feel; sad vs neutral indexes self-related internal state simulation. Construct evidence span: "Self-reference SRS-SRN".

Although prompted by someone else's situation, the instruction is first-person self-feeling; not primarily assessing others' mental states. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adult sample; group-level whole-brain task contrast between social-cognitive conditions (empathy vs ToM) regardless of emotional context. Construct evidence span: "Empathy > ToM(ES + EN)-(TS + TN)".
Empathy > ToM contrast compares types of internal-state inference, not affiliation/attachment or bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not a social communication contrast (no facial/gaze/voice communication manipulation). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Neither condition is primarily self-focused; contrast is between other-oriented empathy and other-oriented ToM. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Both empathy and ToM involve understanding others’ feelings/mental states; their comparison targets mechanisms of other-state inference. Construct evidence span: "Empathy > ToM(ES + EN)-(TS + TN)".
Healthy adult sample; group-level whole-brain task contrast between ToM and empathy conditions regardless of emotional context. Construct evidence span: "ToM > Empathy(TS + TN)-(ES + EN)".
ToM > empathy indexes cognitive demands of mentalizing, not affiliation/attachment or bonding processes. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Contrast does not isolate social communication reception/production. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Both conditions are other-focused rather than self-referential. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
ToM condition requires reasoning about another’s state/what would make them feel better; contrasted with empathy (also other-focused). Construct evidence span: "ToM > Empathy(TS + TN)-(ES + EN)".
Healthy adult sample; group-level whole-brain task contrast comparing empathy with self-reference across emotional contexts. Construct evidence span: "Empathy > Self-reference(ES + EN)-(SRS + SRN)".
Empathy vs self-reference concerns perspective-taking on internal states, not relationship bonding/attachment. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not designed to isolate communicative cue processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Contrast involves self-reference condition (imagining how one would feel), and the comparison targets self vs other perspective components. Construct evidence span: "Empathy > Self-reference(ES + EN)-(SRS + SRN)".

Empathy condition requires inferring others' feelings and is directly contrasted with self-reference, capturing other-focused internal state understanding. Construct evidence span: "Empathy > Self-reference(ES + EN)-(SRS + SRN)".
Healthy adult sample; group-level whole-brain task contrast comparing self-reference with empathy across emotional contexts. Construct evidence span: "Self-reference > Empathy(SRS + SRN)-(ES + EN)".
Self-reference > empathy is not an affiliation/attachment manipulation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not a social communication contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Self-reference condition explicitly asks participants to imagine their own feelings; contrast isolates self-related processing relative to empathy. Construct evidence span: "Self-reference > Empathy(SRS + SRN)-(ES + EN)".

Empathy provides other-focused mental state inference baseline; the contrast against it still pertains to mechanisms differentiating self vs other internal state representations within social cognition. Construct evidence span: "Self-reference > Empathy(SRS + SRN)-(ES + EN)".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain conjunction contrast). Social trait judgments (trustworthiness/attractiveness) are explicitly social evaluation. Construct evidence span: "Main effects of social judgments" / "[(TR - HA) ∩ (AT - HA) ∩ (TR - AG) ∩ (AT - AG)]".

Task is explicit trait evaluation of voices (trustworthiness/attractiveness) and does not measure bonding/attachment, cooperation/competition, or relationship-specific affiliation. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "(TR ... AT ...)".

Participants process socially meaningful vocal signals to judge traits/emotion; receptive non-facial social communication via auditory stimuli is central to the task. Construct evidence span: "vocal stimuli" with "trustworthiness"/"attractiveness" judgments in "[(TR - HA) ∩ (AT - HA) ∩ (TR - AG) ∩ (AT - AG)]".

No explicit self-referential judgment or self-vs-other contrast; judgments are about characteristics of the heard speaker. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "How trustworthy?" / "How attractive?".

Analysis reflects explicit evaluation of others’ traits (trustworthiness, attractiveness) from voices, i.e., assessing properties of another person. Construct evidence span: "trustworthiness (TR) and attractiveness (AT)" judgments in "[(TR - HA) ∩ (AT - HA) ∩ (TR - AG) ∩ (AT - AG)]".

Meets global criteria; conjunction isolates overlapping activity during social trait and emotional judgments relative to age control, capturing social/emotional appraisal of vocal stimuli. Construct evidence span: "Overlapping effects during social and emotional judgments" / "[(TR - AG) ∩ (AT - AG) ∩ (HA - AG)]".

Contrast concerns trait/emotion evaluation of unfamiliar voices, not attachment/affiliation bonding or relationship contexts. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "(TR - AG) ∩ (AT - AG) ∩ (HA - AG)".

Involves receptive processing of non-facial social signals (voices) including affect recognition (happiness) and trait impressions, both core social-communication functions. Construct evidence span: "social and emotional judgments" / "(HA - AG)" with vocal stimuli.

No self-referential processing contrast; judgments are about speaker traits/emotion. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "TR" "AT" "HA" judgments.

Explicitly evaluates others’ emotional state (happiness) and traits (trustworthiness/attractiveness) from voices—reasoning about characteristics of another person. Construct evidence span: "[(TR - AG) ∩ (AT - AG) ∩ (HA - AG)]".

Meets global criteria; conjunction captures overlap between social trait judgments and cognitive trait judgment (age) relative to happiness, reflecting social/cognitive appraisal of people from voices. Construct evidence span: "Overlapping effects during social and cognitive judgments" / "[(TR - HA) ∩ (AT - HA) ∩ (AG - HA)]".

Judging trustworthiness/attractiveness/age from voices is not an affiliation/attachment or bonding task. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "(TR - HA) ∩ (AT - HA) ∩ (AG - HA)".

Receptive non-facial social communication via auditory stimuli (voices) supporting person characterization (traits/age). Construct evidence span: "(TR - HA)" / "(AT - HA)" / "(AG - HA)" in a voice judgment task.
No manipulation of self-focused evaluation; all judgments target the heard speaker. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "How old?" etc.

Involves explicit inference about others’ stable characteristics (trustworthiness, attractiveness, age) from voices—core perception/understanding of others. Construct evidence span: "[(TR - HA) ∩ (AT - HA) ∩ (AG - HA)]".
