Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast for observing meaningful dyadic social interactions vs scrambled. Construct evidence span: "Regions more responsive to meaningful than scrambled videos".

Task involves perceiving dyadic nonverbal interactions/naturalness, not affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship evaluation. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Observing nonverbal communicative dyadic interactions (meaningful vs scrambled) indexes receptive non-facial social communication processing. Construct evidence span: "meaningful compared with scrambled videos".
No self-referential judgment or self/agency contrast; participants rate naturalness of observed others. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Observing actions/interactions of other agents (meaningful dyads vs scrambled) targets action/animacy perception of others. Construct evidence span: "meaningful compared with scrambled videos".

Healthy adults; whole-brain main effect contrast manipulating social contingency in dyadic interactions (contingent > mirrored), a core social processing manipulation. Construct evidence span: "Contingent > Mirrored".
Contrast tests movement contingency perception, not affiliation/attachment (no bonding/cooperation/relationship context). Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Contingency in nonverbal dyadic interaction is a receptive non-facial social-communication cue (coordination/contingent responding). Construct evidence span: "Contingent > Mirrored".
No self-focused processing; evaluates observed dyads only. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Contrast isolates perception/understanding of others' interactive actions (contingent vs noncontingent), fitting action perception/animacy-related social inference. Construct evidence span: "Contingent > Mirrored".
Healthy adults; whole-brain contrast for noncontingent (mirrored) vs contingent dyadic motion, still within social interaction perception task. Construct evidence span: "Mirrored > Contingent".
Mirrored vs contingent does not assess affiliation/attachment/bonding. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Manipulation affects interpretation of nonverbal dyadic coordination, a key social-communication cue even when disrupted. Construct evidence span: "Mirrored > Contingent".
No self-referential component. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Requires evaluating others' actions under atypical/noncontingent interaction structure (still action/animacy/intentionality-related). Construct evidence span: "Mirrored > Contingent".

Healthy adults; whole-brain main effect of atypical kinematics (rigid > smooth) during observation of dyadic interactions, interpreted as prompting inferences about intentions—within social processing. Construct evidence span: "Rigid > Smooth".
Kinematic fluency manipulation does not involve bonding/cooperation/attachment. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Rigid vs smooth alters perceived naturalness/communicative meaning of nonverbal movement cues in dyadic interaction (receptive non-facial communication). Construct evidence span: "Rigid > Smooth".
No self/agency or self-knowledge judgment; stimuli are others. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Targets perception of others' action kinematics and inferred intentionality/animacy (rigid/nonbiological vs smooth/biological) in observed agents. Construct evidence span: "Rigid > Smooth".
Excluded because this is an interaction contrast, which violates global criteria for inclusion. Construct evidence span: "Interaction: (Contingent > Mirrored) > (Smooth > Rigid)".
Excluded because this is an interaction contrast (global exclusion). Construct evidence span: "Interaction: (Contingent > Mirrored) > (Smooth > Rigid)".
Excluded because this is an interaction contrast (global exclusion). Construct evidence span: "Interaction: (Contingent > Mirrored) > (Smooth > Rigid)".
Excluded because this is an interaction contrast (global exclusion). Construct evidence span: "Interaction: (Contingent > Mirrored) > (Smooth > Rigid)".
Excluded because this is an interaction contrast (global exclusion). Construct evidence span: "Interaction: (Contingent > Mirrored) > (Smooth > Rigid)".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy young adult interns; group-level whole-brain parametric modulation during encoding. Construct evidence span: "intention to propagate each idea as a parametric modulator".
No evidence this analysis measures bonding/attachment/cooperation; it is about intention to recommend ideas. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Although the broader study involves communicating later, this analysis models neural responses during initial exposure/intention ratings, not communicative signal reception/production. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: whole-brain parametric modulator of interns' own intention ratings, interpreted as self-relevance/valuation. Construct evidence span: "activations in medial prefrontal cortex (MPFC) and precuneus/posterior cingulate cortex (PC/PCC) commonly associated with self-relevance processing".

This analysis focuses on the intern's own intention/preferences (self-relevance) rather than reasoning about others' mental states. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain regression (salesperson index) linked to mentalizing during encoding. Construct evidence span: "salesperson effect was exclusively associated with activity in the interns’ bilateral TPJ".
Salesperson effect indexes persuasion/mentalizing ability, not attachment/affiliation or cooperative bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Outcome relates to persuasion success, but the neural model is not of communicative cue processing/production (e.g., faces, gaze, speech perception) during interaction; it's encoding-related activity predicting influence. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
This analysis targets ability to persuade/mentalize (TPJ) rather than self-judgment or explicit self-knowledge. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: salesperson index reflects successful perspective-taking/mentalizing; TPJ is highlighted as mentalizing network. Construct evidence span: "bilateral TPJ, a primary component of the mentalizing network".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain parametric modulation by idea propagation success (social influence). Construct evidence span: "buzz effect was associated with increased activity...with mentalizing (TPJ and DMPFC)".
Buzz effect concerns message propagation/persuasion success rather than affiliative bonding or attachment. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Although about spreading messages, the fMRI analysis is during initial exposure to written/auditory show descriptions, not a social-communication task contrast (e.g., faces/gaze/speech interaction). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Buzz effect modulator is producers' aggregate preference (message success) rather than self-referential judgment. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: propagation success is tied to mentalizing regions (TPJ/DMPFC), implying reasoning about others' preferences. Construct evidence span: "mentalizing (TPJ and DMPFC)".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate conjunction) and involves social/action understanding processes during observation/execution of gestures. Construct evidence span: "(IMI_O > CT_O) ∩ (IMI_E > CT_E)".

Does not specifically measure affiliation/attachment or social bonding; it contrasts imitation vs motor control across gesture types. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Imitation/observation of communicative gestures (non-facial) is a social communication task, even though contrast is IMI>CT across gesture categories. Construct evidence span: "Imitation vs control task".

Contrast is not a self-referential judgment/manipulation (no self>other or self-trait/agency condition); mainly imitation vs control motor task. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Task requires perceiving and mapping others' actions/intentions (action perception) during observation for imitation; contrast isolates imitation network engaged by observing another's gesture. Construct evidence span: "(IMI_O > CT_O)".

Meets global criteria and targets social gesture processing (social vs non-social gestures) modulated by imitation during observation. Construct evidence span: "(S-IMI_O > NS-IMI_O) > (S-CT_O > NS-CT_O)".
Not an affiliation/attachment manipulation (no bonding/relationship, loved-one, cooperation/competition). Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Contrast specifically compares social communicative gestures vs non-social gestures during observation, modulated by intention to imitate—fits reception of non-facial communication. Construct evidence span: "S-IMI_O > NS-IMI_O".

Although study discusses self-referential processes, this specific analysis is social vs non-social gesture processing during observation and does not operationalize self-focused judgment/agency (no self condition). Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Directly targets understanding others' communicative intentions by contrasting social vs non-social gestures during observation (mental state/action understanding of others). Construct evidence span: "social vs non-social".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate activation contrast during laughter perception. Construct evidence span: "CSL>TIC".

Although global criteria are met, this contrast does not specifically measure affiliation/attachment (no bonding/close-other manipulation; it contrasts complex social vs tickling laughter). Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Task involves receptive processing of socially meaningful vocalizations (laughter types conveying socio-relational information), and contrast isolates complex social laughter vs tickling. Construct evidence span: "CSL>TIC".
Does not involve self-referential judgments/agency/self-knowledge; stimuli are others' laughter. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Complex social laughter is described as carrying socio-relational information and engaging mentalizing; contrast targets processing of others' intentions/mental states in vocal cues. Construct evidence span: "Perception of CSL, associated with more complex social information" (CSL>TIC).

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate). Contrast tests differential processing of laughter types (tickling vs complex social) within social-auditory paradigm. Construct evidence span: "TIC>CSL".

Contrast reflects acoustic complexity differences (tickling vs complex social), not affiliation/attachment bonds or close-relationship processing. Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Receptive processing of nonverbal social vocalizations; even tickling laughter is a communicative signal and contrast is within laughter perception. Construct evidence span: "TIC>CSL".
No self-referential/agency/self-knowledge component. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

This contrast is motivated as sensitivity to "higher degree of acoustic complexity of tickling laughter" rather than inference about others’ mental states/traits. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast of explicit vs implicit processing of social information in laughter. Construct evidence span: "CAT>COU".

Explicit categorization of laughter type is not an affiliation/attachment manipulation (no bonding/close-other/relationship context). Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Contrast targets explicit evaluation/recognition of socially relevant nonverbal vocal signals (laughter type categorization vs bout counting). Construct evidence span: "explicit processing of social information in the form of a laughter type categorization task (CAT)" / "CAT>COU".
No self-judgment or self-referential processing; task is categorizing heard laughter. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Categorizing laughter types entails interpreting socially meaningful cues produced by others (e.g., joy vs taunt), which relates to understanding others’ affect/intent. Construct evidence span: "laughter type categorization task (CAT)" / "explicit processing of social information".

Excluded because this is a conjunction/ROI-definition of "regions with common hemodynamic activation" rather than a task-evoked whole-brain contrast map meeting the intended univariate contrast requirement. Missing global inclusion: GLOBAL_I2.
Not a whole-brain univariate contrast; also does not measure affiliation/attachment. Missing criteria: GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not a whole-brain univariate contrast (conjunction of main effects used to define common-activation ROIs). Missing global inclusion: GLOBAL_I2.
Not a whole-brain univariate contrast and no self-related processing. Missing criteria: GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Not a whole-brain univariate contrast; conjunction used to identify common activation regions. Missing global inclusion: GLOBAL_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults, group-level whole-brain contrast of responses to romantic partner feedback. Construct evidence span: "Yes (Match + Unrequited) > No (Rejection + Disinterest)".

Romantic interest vs rejection reflects affiliative/social reward and bonding-relevant feedback within a speed-dating context. Construct evidence span: "Yes (Match + Unrequited) > No (Rejection + Disinterest)".

Although stimuli are faces/decisions, this contrast targets evaluative social feedback (interest vs rejection) rather than communication cue processing (e.g., faces>nonfaces, emotion recognition). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Yes (Match + Unrequited) > No (Rejection + Disinterest)".

Contrast is primarily about partner's decision (social reward/punishment) rather than explicit self-referential judgment or self vs other evaluation. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Yes (Match + Unrequited) > No (Rejection + Disinterest)".

Does not explicitly require reasoning about partner mental states (ToM/empathy); it contrasts received yes vs no feedback. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Yes (Match + Unrequited) > No (Rejection + Disinterest)".
Healthy adult, group-level whole-brain contrast for social feedback valence (rejection/disinterest vs acceptance). Construct evidence span: "No (Rejection + Disinterest) > Yes (Match + Unrequited)".

Negative affiliative feedback (romantic rejection/disinterest) is directly relevant to attachment/affiliation processes. Construct evidence span: "No (Rejection + Disinterest) > Yes (Match + Unrequited)".

Contrast reflects outcome evaluation (no vs yes) rather than processing communicative facial/vocal cues. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "No (Rejection + Disinterest) > Yes (Match + Unrequited)".

Not an explicit self-knowledge/agency manipulation; it is outcome feedback. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "No (Rejection + Disinterest) > Yes (Match + Unrequited)".

Does not isolate mental state inference about the partner; it is receipt of negative feedback. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "No (Rejection + Disinterest) > Yes (Match + Unrequited)".
Whole-brain group contrast of mutually expressed romantic interest (match) vs unrequited interest; directly social and task-evoked. Construct evidence span: "Match > Unrequited".
Directly indexes affiliative/bonding reward of reciprocal romantic interest vs being liked by an undesired partner. Construct evidence span: "Match > Unrequited".

Not a communication-perception contrast; both conditions involve seeing partner decisions/face stimuli, differing in relational outcome value. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Match > Unrequited".

Although matches may affect self-image, the contrast does not require explicit self-referential judgments. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Match > Unrequited".

Does not explicitly require mentalizing/empathy about others; it contrasts relational outcomes. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Match > Unrequited".

Whole-brain group contrast isolating rejection from a desired partner (rejection) vs no from an undesired partner (disinterest); social feedback processing. Construct evidence span: "Rejection > Disinterest".
Rejection from someone the participant liked is directly relevant to attachment/affiliation threat and social pain. Construct evidence span: "Rejection > Disinterest".

Targets affective evaluation of rejection vs disinterest, not decoding communicative signals. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Rejection > Disinterest".

Not an explicit self-judgment or self vs other evaluation contrast; primarily negative feedback processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Rejection > Disinterest".

Does not isolate reasoning about partner traits/mental states; it isolates rejection-specific negative feedback. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Rejection > Disinterest".

Whole-brain group contrast of expectancy violation in social feedback (mismatched vs matched romantic decisions), a core social-cognitive process. Construct evidence span: "Mismatched (Rejection + Unrequited) > matched (Match + Disinterest)".

Mismatched romantic decisions reflect disrupted reciprocity in affiliative contexts (unexpected rejection/unexpected interest), relevant to bonding dynamics. Construct evidence span: "Mismatched (Rejection + Unrequited) > matched (Match + Disinterest)".

Mismatch pertains to outcome expectancy/reciprocity rather than processing communicative cues (faces/emotions) per se. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Mismatched (Rejection + Unrequited) > matched (Match + Disinterest)".

Not an explicit self-referential judgment manipulation; mismatch may impact self but is not designed as self-knowledge/agency contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Mismatched (Rejection + Unrequited) > matched (Match + Disinterest)".

Mismatched outcomes require updating beliefs about the partner’s intentions/feelings (mentalizing) and is reported to recruit pSTS; fits understanding others’ mental states. Construct evidence span: "Mismatched (Rejection + Unrequited) > matched (Match + Disinterest)".

Whole-brain group-level parametric effect tied to surprise about others’ decisions in a social learning (RL) model during romantic feedback. Construct evidence span: "Unsigned prediction errors from RL model".

Prediction errors are computed over romantic partner interest/rejection outcomes, reflecting social learning in an affiliative context. Construct evidence span: "Unsigned prediction errors from RL model".

Parametric PE relates to outcome expectancy/surprise rather than decoding communicative signals. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Unsigned prediction errors from RL model".
RL model explicitly frames learning about one’s own desirability from others’ decisions (self-image-related expectation updating). Construct evidence span: "Unsigned prediction errors from RL model".

Prediction errors are computed on partners’ decisions and reflect updating expectations about others’ behavior; engages mentalizing-related mPFC per study rationale. Construct evidence span: "Unsigned prediction errors from RL model".

Whole-brain group contrast at partner-face phase comparing partners participant liked (said yes) vs disliked (said no), capturing anticipated social reward/risk. Construct evidence span: "Partners who were given a yes > those given a no".
Participant’s approach/interest toward a potential romantic partner (yes vs no) reflects affiliative motivation/selection. Construct evidence span: "Partners who were given a yes > those given a no".

Although faces are shown, the contrast is not faces vs nonfaces or emotion/gaze processing; it reflects participant preference/anticipation. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Partners who were given a yes > those given a no".

Not self-referential judgment; it is decision-based preference toward others. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Partners who were given a yes > those given a no".

Does not isolate understanding others’ mental states; it reflects participant’s own choice/valuation. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Partners who were given a yes > those given a no".

Whole-brain group contrast at partner-face phase comparing partners participant rejected (said no) vs accepted (said yes), part of social evaluation in romantic interaction context. Construct evidence span: "Partners who were given a no > those given a yes".
Represents reduced affiliative motivation/avoidance toward a potential romantic partner (no vs yes). Construct evidence span: "Partners who were given a no > those given a yes".

Faces are present in both conditions; contrast reflects preference/decision category rather than communicative cue processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Partners who were given a no > those given a yes".
Not an explicit self-referential task/contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Partners who were given a no > those given a yes".

Does not isolate mental-state reasoning about others; it is participant evaluation/choice. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Partners who were given a no > those given a yes".

Meets global criteria (healthy participants; group-level whole-brain ALE of person-memory fMRI studies) and the contrast targets processing of socially relevant stimuli (famous people). Construct evidence span: "FAMOUS FACES vs. BASELINE".

Contrast is famous faces vs baseline; it does not specifically operationalize affiliation/attachment or close-bond processing (e.g., loved one/close other). Construct evidence span: "FAMOUS FACES vs. BASELINE". Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Viewing/processing faces (famous faces) is a core social-communication receptive facial processing paradigm. Construct evidence span: "FAMOUS FACES vs. BASELINE".
No self-referential judgment or self-stimuli; contrast is famous faces vs baseline. Construct evidence span: "FAMOUS FACES vs. BASELINE". Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Famous-face recognition entails representing/identifying other people (person knowledge/identity), fitting perception/understanding of others. Construct evidence span: "FAMOUS FACES vs. BASELINE".

Meets global criteria (healthy participants; group-level whole-brain ALE of person-memory fMRI studies) and involves socially relevant stimuli (personally familiar people). Construct evidence span: "FAMILIAR FACES vs. BASELINE".
Personally familiar faces (friends/family) tap close social bonds and attachment-related processing even when contrasted to baseline. Construct evidence span: "FAMILIAR FACES vs. BASELINE".
Processing familiar faces is receptive facial social communication (face recognition). Construct evidence span: "FAMILIAR FACES vs. BASELINE".
No self-judgment or self-stimuli specified; it is familiar faces vs baseline. Construct evidence span: "FAMILIAR FACES vs. BASELINE". Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Recognizing/representing personally familiar others (friends/family) engages perception/understanding of others. Construct evidence span: "FAMILIAR FACES vs. BASELINE".

Conjunction map (FAMOUS ∩ FAMILIAR) is not a standard univariate whole-brain group contrast/map suitable for inclusion under GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "FAMOUS ∩ FAMILIAR FACES". Missing: GLOBAL_I2.
Conjunction (FAMOUS ∩ FAMILIAR) is not an eligible whole-brain univariate contrast/map (fails GLOBAL_I2). Construct evidence span: "FAMOUS ∩ FAMILIAR FACES". Missing: GLOBAL_I2.
Conjunction (FAMOUS ∩ FAMILIAR) is not an eligible whole-brain univariate contrast/map (fails GLOBAL_I2). Construct evidence span: "FAMOUS ∩ FAMILIAR FACES". Missing: GLOBAL_I2.
Conjunction (FAMOUS ∩ FAMILIAR) is not an eligible whole-brain univariate contrast/map (fails GLOBAL_I2). Construct evidence span: "FAMOUS ∩ FAMILIAR FACES". Missing: GLOBAL_I2.
Conjunction (FAMOUS ∩ FAMILIAR) is not an eligible whole-brain univariate contrast/map (fails GLOBAL_I2). Construct evidence span: "FAMOUS ∩ FAMILIAR FACES". Missing: GLOBAL_I2.
Meets global criteria (healthy adults; whole-brain group GLM) and uses social stimuli (faces) vs non-social control (landmarks). Construct evidence span: "Faces > Landmarks".
Faces > landmarks is general face perception and not specifically bonding/attachment. Construct evidence span: "Faces > Landmarks". Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Canonical receptive facial communication contrast (faces > non-faces). Construct evidence span: "Faces > Landmarks".
No self-related processing; stimuli are faces vs landmarks. Construct evidence span: "Faces > Landmarks". Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Faces > landmarks indexes perception of other agents/people. Construct evidence span: "Faces > Landmarks".
Landmarks > faces reflects non-social scene/place processing rather than social processing. Construct evidence span: "Landmarks > Faces". Missing: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
Landmarks > faces is not an affiliation/attachment task. Construct evidence span: "Landmarks > Faces". Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Landmarks > faces is the inverse of a face/social-communication contrast. Construct evidence span: "Landmarks > Faces". Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-processing; landmarks > faces. Construct evidence span: "Landmarks > Faces". Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Does not assess others; it is place processing relative to faces. Construct evidence span: "Landmarks > Faces". Missing: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level task-evoked whole-brain contrast). Contrast explicitly compares first-person vs third-person perspective taking during empathy-for-pain paradigm, which is a social-cognitive task. Construct evidence span: "Self > Other".

No evidence this contrast indexes affiliation/attachment (bonding/close relationships, loved-one, cooperation/competition). Construct evidence span: "Self > Other". Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Task is perspective taking/empathy for pain rather than social communication (e.g., faces/gaze/communicative signals). Construct evidence span: "Self > Other". Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Contrast directly targets self-referential perspective taking (imagining oneself in pain) vs other-perspective. Construct evidence span: "Self > Other".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level task-evoked whole-brain contrast). Contrast isolates third-person/other perspective taking vs self, a core social-cognitive manipulation. Construct evidence span: "Other > Self".

This contrast emphasizes shifting away from self perspective; not primarily self-knowledge/agency. Construct evidence span: "Other > Self". Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Other-perspective condition requires representing another individual's experience (empathy/theory-of-mind–related perspective taking). Construct evidence span: "Other > Self".

Healthy adults performing a social prediction task; group-level whole-brain parametric effect. Construct evidence span: "Simulated-other's reward prediction error".

Task involves simulating/predicting another's value-based choices, not bonding/attachment, cooperation/competition, or social comparison in an affiliation/relationship context; missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Simulated-other's reward prediction error".

No communicative signals (faces/voice/gaze/gesture) are manipulated; analysis is about prediction errors during observing another's decisions. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Simulated-other's reward prediction error".

Regressor is simulated-other's RPE (about other’s outcomes), not self-referential judgment/agency. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Simulated-other's reward prediction error".

Analysis models learning signals about another agent’s hidden valuation (simulated-other RPE), which is reasoning about others' internal states/decision process. Construct evidence span: "Simulated-other's reward prediction error".
Healthy adults in social prediction task; group-level whole-brain parametric effect. Construct evidence span: "Simulated-other's action prediction error".

Action prediction error about another’s choices does not index affiliation/attachment or relationship bonding; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Simulated-other's action prediction error".

No social-communicative stimuli or communication exchange; it is observational choice prediction. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Simulated-other's action prediction error".

Regressor concerns prediction error for other's actions, not self-referential processing. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Simulated-other's action prediction error".

Action prediction error is computed from discrepancy between predicted and observed other’s choices—core action/mental-state inference about another agent. Construct evidence span: "Simulated-other's action prediction error".

Decision-phase reward probability during the Other task reflects making predictions about another’s choices in a social learning context; group-level whole-brain map. Construct evidence span: "Reward probability".

Reward probability regressor reflects decision variable for predicting another's choices, not affiliation/attachment/cooperation/competition/relationship bonding; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward probability".
No communicative content; decision variable in observational learning task. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward probability".

Although it is the subject’s decision variable, it is not explicitly self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge; it is instrumental decision computation. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward probability".

In the Other task, reward probability is computed to predict the other’s decisions (simulation-based), supporting understanding of others’ choice behavior. Construct evidence span: "Reward probability".
This is the Control task (self reward prediction error) without a social component; missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1/SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward prediction error".
Nonsocial control learning signal; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward prediction error".
No communication stimuli or social interaction; missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward prediction error".

Standard reinforcement-learning RPE during value-based choice is not explicitly self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge as defined; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward prediction error".
No other-agent representation in the Control task; missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward prediction error".
Control task reward probability is nonsocial decision variable; missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1/SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward probability".
No affiliation/attachment content; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward probability".
No social communication processes; missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward probability".

Reward probability during nonsocial value-based choice is not explicitly self-referential judgment or self-knowledge; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward probability".
No other-agent processing in Control task; missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Reward probability".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast of task-evoked activity during emotional prosody comprehension. Construct evidence span: "Subtraction of neutral form emotional trials".

No affiliation/attachment bonding, kinship, cooperation/competition, or relationship/attachment manipulation; task is emotion/prosody recognition. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Emotional prosody comprehension is receptive non-facial social communication (decoding emotion from tone of voice) contrasted vs neutral prosody. Construct evidence span: "Subtraction of neutral form emotional trials".
Contrast does not involve self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge; it is decoding others' vocal emotions. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Task requires perceiving/inferring others' emotional states from vocal cues (emotional vs neutral prosody). Construct evidence span: "Subtraction of neutral form emotional trials".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; whole-brain univariate group contrast). Contrast isolates additional social-cognitive processing for complex vs simple emotional prosody. Construct evidence span: "Subtraction of simple from complex emotion trials".

Complex vs simple prosody does not manipulate bonding/attachment/affiliation (e.g., loved one, cooperation); it manipulates emotion complexity. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Contrast is within a social-communication task (receptive decoding of emotional tone); complex vs simple emotions reflect increased communicative/interpretive demands. Construct evidence span: "Subtraction of simple from complex emotion trials".
No self-focused appraisal; participants categorize others' expressed emotions. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Complex emotion comprehension is framed as requiring mental state attribution/ToM about others; contrast targets understanding others' emotional/mental states (complex > simple). Construct evidence span: "Subtraction of simple from complex emotion trials".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group whole-brain univariate). Contrast examines complex vs simple emotional prosody with pitch controlled, still a social-emotion comprehension manipulation. Construct evidence span: "Subtraction of simple from complex emotion trials (controlled for pitch)".

No attachment/affiliation manipulation; pitch-controlled complex vs simple prosody remains an emotion-decoding task. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Emotional prosody decoding is receptive non-facial social communication; pitch-controlled complex>simple contrast retains this communicative construct. Construct evidence span: "Subtraction of simple from complex emotion trials (controlled for pitch)".
No self-referential or agency/self-knowledge component; contrast concerns interpreting others' expressed emotions. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Complex emotion prosody comprehension (pitch-controlled) still targets inference of others' emotional/mental states; aligns with understanding others. Construct evidence span: "Subtraction of simple from complex emotion trials (controlled for pitch)".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast canonical biological motion > scrambled). Task indexes social perception via biological motion perception. Construct evidence span: "activation during observation of biological motion displays, as compared with scrambled displays".

No affiliation/attachment manipulation (e.g., bonding, kinship, cooperation) in biological motion > scrambled contrast; thus local inclusion AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2 not met.

Task is biological motion perception (canonical vs scrambled) rather than social communication (faces/gaze/expressive/communicative signals); local inclusion SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2 not met. Construct evidence span: "biological motion displays, as compared with scrambled displays".
No self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge contrast; local inclusion PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/PERCEPTION_SELF_I2 not met.

Canonical vs scrambled point-light human locomotion is an action/animacy perception task about others, matching Perception/Understanding of Others. Construct evidence span: "biological motion displays, as compared with scrambled displays".

Excluded because this is a seed-voxel functional connectivity analysis, not a group-level univariate whole-brain task-evoked activation contrast. Construct evidence span: "functional connectivity with the left cerebellar lobule Crus I".

Excluded because this is a seed-voxel functional connectivity analysis (and not an affiliation/attachment task). Construct evidence span: "functional connectivity with the left cerebellar lobule Crus I".
Excluded because this is a seed-voxel functional connectivity analysis, which is globally excluded. Construct evidence span: "functional connectivity with the left cerebellar lobule Crus I".
Excluded because this is a seed-voxel functional connectivity analysis, which is globally excluded. Construct evidence span: "functional connectivity with the left cerebellar lobule Crus I".

Excluded because this is a seed-voxel functional connectivity analysis (effective/functional connectivity), which is globally excluded. Construct evidence span: "functional connectivity with the left cerebellar lobule Crus I".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain Stroop contrast), but this contrast is executive conflict (Stroop) rather than social processing; social interaction factor is collapsed across Human+Machine. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.

Global criteria met, but contrast is Stroop interference collapsed across opponent type and does not measure affiliation/attachment/competition per se. Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global criteria met, but no social-communication stimulus/contrast (e.g., faces/gaze/voice) is isolated; this is Stroop interference. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Global criteria met, but no self-referential judgment/agency/self>other manipulation; Stroop conflict only. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Global criteria met, but no explicit ToM/empathy/other-mental-state contrast; opponent factor is not tested here. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Global criteria met, but contrast is Congruent>Incongruent (reverse Stroop) collapsed across Human+Machine and does not measure social processing. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
Global criteria met, but not a competition/affiliation contrast; it is reverse Stroop. Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Global criteria met, but no social communication manipulation; reverse Stroop only. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Global criteria met, but no self-related processing contrast. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Global criteria met, but no explicit other-mental-state processing contrast. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Human–human competition vs machine competition is a social-interaction/mentalizing manipulation at group-level whole-brain in healthy adults. Construct evidence span: "H(I + C) > M(I + C)".

Contrast targets social competition (human opponent vs machine), fitting affiliation/attachment domain examples (competition/social comparison) at whole-brain group level. Construct evidence span: "H(I + C) > M(I + C)".

Although social competition is manipulated, the task does not isolate communicative signals (faces/gaze/voice); it is opponent-type/ToM context rather than communication. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Competition may involve self-monitoring, but this contrast does not explicitly manipulate self-referential judgments (no Self>Other). Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Human vs machine opponent manipulation is intended to elicit Theory of Mind/understanding opponent intentions (other-mental-state reasoning). Construct evidence span: "H(I + C) > M(I + C)".

Global criteria met, but this is the opposite direction (Machine>Human) and the paper reports no suprathreshold voxels; without a reported whole-brain social effect, cannot include. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
Global criteria met, but Machine>Human yields no suprathreshold voxels (no effect to annotate). Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
No social communication construct; also no suprathreshold voxels. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self construct and no suprathreshold voxels. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Opposite of ToM manipulation and no suprathreshold voxels; cannot include. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
This is an interaction contrast ([H(I>C)]>[M(I>C)]), which is globally excluded as an interaction. Construct evidence span: "[H(I > C)] > [M(I > C)]".
Interaction contrast is globally excluded. Construct evidence span: "[H(I > C)] > [M(I > C)]".
Interaction contrast is globally excluded. Construct evidence span: "[H(I > C)] > [M(I > C)]".

Interaction contrast ([M(I>C)]>[H(I>C)]) is globally excluded; additionally reported as no suprathreshold voxels. Construct evidence span: "[M(I > C)] > [H(I > C)]".
Interaction contrast is globally excluded; also no suprathreshold voxels. Construct evidence span: "[M(I > C)] > [H(I > C)]".

Global inclusion criteria met (healthy adults; group-level task-evoked whole-brain contrast). Contrast targets prosocial decisions toward family (social context) and recruits mentalizing/self-control regions. Construct evidence span: "Costly-Donation relative to Noncostly-Reward trials engaged brain regions involved in mentalizing".

Global inclusion criteria met. Task involves prosocial costly giving to family (kinship/close-relationship context), relevant to affiliation/attachment processes. Construct evidence span: "prosocial behaviors towards the family".

No social-communication stimuli or contrasts (e.g., faces/voices/gaze) are involved; contrast is economic decision-making about donations/rewards. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Contrast does not involve explicit self-referential judgments or self vs other evaluations; it is donation vs reward decision-making. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Global inclusion criteria met. Costly donations to family plausibly require reasoning about others' needs/values (mentalizing); analysis reports DMPFC activation linked with mentalizing. Construct evidence span: "greater activity in the DMPFC, a region linked with mentalizing".

Excluded because this is a whole-brain regression with family obligation preferences (individual-differences/ROI-constrained) rather than a standalone generalizable whole-brain task-evoked map for the sample; additionally relies on a priori ROI correction. Exclusion/criteria issues: violates GLOBAL_I2 and triggers ROI exclusion. Missing inclusion criterion: GLOBAL_I2.

Excluded due to ROI-based analysis/regression (a priori self-control/mentalizing ROIs) rather than eligible whole-brain map. Construct evidence span: "All a priori regions are listed... corrected... within the regions of interest".
Excluded due to ROI-based analysis/regression and also no social communication construct. Exclusion applies.
Excluded due to ROI-based analysis/regression and no self-referential judgment contrast. Exclusion applies.
Excluded due to ROI-based analysis/regression (mentalizing ROI) rather than eligible whole-brain map. Construct evidence span: "corrected for multiple comparisons within the regions of interest".
Excluded because this is a PPI seed-based connectivity analysis with ventral striatum. Construct evidence span: "psychophysiological interaction (PPI) analyses".

Excluded because this is a PPI seed-based connectivity analysis (ventral striatum connectivity), not an eligible univariate task-evoked map. Construct evidence span: "functional coupling between the ventral striatum and the left VLPFC".
Excluded because this is a PPI seed-based connectivity analysis and not social-communication content. Exclusion applies.
Excluded because this is a PPI seed-based connectivity analysis. Exclusion applies.

Excluded because this is a PPI seed-based connectivity analysis (ventral striatum coupling with MPFC/DMPFC). Construct evidence span: "functional coupling between the ventral striatum and ... MPFC and DMPFC".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast). The contrast isolates retrieval of social context during episodic memory, i.e., processing socially contextual information. Construct evidence span: "Activations in the retrieval of social context" / "(rSS + rSO) − (rNS + rNO)".

Although global criteria are met, this contrast is about social vs solitary context in memory retrieval rather than affiliation/attachment bonding, kinship, or cooperation per se; no explicit attachment/affiliation construct is measured. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Although global criteria are met, the contrast does not test communicative signal reception/production (faces, gaze, speech pragmatics); it tests social context effects in episodic retrieval. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Although global criteria are met, this analysis targets social context retrieval (social vs solitary), not self-referential judgment or self>other agency/self-knowledge. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Although global criteria are met, the contrast is not about reasoning about others' mental states/traits or action perception; it's social-context vs non-social context during word recognition. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast). The contrast tests self-generated vs other-generated encoding context during retrieval, engaging self-referential/social-cognitive processing within social interaction paradigm. Construct evidence span: "Activations in the retrieval of self-generation" / "(rSS + rNS) − (rSO + rNO)".

Although global criteria are met, the analysis examines self-generation/source effects in episodic retrieval, not affiliation/attachment, bonding, or cooperation/competition outcomes. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Although global criteria are met, the analysis does not measure social communication signals (faces/gaze/prosody) but source/self-generation effects during word recognition. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Meets global criteria and specifically targets self-related source/self-generation (self vs other) during retrieval, consistent with self-referential processing. Construct evidence span: "self-generation {(rSS + rNS) − (rSO + rNO)}".

Although the contrast includes other-generated items in the comparison, it is not designed to measure understanding others' mental states/traits; it's primarily self vs other source/self-generation. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate conjunction for Cartoons vs Neutral). Humor-cartoon processing is a social-cognitive task involving interpreting socially meaningful humorous scenes. Construct evidence span: "conjunction analysis for cartoons vs. neutral pictures".

Not an affiliation/attachment paradigm (no bonding, loved-one/kinship, cooperation/competition, or relationship manipulation). Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "cartoons vs. neutral pictures".

Task is viewing humorous cartoons (not faces/gaze/prosody/communicative signals) and contrast is cartoons vs neutral, so it does not specifically measure social communication. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "cartoons vs. neutral pictures".

No self-referential judgment or self vs other manipulation; cartoons vs neutral does not measure self-perception. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "cartoons vs. neutral pictures".

Although humor can involve mentalizing, this specific analysis/contrast is not designed to measure perception/understanding of others (no ToM/empathy condition or other-targeted contrast). Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "cartoons vs. neutral pictures".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate conjunction for cartoons parametric modulation with funniness ratings). Humor appreciation is part of social cognition broadly construed. Construct evidence span: "conjunction analysis for the parametric modulation".

Parametric modulation of funniness during cartoon viewing does not index affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship formation. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "parametric modulation".

No explicit social-communicative cue processing (faces, gaze, prosody, communicative gestures) or communication contrast; it is funniness-parametric modulation. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "parametric modulation".

Although participants provide subjective ratings, the analysis is not self-referential cognition (no self-trait/state judgment contrast such as Self>Other). Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "parametric modulation".

This parametric modulation reflects subjective amusement rather than an explicit other-mental-state or empathy manipulation/contrast. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "parametric modulation".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast (SI-NSI) isolating social interaction processing. Construct evidence span: "Brain areas showing increased activity in response to the social interaction condition".

Global criteria are met, but this contrast is not designed to measure bonding/attachment or affiliative valuation (no loved-one/kinship/cooperation manipulation). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "social interaction condition" (SI) vs "no social interaction" (NSI) point-light displays.

Global criteria are met; task requires perceiving and categorizing nonverbal social interaction cues from whole-body point-light displays (receptive non-facial social communication). Construct evidence span: "observation of social interaction displays (SI) versus non social interaction displays (NSI)".

Global criteria are met, but no self-referential judgment, self-agency, or self-knowledge manipulation/contrast is present. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Are the two persons acting together or separately?".

Global criteria are met; contrast targets perception/understanding of others' actions and social intentions from biological motion (point-light) and social interaction detection (action perception/mental state inference). Construct evidence span: "understanding the social intentions carried by body language only" / "SI - NSI".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task-evoked map for a social preference judgment about faces. Construct evidence span: "Name: Preference".

Task is social evaluation of faces ('like?') but does not specifically operationalize affiliation/attachment (e.g., bonding/kinship/cooperation). Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Preference".

Although faces are viewed, the analysis is not a communication contrast (e.g., faces>nonfaces or emotion recognition) but a generic task main effect; construct-specific evidence for social communication is insufficient. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Preference".
Preference judgment is explicitly self-referential ('Do I like this person?'), indexing self-related appraisal. Construct evidence span: "Name: Preference".
Participants evaluate another person (face) and form a social judgment, engaging perception/assessment of others. Construct evidence span: "Name: Preference".
Gender identification is a nonsocial control task (perceptual/decision) and does not measure social processing per se. Missing: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Gender".
Gender identification does not operationalize affiliation/attachment. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Gender".
Viewing and categorizing face gender engages receptive facial communication (face perception). Construct evidence span: "Name: Gender".
Task does not involve self-referential judgment. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Gender".
Gender identification requires extracting person-related attributes from others' faces (other-perception). Construct evidence span: "Name: Gender".
Whole-brain group contrast isolates social preference evaluation relative to gender identification, directly measuring social processing. Construct evidence span: "Name: Preference > Gender".

Contrast reflects subjective liking judgment but not affiliation/attachment behavior or relationship/bond manipulation. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Preference > Gender".

Both conditions involve viewing faces; the contrast targets preference evaluation rather than communication/face perception per se, so social communication is not specifically measured. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Preference > Gender".
Preference judgment is self-referential appraisal relative to gender task. Construct evidence span: "Name: Preference > Gender".
Contrast involves evaluating others (faces) for liking, a social evaluation of another person. Construct evidence span: "Name: Preference > Gender".

Reverse contrast (Gender > Preference) reflects greater engagement for gender identification/control and/or deactivation differences, not increased social processing. Missing: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Gender > Preference".
Does not measure affiliation/attachment. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Gender > Preference".

While both tasks involve faces, this contrast does not isolate communicative face processing; it captures gender identification relative to preference evaluation. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Gender > Preference".
No self-referential processing isolated in this reverse contrast. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name: Gender > Preference".
Contrast emphasizes gender categorization of others' faces relative to preference judgment; still an other-perception attribute extraction task. Construct evidence span: "Name: Gender > Preference".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast). Contrast indexes live contingent social interaction vs recorded interaction, i.e., task-evoked social processing broadly. Construct evidence span: "Live>Recorded".

Global criteria met. Live vs recorded contrasts contingent interaction/self-relevance and cooperative social engagement, which can index affiliative/cooperative bonding/reward aspects of social interaction. Construct evidence span: "Live>Recorded".

Global criteria met. Live interaction involves reciprocal face-to-face communication cues (gaze/speech) with contingency relative to recorded viewing, fitting social communication (receptive/interactive signaling). Construct evidence span: "Live>Recorded".

Not included: although Live>Recorded may involve self-relevance, this contrast is not designed to isolate self-referential judgment/agency (no Self>Other or explicit self-evaluation). Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Global criteria met. Live>Recorded targets interpreting another person's contingent actions/speech (social cognition/mentalizing and action perception). Construct evidence span: "Live>Recorded".

Not included: contrast is Recorded>Live, emphasizing reduced engagement during live interaction and greater activity in motor regions; does not measure social processing construct positively. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
Not included: Recorded>Live does not operationalize affiliation/attachment or cooperation-related bonding. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not included: contrast does not index communicative cue processing; reported effects are motor/supplementary motor areas. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Not included: no self-referential/agency manipulation; Recorded>Live mainly reflects motor differences. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Not included: Recorded>Live is not a measure of understanding others; effects are not framed as other-perception/mental state processing. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate JA>SA). Joint attention is a core social processing component. Construct evidence span: "JA>SA".

Not included: JA>SA isolates coordinating attention via gaze, not affiliation/attachment or bonding/cooperation per se. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Global criteria met. Joint attention via gaze-following with a live partner is social communication (nonverbal communicative signaling). Construct evidence span: "JA>SA".

Not included: contrast does not target self-knowledge or agency/self-referential judgment; it targets joint vs solo attention with partner cues. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Global criteria met. JA>SA requires interpreting the experimenter’s gaze/intent to locate target (action perception/mental state inference). Construct evidence span: "JA>SA".

Not included: SA>JA (if any) would reflect greater activation during solo attention; table reports no regions with SA>JA, so no positive social-processing effect to include. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
Not included: SA>JA does not operationalize affiliation/attachment. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not included: SA>JA is not a social communication contrast (and no significant SA>JA activations reported). Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Not included: no explicit self-referential manipulation; SA>JA pertains to attention condition and has no reported activations. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Not included: SA>JA would not index understanding others (and no significant SA>JA activations reported). Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain place>body contrast) and the task is explicitly social (joint attention/attributing what another person sees). Construct evidence span: "Places > bodies".

Global criteria pass, but the contrast does not operationalize bonding/attachment, kinship, cooperation/competition, or relationship-focused evaluation; instead it varies stimulus category (place vs body) during gaze-based joint attention. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Although the paradigm involves gaze-following/joint attention, this specific contrast (places > bodies) tests attributed content category rather than a communication manipulation (e.g., gaze direction, faces>nonfaces). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge manipulation; contrast is places > bodies while observing another's gaze. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Meets global criteria and the task requires representing another agent's perceptual/mental state (what the avatar sees) even though the contrast is category-based. Construct evidence span: "Places > bodies".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain body>place contrast) and the task is social cognition/joint attention to what another person sees. Construct evidence span: "Bodies > places".

Global criteria pass, but the contrast does not probe affiliation/attachment, social bonding, or relationship valuation; it is a stimulus-category contrast (body vs place) during observation of another's gaze. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Despite use of gaze and an agent, this contrast (bodies > places) is not a social communication manipulation (e.g., gaze direction, facial expressions, communicative signals) but a content-category comparison. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-related processing is contrasted; participants observe an avatar and the contrast varies what the avatar is viewing. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Meets global criteria and directly pertains to understanding others’ perceptual/mental state content (what another person sees), consistent with ToM/joint attention framing. Construct evidence span: "Bodies > places".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate task contrast. Task involves self-referential trait judgments (social-cognitive/self-processing) contrasted with syllable counting. Construct evidence span: "Self > syllables".

Contrast is self-trait judgment vs syllable counting; does not specifically measure affiliation/attachment or bonding (no loved-one/relationship manipulation). Missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self > syllables".

No communicative stimuli/behavior (e.g., faces, gaze, emotion recognition); this is self-trait appraisal vs syllable counting. Missing local inclusion criteria SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self > syllables".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate task contrast. Self-referential trait judgment directly targets self-knowledge. Construct evidence span: "Self > syllables".

This contrast isolates self-referential processing relative to a non-social control; it does not require reasoning about others. Missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self > syllables".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate task contrast. Task involves judging personality traits of a friend (other-referential social cognition) vs syllable counting. Construct evidence span: "Other > syllables".

Although the target is a close friend, the contrast measures trait inference about another (social cognition) rather than attachment/affiliative bonding or relationship evaluation per se. Missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Other > syllables".

No social-communicative signal processing (faces/gaze/prosody) is manipulated; it is trait judgment about a friend vs syllable counting. Missing local inclusion criteria SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Other > syllables".

This contrast targets other-referential trait judgments rather than self-knowledge; self-processing is not required beyond general task demands. Missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Other > syllables".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate task contrast. Judging whether trait words describe a friend requires representing/assessing another person’s traits. Construct evidence span: "Other > syllables".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate task contrast. Directly contrasts self vs other trait judgments, both social-cognitive references. Construct evidence span: "Self > other".

Self vs friend trait-judgment contrast does not measure affiliative bonding/attachment; it measures reference (self vs other) in trait evaluation. Missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self > other".

No social communication stimuli or communicative exchange is involved; it is reference-based trait appraisal. Missing local inclusion criteria SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self > other".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast isolates self-referential trait processing relative to judgments about another person, mapping to self-knowledge. Construct evidence span: "Self > other".

Direction is self > other; does not index processing/understanding of others (would be other > self). Missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Self > other".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate task contrast. Directly contrasts trait judgments about a friend vs self, both within social-cognitive domain. Construct evidence span: "Other > self".

Although the target is a friend, the contrast measures other-referential trait inference relative to self, not affiliative/attachment processes (no bonding manipulation like loved one vs stranger). Missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Other > self".

No communicative cue processing; contrast is other- vs self-referential trait appraisal. Missing local inclusion criteria SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Other > self".
Direction is other > self; does not isolate self-processing. Missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Other > self".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast isolates processing/representation of another person’s traits relative to self. Construct evidence span: "Other > self".

Healthy adults and group-level whole-brain task-evoked contrast. Contrast targets empathic/social processing elicited by viewing emotional social scenes. Construct evidence span: "Emotional > neutral stimuli".

Global criteria met, but contrast is emotional vs neutral scenes and does not specifically measure bonding/attachment or affiliative interaction. Missing local inclusion criteria (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2).

Global criteria met, but stimuli are line drawings of social situations (faces intentionally blank) and contrast is emotional vs neutral; not a communication signal (faces/gaze/voice/gesture) contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2).
Global criteria met, but no self-referential judgment/agency/self vs other manipulation. Missing local inclusion criteria (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2).

Healthy adults and whole-brain contrast; emotional vs neutral social scenes requires inferring/processing others' affect from context/body posture (empathy-related). Construct evidence span: "Emotional > neutral stimuli".

Healthy adults and group-level whole-brain task-evoked inverse social contrast; reduced responses for emotional vs neutral during viewing of social scenes still indexes social processing modulation by emotional content. Construct evidence span: "Neutral > emotional stimuli".

Global criteria met, but contrast does not assess affiliative bonding/attachment (e.g., loved one, closeness, cooperation). Missing local inclusion criteria (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2).
Global criteria met, but no explicit communicative signal processing manipulation (faces/gaze/voice/gesture). Missing local inclusion criteria (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2).
Global criteria met, but no self-focused processing or self vs other contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2).

Healthy adults and whole-brain contrast; neutral vs emotional social scenes modulates processing of others' affective states (empathy-related) albeit in the opposite direction. Construct evidence span: "Neutral > emotional stimuli".

Healthy adults and group-level whole-brain task-evoked contrast; two-person (social relation) vs one-person scenes probes social cognition/interaction perception. Construct evidence span: "Social relation > single person".

Global criteria met, but manipulation is number of persons (social relation vs single), not affiliative bonding/attachment or cooperation/competition. Missing local inclusion criteria (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2).

Global criteria met, but not focused on communicative signals (faces/gaze/voice/gesture) and drawings lack facial features; contrast is social relation vs single person. Missing local inclusion criteria (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2).
Global criteria met, but no self-referential component. Missing local inclusion criteria (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2).

Healthy adults and whole-brain contrast; perceiving/understanding others and their relationship (two persons vs one) engages mentalizing/social relation inference. Construct evidence span: "Social relation > single person".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain GLM contrast). This ToM>PI contrast measures social cognition/mentalizing during naturalistic movie viewing. Construct evidence span: "Movie scenes theory of mind (ToM) > movie scenes physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met but task/contrast is Theory of Mind (mental state inference) rather than bonding/attachment/affiliation (e.g., loved-one, cooperation/competition). Missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "theory of mind (ToM) > ... physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met but contrast targets mental state reasoning (ToM) vs physical inference; it is not specifically a social-communication manipulation (e.g., faces>nonfaces, gaze, vocal prosody). Missing local inclusion criteria SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Movie scenes theory of mind (ToM) > movie scenes physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met but no self-referential judgment or self>other manipulation; participants infer others' mental states. Missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "theory of mind (ToM) > ... physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain GLM). Contrast is explicit/implicit Theory of Mind about characters (reasoning about others’ feelings/thoughts/intentions) vs physical inference control. Construct evidence span: "Movie scenes theory of mind (ToM) > movie scenes physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain GLM contrast). Silent answer ToM>PI indexes explicit mental state reasoning (social cognition). Construct evidence span: "Silent answer theory of mind (ToM) > silent answer physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met but no affiliation/attachment manipulation; contrast is ToM mental state inference vs physical inference. Missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Silent answer theory of mind (ToM) > ... physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met but silent ToM reasoning is not a social-communication task/contrast (no facial/vocal/gaze communication manipulation). Missing local inclusion criteria SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Silent answer theory of mind (ToM) > silent answer physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met but no self-referential processing contrast; task is about others. Missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Silent answer theory of mind (ToM) > silent answer physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; whole-brain GLM). Silent-answer ToM>PI reflects explicit inference about others' mental states (thoughts/intentions/emotions) vs physical inference. Construct evidence span: "Silent answer theory of mind (ToM) > silent answer physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain GLM contrast). MC answer ToM>PI measures explicit mentalizing/social cognition. Construct evidence span: "MC answer theory of mind (ToM) > MC answer physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met but contrast is ToM vs physical inference, not affiliation/attachment (no bonding/relationship or loved-one manipulation). Missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "MC answer theory of mind (ToM) > ... physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met but this is explicit ToM reasoning; not a social communication-specific contrast (e.g., faces, gaze, prosody). Missing local inclusion criteria SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "MC answer theory of mind (ToM) > MC answer physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met but no self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "MC answer theory of mind (ToM) > MC answer physical inference (PI)".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain GLM). MC answer ToM>PI explicitly requires reasoning about others' feelings/thoughts/intentions vs physical inference control. Construct evidence span: "MC answer theory of mind (ToM) > MC answer physical inference (PI)".
