Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level univariate whole-brain main effect). Task involves viewing social stimuli (eyes) and engages face/social salience processing. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of viewing eyes".

Task is eye/face perception and pupillary coherence; does not measure bonding/attachment, cooperation/competition, or kinship/relationship contrasts; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Whole-brain main effect during receptive facial communication (viewing isolated eyes). Construct evidence span: "Main effect of viewing eyes".
Analysis is perception of external eye stimuli; no self-referential judgment/agency manipulation; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Viewing others' eyes supports perception of others/social cues, even without explicit mental-state reasoning. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of viewing eyes".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level univariate whole-brain main effect). Contrast indexes processing of dynamic pupil changes in another during social viewing context. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of change in observed and observer's pupil size".
No affiliation/attachment manipulation (e.g., loved one, cooperation/competition); missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Dynamic changes in pupils/eyes are receptive facial communication cues; analysis tests brain response to change in observed pupil size. Construct evidence span: "change in observed and observer's pupil size".

Although observer pupil varies, the contrast is not self-focused judgment/agency; it models variance in observed/observer pupil size during viewing, not self-referential processing; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Analysis targets perception of others' autonomic cue (pupil size) as socially salient information. Construct evidence span: "change in observed pupil size".

Global exclusion: contrast is task vs REST ("STEP1+STEP2 - REST"), not a task-evoked whole-brain map isolating social processing; fails to specifically measure social processing (missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP1+STEP2 - REST".
Fails local construct: no affiliation/attachment manipulation (missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP1+STEP2 - REST".

Fails local construct: passive listening to footsteps vs rest does not measure social communication (missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP1+STEP2 - REST".
Fails local construct: no self-referential judgment or self-related stimuli (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP1+STEP2 - REST".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast). Local criteria met: auditory biological action perception of others (human footsteps) vs rest. Construct evidence span: "STEP1+STEP2 - REST".

Global exclusion: control auditory noise vs REST ("NOISE1+NOISE2 - REST") does not measure social processing (missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2). Construct evidence span: "NOISE1+NOISE2 - REST".
Fails local construct: nonsocial noise condition; no affiliation/attachment content (missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2). Construct evidence span: "NOISE1+NOISE2 - REST".
Fails local construct: nonsocial noise listening does not measure social communication (missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2). Construct evidence span: "NOISE1+NOISE2 - REST".
Fails local construct: no self-perception task or stimuli (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "NOISE1+NOISE2 - REST".
Fails local construct: nonsocial noise does not involve perceiving/understanding others (missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2). Construct evidence span: "NOISE1+NOISE2 - REST".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate). Local criteria met: isolates increased responses to socially complex auditory scene (two walkers) beyond acoustic controls. Construct evidence span: "(STEP2 - NOISE2) - (STEP1 - NOISE1)".

Fails local construct: contrast manipulates number of walkers/scene complexity, not bonding/attachment/cooperation (missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2). Construct evidence span: "(STEP2 - NOISE2) - (STEP1 - NOISE1)".

Fails local construct: no communicative signals (speech, faces, gestures) or explicit communication task; just footsteps (missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2). Construct evidence span: "(STEP2 - NOISE2) - (STEP1 - NOISE1)".
Fails local construct: no self-related judgments/stimuli (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "(STEP2 - NOISE2) - (STEP1 - NOISE1)".

Global criteria met. Local criteria met: contrast targets action/animacy perception of others in an auditory social scene (two agents vs one), controlling for acoustics. Construct evidence span: "(STEP2 - NOISE2) - (STEP1 - NOISE1)".
Global criteria met. Local criteria met: human footsteps (agent/action sounds) contrasted with matched noise engages social-cue processing broadly. Construct evidence span: "STEP1 - NOISE1".

Fails local construct: single-person walking vs noise does not index affiliation/attachment/bonding (missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP1 - NOISE1".

Fails local construct: non-communicative action sounds; no communication exchange or recognition task (missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP1 - NOISE1".
Fails local construct: no self-referential component (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP1 - NOISE1".
Global criteria met. Local criteria met: perceiving others’ actions/biological motion from auditory footsteps vs matched noise. Construct evidence span: "STEP1 - NOISE1".
Global criteria met. Local criteria met: two-person walking together vs matched noise isolates processing of a more socially complex auditory scene. Construct evidence span: "STEP2 - NOISE2".

Fails local construct: two people walking together is not an affiliation/attachment manipulation (no bonding/cooperation/relationship context; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP2 - NOISE2".

Fails local construct: footsteps are non-facial, nonverbal action cues but not communicative exchange; no social-communication task/contrast (missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP2 - NOISE2".
Fails local construct: no self-perception/self-judgment manipulation (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP2 - NOISE2".
Global criteria met. Local criteria met: auditory action/animacy perception of others with multiple agents (two walkers) vs matched noise. Construct evidence span: "STEP2 - NOISE2".

Fails local construct specificity: STEP2 - STEP1 mainly captures acoustic/quantity differences between two vs one walker rather than a controlled social-processing measure (missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP2 - STEP1".
Fails local construct: no affiliation/attachment manipulation (missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP2 - STEP1".
Fails local construct: no communication signals/task (missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP2 - STEP1".
Fails local construct: no self-related processing (missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP2 - STEP1".

Fails local construct specificity: two vs one walker without acoustic control does not cleanly measure perception/understanding of others (missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2). Construct evidence span: "STEP2 - STEP1".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast) and targets social interaction observation. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of type of observed action [(Social Gaze + Social No-gaze) – (Individual gaze + Individual No-gaze)]".

Contrast concerns observing social vs individual actions, not bonding/affiliation or relationship-based attachment. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Although stimuli include gaze, this analysis is a main effect of social vs individual action (interaction context) rather than a communication-specific contrast (e.g., gaze present vs absent, faces vs non-faces). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-referential judgment or self-perception manipulation; participants observe others. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Whole-brain contrast in healthy adults during observation/interpretation of others' actions and social interactions (social vs individual). Construct evidence span: "type of observed action [(Social Gaze + Social No-gaze) – (Individual gaze + Individual No-gaze)]".

Meets global criteria and manipulates availability of others' gaze, a core social cue. Construct evidence span: "Main effect of gaze [(Social Gaze + Individual Gaze) – (Social No-gaze + Individual No-gaze)]".
Gaze availability is a social cue but not an affiliation/attachment or bonding manipulation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Directly tests receptive processing of gaze cues (present vs masked), aligning with non-facial communication reception. Construct evidence span: "main effect of gaze" / "GAZE versus NO-GAZE".
No self-referential processing; gaze pertains to others. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Gaze is an important cue for inferring others’ intentions/mental states; this contrast isolates gaze-cue processing during observation of others. Construct evidence span: "(Social Gaze + Individual Gaze) – (Social No-gaze + Individual No-gaze)".

Meets global criteria; interaction tests how gaze modulates neural response to social vs individual actions (social interaction understanding). Construct evidence span: "Interaction type of observed action by gaze" / "[(Individual Gaze + Social No-gaze) – (Individual No-gaze + Social Gaze)]".

Interaction concerns social vs individual action observation under gaze/no-gaze, not attachment/affiliation bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Interaction explicitly involves gaze cue availability (a communicative signal) and its effect on interpreting observed actions. Construct evidence span: "type of observed action by gaze".
No self-focused manipulation; participants observe others’ interactions. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Interaction probes inferential processing about others’ actions/intentions when gaze cues are absent vs present, consistent with understanding others. Construct evidence span: "interaction [(Individual Gaze + Social No-gaze) – (Individual No-gaze + Social Gaze)]".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain Angry>Happy contrast) and indexes social perception of emotional facial expressions during social approach. Construct evidence span: "Angry>Happy".

Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Angry>Happy".
Contrast involves receptive facial communication/emotion recognition (angry vs happy expressions with eye contact) during passive viewing. Construct evidence span: "Angry>Happy".

No self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge manipulation; participants passively view others. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Angry>Happy".

Assesses understanding others' emotional state/intent from dynamic facial expression and approach (threat vs friendly), i.e., perception/understanding of others. Construct evidence span: "Angry>Happy".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain Happy>Angry contrast) and indexes social perception of positive/affiliative facial expressions during social approach. Construct evidence span: "Happy>Angry".

Happy>Angry reflects processing of positive facial affect/approach but not an attachment/affiliation-bonding task (no relationship, cooperation, or attachment measure). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Happy>Angry".
Contrast involves receptive facial communication (happy vs angry expressions; interpreting socially relevant facial affect). Construct evidence span: "Happy>Angry".
No self-focused processing required; stimuli concern others' expressions/approach. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Happy>Angry".
Indexes perception/understanding of others' positive emotional state/affiliative intent from dynamic social approach. Construct evidence span: "Happy>Angry".

Whole-brain group contrast in healthy adults; card-phase descriptive rule violation detection contrasted with precaution violation detection within social behavior scenarios. Construct evidence span: "Cards: Descriptives > Precautions".
Not measuring affiliation/attachment. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Cards: Descriptives > Precautions".
Not social communication. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Cards: Descriptives > Precautions".
No self component. Missing: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Cards: Descriptives > Precautions".
Descriptive violations require representing others' behaviors/traits relative to a rule (other-understanding). Construct evidence span: "Cards: Descriptives > Precautions".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast). Task manipulates attribution of intentions/mentalizing about a presumed human agent vs computer, which is social processing broadly. Construct evidence span: "Contrasting the brain activity of the Composer condition against the Computer condition".

Global criteria met, but contrast is about attributing intentions to a composer vs computer and does not assess bonding, attachment, cooperation/competition, or close-relationship evaluation; missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Composer condition against the Computer condition".

Global criteria met, but no communicative signal exchange (faces, gaze, vocal prosody as communication, gestures) is measured; participants listen to music labeled composer vs computer and rate pleasantness. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "rate the perceived pleasantness of each piece of music".

Global criteria met, but analysis is not self-referential judgment or self-agency; it concerns inferring intentions of an external creator. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "attributing the expression of intentions to the composed pieces".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain). Contrast targets theory-of-mind/mental state attribution about another (composer) vs non-agent computer, matching understanding others' mental states. Construct evidence span: "activations in the cortical network typically reported for mental state attribution".

Analysis labeled "Not predicted" lacks description of the contrast/statistical map, so cannot verify it is a task-evoked group-level whole-brain map meeting GLOBAL_I2 nor that it measures social processing (SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1/ I2). Missing GLOBAL_I2, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Not predicted".

Insufficient analysis-level detail to confirm a whole-brain group-level task-evoked map (GLOBAL_I2) or an affiliation/attachment construct (AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/I2). Missing GLOBAL_I2, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Not predicted".

Insufficient analysis-level detail to confirm whole-brain group-level task-evoked evidence (GLOBAL_I2) or a social communication contrast (SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1/I2). Missing GLOBAL_I2, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Not predicted".

Insufficient analysis-level detail to confirm whole-brain group-level task-evoked evidence (GLOBAL_I2) or self-related processing (PERCEPTION_SELF_I1/I2). Missing GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Not predicted".

Insufficient analysis-level detail to confirm whole-brain group-level task-evoked evidence (GLOBAL_I2) or other-mental-state/action/animacy perception (PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1/I2). Missing GLOBAL_I2, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Not predicted".

Global exclusion applies: this analysis is a task-evoked whole-brain TD prediction-error map but for the Pavlovian (non-social) task (fractals predicting water). Construct evidence span: "P". Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.

Not an affiliation/attachment task; Pavlovian conditioning with water reinforcement. Construct evidence span: "P". Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Not a social communication task (no faces, gaze, prosody, communicative signals); Pavlovian water-learning. Construct evidence span: "P". Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Does not require self-referential judgment/agency manipulation; Pavlovian water-learning. Construct evidence span: "P". Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
No reasoning about others/mental states/action perception; Pavlovian water-learning. Construct evidence span: "P". Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain TD prediction-error map during a social interaction/inclusion-exclusion learning task. Construct evidence span: "Social task".

Social inclusion/exclusion (group belonging) is an affiliation-related social bond construct; analysis models TD learning signal for social inclusion as reinforcement. Construct evidence span: "social inclusion".

Although interactive, the task does not primarily measure communicative signal reception/production (e.g., faces, gaze, vocal prosody); it manipulates social inclusion probability. Construct evidence span: "social motor response learning". Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

No explicit self-referential judgments or self vs other contrast; primarily models learning about inclusion by others. Construct evidence span: "social inclusion". Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Task requires monitoring others' actions (ball throws) and learning/predicting whether others will include the participant; fits action perception/understanding others' intentions at a basic level. Construct evidence span: "viewing a social animation… interaction between three human figures".

Global exclusion: this is a paired t-test comparing tasks (Pavlovian vs Social), i.e., a between-condition contrast across tasks rather than a single-task within-group whole-brain map eligible under GLOBAL_I2. Construct evidence span: "Brain activation in Pavlovian compared with social task".
Excluded due to between-condition/task comparison (paired contrast) rather than eligible within-task whole-brain map. Construct evidence span: "Pavlovian compared with social".
Excluded due to between-condition/task comparison (paired contrast). Construct evidence span: "Pavlovian compared with social".
Excluded due to between-condition/task comparison (paired contrast). Construct evidence span: "Pavlovian compared with social".
Excluded due to between-condition/task comparison (paired contrast). Construct evidence span: "Pavlovian compared with social".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task-evoked contrast). Task is social/empathy-related involving emotion attribution to self vs baseline. Construct evidence span: "SELF versus high-level baseline".

No affiliation/attachment manipulation (bonding, kinship, loved-one vs stranger, cooperation/competition) in this contrast; it is emotion attribution from faces. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Task requires processing emotional facial expressions in an interpersonal context (evaluating/self-focusing on emotions elicited by faces), consistent with receptive facial communication. Construct evidence span: "SELF versus high-level baseline".

Explicit self-focused emotional appraisal (participants concentrate on their own feelings to faces) relative to baseline; fits self-perception/understanding. Construct evidence span: "SELF versus high-level baseline".

Although SELF condition is self-focused, it is evoked by viewing others' emotional faces and entails understanding others' affective cues; overlaps with perception/understanding of others in empathy context. Construct evidence span: "SELF versus high-level baseline".

Meets global criteria; group-level whole-brain task-evoked contrast for a social/empathy task evaluating others' emotional state from faces. Construct evidence span: "OTHER versus high-level baseline".
Contrast assesses judging emotion expressed by faces, not affiliation/attachment or bonding processes. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Directly involves receptive facial social communication (emotion recognition/characterization from faces). Construct evidence span: "OTHER versus high-level baseline".
This contrast is other-focused (evaluate emotional state expressed by faces) rather than self-judgment. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Explicit reasoning about others' emotional states (emotion attribution to the face stimulus) matches understanding mental states of others. Construct evidence span: "OTHER versus high-level baseline".

Not a group-level whole-brain univariate task-evoked map; this is a correlation/extracted-parameter analysis. Excluded by global criteria: GLOBAL_I2 not met; also reflects ROI/extraction. Construct evidence span: "Correlations between mirror neuron activation in the SELF/OTHER task and individual empathy scores (BEES)".
Correlation/ROI-style analysis; additionally not an affiliation/attachment task. Excluded by GLOBAL_E1; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1/AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Correlation/ROI-style analysis rather than whole-brain group contrast. Excluded by GLOBAL_E1; GLOBAL_I2 not met.
Correlation/ROI-style analysis rather than whole-brain group contrast. Excluded by GLOBAL_E1; GLOBAL_I2 not met.
Correlation/ROI-style analysis rather than whole-brain group contrast. Excluded by GLOBAL_E1; GLOBAL_I2 not met.
Between-group (female vs male) interaction contrast; excluded by GLOBAL_E3 despite being task-based. Construct evidence span: "Interaction (SELF_f – B_f) – (SELF_m – B_m)".
Between-group interaction excluded by GLOBAL_E3; also not affiliation/attachment-specific. Construct evidence span: "Interaction (SELF_f – B_f) – (SELF_m – B_m)".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast. Task involves recognizing self vs friend faces/names (social person-related processing). Construct evidence span: "Main effect [(Sf + Sn)–(Ff + Fn) masked by Sf–Ff and Sn–Fn]".

Contrast is self vs friend, not measuring bonding/attachment/affiliative interaction. Missing local criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "(Sf + Sn)–(Ff + Fn)".

Not a social communication contrast (no faces>nonfaces, emotion, gaze, communicative signals); it is self vs friend across stimulus types. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "(Sf + Sn)–(Ff + Fn)".
Direct self-referential processing contrast (self vs friend) across face and name modalities. Construct evidence span: "(Sf + Sn)–(Ff + Fn)".
Contrast is not about inferring others' mental states/traits; it privileges self over friend. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "(Sf + Sn)–(Ff + Fn)".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate simple effect. Self vs friend face recognition is social/person processing. Construct evidence span: "Face (simple effect) [Sf–Ff]".
Not an affiliation/attachment measure; merely compares self vs friend during face recognition. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Sf–Ff]".

Although faces are stimuli, contrast is self-face vs friend-face (identity/self-relevance), not communication cues (emotion/gaze) or faces>nonfaces. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Sf–Ff]".
Self-recognition (self-face) contrasted with friend-face indexes self-referential/self-identification processing. Construct evidence span: "[Sf–Ff]".
Does not target understanding others; it is self>friend. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Sf–Ff]".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate simple effect. Self-name vs friend-name recognition is person-related social processing. Construct evidence span: "Name (simple effect) [Sn–Fn]".
Not assessing attachment/affiliation; compares self vs friend names. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Sn–Fn]".

Written-name recognition is not a social communication task as defined (no communicative cues, emotion, gaze; not faces>nonfaces). Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Sn–Fn]".
Self-referential processing via own name vs friend name. Construct evidence span: "[Sn–Fn]".
Not designed to infer others' states/traits; self>friend. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Sn–Fn]".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate interaction identifying self-face-specific activity vs self-name. This is within social/person-related self-recognition. Construct evidence span: "Face specific (interaction) [(Sf–Ff)–(Sn–Fn) masked by Sf–Ff]".

No affiliation/attachment manipulation; interaction is about self-face specificity vs self-name. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "[(Sf–Ff)–(Sn–Fn)]".

Not a social communication contrast; it isolates self-specificity across face vs name rather than communicative cues or faces>nonfaces. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Face specific (interaction) [(Sf–Ff)–(Sn–Fn)]".
Interaction tests modality-specific self-recognition (self-face vs friend-face relative to self-name vs friend-name). Construct evidence span: "[(Sf–Ff)–(Sn–Fn) masked by Sf–Ff]".
Does not target reasoning about others; focuses on self-specificity. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "[(Sf–Ff)–(Sn–Fn)]".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate interaction aiming to isolate self-name-specific activation (self vs friend) vs face. Person-related self-processing. Construct evidence span: "Name specific (interaction) [(Sn–Fn)–(Sf–Ff) masked by Sn–Fn]".
No affiliation/attachment manipulation; contrast is self-name specificity. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "[(Sn–Fn)–(Sf–Ff)]".

Not social communication; focuses on self vs friend and modality interaction, not communicative cues. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name specific (interaction)".
Targets self-referential processing for names relative to faces via interaction. Construct evidence span: "[(Sn–Fn)–(Sf–Ff) masked by Sn–Fn]".
Does not measure understanding others; self-specificity focus. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "(Sn–Fn)".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate main effect friend>self across faces/names (person-type comparison). This is social person-related processing. Construct evidence span: "Main effect [(Ff + Fn)–(Sf + Sn) masked by Ff–Sf and Fn–Sn]".

Friend>self does not operationalize affiliation/attachment/bonding; it reflects differential processing of friend vs self during recognition. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "(Ff + Fn)–(Sf + Sn)".
Not a social communication contrast; it is friend vs self across stimulus types. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "(Ff + Fn)–(Sf + Sn)".
Self-processing is implicated via self vs friend comparison (here friend>self reflects self-specific reduction). Construct evidence span: "(Ff + Fn)–(Sf + Sn)".
Does not involve mental-state/trait reasoning about others; simply friend vs self recognition. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "friend–self".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate simple effect comparing friend-face vs self-face. Social person-related processing. Construct evidence span: "Face (simple effect) [Ff–Sf]".
Not an attachment/affiliation measure; recognition contrast friend vs self. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Ff–Sf]".
Not targeting communicative facial signals; it is identity/self-vs-friend recognition. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Ff–Sf]".
Self-recognition processing indexed via self vs friend comparison (friend>self reflects self-specific attenuation). Construct evidence span: "[Ff–Sf]".
Does not assess others' mental states; just friend-face vs self-face. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "friend–self".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate simple effect comparing friend-name vs self-name. Social person-related processing. Construct evidence span: "Name (simple effect) [Fn–Sn]".
Not measuring affiliation/attachment; it's friend-name vs self-name. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Fn–Sn]".
Written names are not a social communication task as defined; contrast is identity-related. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Fn–Sn]".
Self-related processing present via self vs friend comparison (friend>self). Construct evidence span: "[Fn–Sn]".
Not about inferring others' mental states; friend vs self recognition. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Fn–Sn]".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate interaction testing friend-face specificity relative to names. Social person-related processing. Construct evidence span: "Face specific (interaction) [(Ff–Sf)–(Fn–Sn) masked by Ff–Sf]".
No attachment/affiliation construct; modality interaction for friend>self. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "[(Ff–Sf)–(Fn–Sn)]".
Not a communication-cue contrast; it is friend>self interaction across face vs name. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Face specific (interaction)".
Indexes self-related processing differences by modality (friend>self for faces relative to names). Construct evidence span: "[(Ff–Sf)–(Fn–Sn)]".
Does not assess understanding of others; friend vs self without mentalizing. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "friend–self".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate interaction testing friend-name specificity relative to faces. Social person-related processing. Construct evidence span: "Name specific (interaction) [(Fn–Sn)–(Ff–Sf) masked by Fn–Sn]".
Not an affiliation/attachment task; modality interaction for friend>self. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "[(Fn–Sn)–(Ff–Sf)]".

Not a social communication contrast; focuses on friend>self and modality interaction. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name specific (interaction)".
Self-related processing differences by modality (friend>self for names relative to faces). Construct evidence span: "[(Fn–Sn)–(Ff–Sf) masked by Fn–Sn]".
No mental-state inference about others; friend vs self. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "friend–self".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate familiarity-dependent activation (self+friend > unfamiliar) across faces/names involves social person processing. Construct evidence span: "Main effect [(Sf + Ff + Sn + Fn)–2(Cf + Cn) masked by Sf–Cf, Ff–Cf, Sn–Cn, and Fn–Cn]".

Familiarity (self+friend) vs unfamiliar is not specifically affiliation/attachment/bonding. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "(Sf + Ff + Sn + Fn)–2(Cf + Cn)".
Not a social communication contrast; it is familiarity (person identity) across modalities. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "self and friend, relative to unfamiliar person".
Self is not isolated (self+friend combined) so the contrast does not specifically measure self-perception. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "(Sf + Ff + Sn + Fn)–2(Cf + Cn)".
Does not assess understanding of others' mental states; familiarity/identity processing only. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "friend/unfamiliar".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate face familiarity effect (self+friend > unfamiliar). Social/person processing. Construct evidence span: "Face (simple effect) [Sf + Ff–2Cf masked by Sf–Cf, and Ff–Cf]".
Familiar vs unfamiliar faces is not specifically affiliation/attachment. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Sf + Ff–2Cf]".
Not a communication-cue contrast (e.g., faces>nonfaces/emotion/gaze); it's familiarity (known vs unknown). Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Sf + Ff–2Cf]".
Self is not contrasted against other; self and friend are combined. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Sf + Ff".
Does not assess others' mental states; familiarity/identity only. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "familiarity-dependent activation".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate name familiarity effect (self+friend > unfamiliar). Social/person processing. Construct evidence span: "Name (simple effect) [Sn + Fn–2Cn masked by Sn–Cn, and Fn–Cn]".
Familiar vs unfamiliar names is not a direct attachment/affiliation measure. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "[Sn + Fn–2Cn]".
Written-name familiarity is not a social communication construct per criteria. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name (simple effect)".
Self is not isolated (self+friend combined). Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Sn + Fn".
No mentalizing/empathy; familiarity/identity only. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "familiarity-dependent".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate interaction for familiarity effect stronger for faces than names. Still social/person processing. Construct evidence span: "Face specific (interaction) [(Sf + Ff–2Cf)–(Sn + Fn–2Cn) masked by Sf + Ff–2Cf]".

Not an affiliation/attachment contrast; it's modality interaction of familiarity. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "(Sf + Ff–2Cf)–(Sn + Fn–2Cn)".
Not social communication; interaction concerns familiarity across face vs name. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Face specific (interaction)".
Self not isolated; uses self+friend combined. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Sf + Ff".
No inference about others' mental states; familiarity interaction only. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Common activation to self and friend".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate interaction for familiarity effect stronger for names than faces. Social/person processing. Construct evidence span: "Name specific (interaction) [(Sn + Fn–2Cn)–(Sf + Ff–2Cf) masked by Sn + Fn–2Cn]".
Not an attachment/affiliation contrast; modality interaction of familiarity. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "(Sn + Fn–2Cn)–(Sf + Ff–2Cf)".
Not social communication; interaction concerns familiarity across names vs faces. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Name specific (interaction)".
Self not isolated; self+friend combined. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Sn + Fn".
Does not assess others' mental states; familiarity interaction only. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "Common activation to self and friend".

Global inclusion not met: analysis metadata does not indicate a group-level whole-brain univariate map (GLOBAL_I2). Additionally, no construct-specific evidence that this table reflects an affiliation/attachment contrast. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2; AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Global inclusion not met (GLOBAL_I2): analysis/table is unnamed with no indication of a whole-brain univariate task-evoked contrast. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2; SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Global inclusion not met (GLOBAL_I2): analysis/table is unnamed with no indication of a group-level whole-brain univariate task-evoked contrast. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2; PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Global inclusion not met: GLOBAL_I2 requires a group-level, univariate whole-brain task-evoked statistical map, but this analysis is not described (table caption is just ")") so we cannot confirm whole-brain voxelwise task contrast. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2; SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.

Global inclusion not met (GLOBAL_I2): analysis/table lacks sufficient description to verify a group-level whole-brain univariate map; also no construct-specific evidence for affiliation/attachment. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2; AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

It is also a brain–behavior covariate analysis tied to in-group–out-group difference. Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2; SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.

Excluded: analysis is a whole-brain correlation with cooperation difference score rather than a group-level task-evoked contrast map (fails GLOBAL_I2). Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2; AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Excluded: analysis is a whole-brain correlation with a behavioral covariate, not a group-level task-evoked contrast map (fails GLOBAL_I2). Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2; SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Excluded: analysis is a brain–behavior correlation (ANCOVA) on in-group–out-group difference, not a group-level task-evoked contrast map (fails GLOBAL_I2). Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2; PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Excluded: analysis is a whole-brain covariate correlation ("cooperation difference score" with in-group–out-group BOLD difference), not a group-level task-evoked contrast map (fails GLOBAL_I2). Missing inclusion criteria: GLOBAL_I2; PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adult sample and group-level whole-brain task-evoked effects from a social interaction simulation involving peers/friends vs celebrities; thus captures social processing broadly. Construct evidence span: "social interaction simulation task".
Task operationalizes close social bonds (friends/positive peers) vs other relationship types, aligning with affiliation/attachment and bonding-related processing. Construct evidence span: "friends".

Although face stimuli are used, the reported whole-brain results are not a contrast isolating communicative signal processing (e.g., faces>nonfaces, gaze, expression), and analysis description is too generic (<0.05 cluster corrected). Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No explicit self-referential judgments or self vs other contrasts; task focuses on reactions to others (peers/celebrities). Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Participants evaluate and respond to other people (peers/celebrities) with affective valence and relationship context, engaging perception/understanding of others. Construct evidence span: "peers vs. familiar celebrities".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast). Task is explicitly social/communicative speech. Construct evidence span: "Communication—control".

Global criteria met, but this contrast is not designed to isolate affiliation/attachment bonding or relationship formation; it is primarily communicative speech vs control, and familiarity/attachment is not contrasted here. Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communication—control".
Global criteria met. Communication task involves production of socially directed speech to an actor (non-facial communication) vs control naming. Construct evidence span: "Communication—control".

Global criteria met, but the contrast does not involve self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge manipulation. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communication—control".

Global criteria met. Communication to an actor while observing their actions engages understanding others in an interaction context (mentalizing/other-oriented social processing) relative to non-social control. Construct evidence span: "Communication—control".

Global criteria met. Description task involves observing an actor’s action/situation and verbally describing it, which includes social/action understanding components. Construct evidence span: "Description—control".

Global criteria met, but no affiliation/attachment manipulation (e.g., loved one vs stranger as primary construct); this is description of actions vs control. Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Description—control".

Global criteria met, but this is descriptive speech (narration) rather than socially directed communicative exchange; does not primarily measure social communication. Missing local inclusion: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Description—control".
Global criteria met, but no self-focused judgment/agency manipulation. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Description—control".
Global criteria met. Describing an observed actor’s action/situation requires action perception/understanding others relative to control naming. Construct evidence span: "Description—control".

Global criteria met. Contrast isolates communicative speech production (social interaction context) vs description, capturing social interaction/mentalizing components. Construct evidence span: "Communication— description".

Global criteria met, but this contrast does not target bonding/affiliation; it contrasts communication vs description irrespective of familiarity. Missing local inclusion: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communication— description".

Global criteria met. Contrast specifically targets socially appropriate communicative speech vs descriptive speech (production of non-facial social communication). Construct evidence span: "Communication— description".

Global criteria met, but no self-referential processing manipulation; difference is communicative intent toward other vs description. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Communication— description".

Global criteria met. Communication vs description is intended to isolate interaction-relevant processing/mentalizing about the interlocutor (context of social interaction) beyond action description. Construct evidence span: "Communication— description".

Global criteria met. Contrast manipulates familiarity of communication partner during communicative speech, a social processing manipulation. Construct evidence span: "Familiar communication – unfamiliar communication".

Global criteria met. Contrast directly tests familiarity (close friend vs unfamiliar) during communication, aligning with attachment/affiliation related social bond cues. Construct evidence span: "Familiar communication – unfamiliar communication".

Global criteria met. Contrast is within communicative speech production and varies social partner familiarity, a key dimension of social communication context. Construct evidence span: "Familiar communication – unfamiliar communication".

Global criteria met, but familiarity modulation here concerns partner/biographical info about the other rather than explicit self-referential judgment. Missing local inclusion: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Familiar communication – unfamiliar communication".

Global criteria met. Familiar vs unfamiliar interlocutor during communication requires accessing/using biographical knowledge and mental models of the other. Construct evidence span: "Familiar communication – unfamiliar communication".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate task-evoked map) and this condition is embedded in a real social interaction involving an opponent administering punishment. Construct evidence span: "watching adjustment of stimulus intensity by the opponent".

Although the task is competitive, this analysis targets anticipation of being punished (pain/threat) rather than bonding/affiliation or attachment-related processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

This analysis is not about decoding communicative signals (faces/voices/gaze/gestures); it is anticipation of aversive stimulation while viewing a scale allegedly set by the opponent. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Not a self-referential judgment/agency contrast; it primarily reflects anticipation of punishment rather than explicit self-knowledge or self vs other evaluation. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

While an opponent is present, the contrast does not involve reasoning about the opponent's mental/emotional state; it is observing punishment intensity to be received. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Meets global criteria and captures interactive retaliation toward an opponent (social interaction/decision to harm another). Construct evidence span: "subjects adjusted the scale for the stimulus intensity they intended to administer to his opponent".

Task is competitive retaliation rather than affiliation/attachment formation or positive bonding (no cooperation/close relationship contrast). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

No explicit communicative cue processing (faces/voices/gaze) is contrasted; main demand is selecting punishment intensity. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Although it involves choosing an action, it is not a self-referential appraisal (self-judgment/self vs other) and the map reflects retaliation intensity modulation. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Primary process is action selection/retaliation; it does not require inferring opponent mental states or empathic understanding during this phase. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Meets global criteria and the conjunction isolates shared activity across retaliation and watching opponent suffer—both explicitly social-interactive conditions. Construct evidence span: "Conjunction; retaliation and watching the opponent".
Conjunction concerns retaliation and observing suffering (competition/aggression), not affiliation/attachment or positive bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Although watching the opponent involves visual observation, this conjunction is not a communication-specific contrast (e.g., faces>nonfaces) and includes retaliation/decision components. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Conjunction does not isolate self-referential appraisal/agency vs other; it mixes retaliation and observation and lacks a self-focused contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Watching the opponent suffer entails perception/understanding of another’s affective state (empathy-related), and the conjunction includes this component as shared social processing. Construct evidence span: "watching the opponent".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast) and involves social cognition during face perception/person knowledge retrieval. Construct evidence span: "faces associated with behaviors evoked a stronger response than novel faces".

No evidence the contrast measures affiliation/attachment, bonding, or close relationship processes; it is person-knowledge/valence retrieval from learned behaviors. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Face perception task (one-back on faces) with learned social/affective person knowledge modulating responses; qualifies as receptive facial social communication processing. Construct evidence span: "faces associated with behaviors versus novel faces".
No self-referential judgment or self vs other contrast; stimuli are unfamiliar others’ faces with behaviors. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Contrast reflects retrieval/representation of others’ traits/affective person knowledge during face perception (thinking about others). Construct evidence span: "regions implicated in social cognition—anterior paracingulate cortex and superior temporal sulcus".

Meets global criteria and tests differential responses to faces based on associated negative trait content (disgusting vs aggressive), engaging social/emotion processing in person perception. Construct evidence span: "faces associated with disgusting behaviors evoked a stronger response ... than faces associated with aggressive behaviors".
Disgust vs aggression trait associations do not index affiliation/attachment or bonding/relationship formation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Uses viewing/recognizing faces (receptive facial information) with social affective knowledge modulating neural response; fits social communication reception domain. Construct evidence span: "faces associated with disgusting behaviors versus faces associated with aggressive behaviors".
No self-focused processing required; contrast is between categories of others’ faces. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Directly contrasts trait/affective knowledge about other people (disgusting vs aggressive behaviors) during face perception—representation of others’ traits. Construct evidence span: "disgusting behaviors ... aggressive behaviors".

Meets global criteria and examines social/affective person-knowledge modulation during face perception (aggressive > disgusting). Construct evidence span: "Faces associated with aggressive behaviors greater than faces associated with disgusting behaviors".
Aggression-vs-disgust trait contrast is not about affiliation/attachment or social bonding. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Face-viewing/recognition task with socially meaningful trait knowledge contrasts; receptive facial social communication is engaged. Construct evidence span: "faces associated with aggressive behaviors ... faces associated with disgusting behaviors".
No self-referential component or self-related contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

Contrast differentiates affective trait knowledge about others (aggressive vs disgusting) during face perception, indexing understanding/representation of others’ traits. Construct evidence span: "aggressive behaviors greater than ... disgusting behaviors".

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast Aa–As), but this is a sensorimotor/agency mismatch task rather than a social-related task; does not measure Social Processing. Missing: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.

Global criteria met, but task/contrast is visuo-motor synchrony/ownership detection and does not assess affiliation, bonding, cooperation/competition, or close-relationship constructs. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global criteria met, but no communicative stimuli (faces, voices, gaze, gestures for communication) or communication judgments; contrast is asynchronous vs synchronous cursor feedback. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: analysis indexes sense of agency/body ownership via detecting whether visual cursor is feedback from one’s own movement, contrasting asynchronous vs synchronous feedback during active movement. Construct evidence span: "Active conditions" / "activity increase in the asynchronous compared with the synchronous condition".

Global criteria met, but contrast targets self-related agency/ownership error (self vs non-self feedback) without representing/inferring another agent’s mental state or traits; not an 'others' perception task. Missing: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Global criteria met (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast Pa–Ps), but task is visuo-proprioceptive synchrony/ownership detection, not social information processing. Missing: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1, SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.

Global criteria met, but no affiliation/attachment manipulation or bonding-related judgments; contrast is asynchronous vs synchronous visual feedback during passive movement. Missing: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global criteria met, but no communicative content or interaction (faces, speech, gaze, gestures); contrast is cursor synchrony during passive movement. Missing: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: analysis probes self-ownership/agency-related monitoring by judging whether cursor movement is feedback from one’s own (passively moved) finger, contrasting asynchronous vs synchronous feedback. Construct evidence span: "Passive conditions" / "activity increase in the asynchronous compared with the synchronous condition".
Global criteria met, but the task does not require inferring others’ actions or mental states; it's self vs non-self feedback attribution. Missing: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast. Measures processing of socially relevant facial expressions vs arbitrary movements. Construct evidence span: "Common activations of social interaction (SOC > ARB)".

Contrast is about decoding socially relevant facial expressions, not bonding/attachment, kinship, cooperation/competition, or relationship-specific affiliation. Missing local criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Task involves receptive social signals (facial expressions) used to initiate contact; contrast isolates socially relevant facial communication. Construct evidence span: "social interaction (SOC > ARB)".
Not a self>other or self-judgment/agency contrast; it collapses across addressee and targets social content in expressions. Missing local criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Involves perceiving/understanding others' socially meaningful communicative intent from facial cues (mental-state/social meaning decoding). Construct evidence span: "social interaction (SOC > ARB)".

Although whole-brain in healthy adults, this contrast targets arbitrary/meaningless facial movements (ARB > SOC), emphasizing nonsocial visual motion/face processing rather than social processing. Missing SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I1 and SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
No affiliation/attachment manipulation (bonding, kinship, cooperation/competition). Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Arbitrary facial movements are explicitly socially irrelevant; does not measure social communication. Construct evidence span: "arbitrary facial movements (ARB > SOC)". Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-referential judgment or self vs other contrast. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Does not assess others’ mental states/traits; focuses on nonsocial motion/face features. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast. Manipulates self-involvement via direct gaze (ME > OTHER), a core social interaction cue. Construct evidence span: "Common activations of self-involvement (ME > OTHER)".

Direct gaze/self-involvement is not an affiliation/attachment or bonding manipulation (no close relationship, cooperation, or attachment context). Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Direct vs averted gaze indexes receptive nonverbal social communication (eye contact/ostensive signal) independent of expression type. Construct evidence span: "self-involvement (ME > OTHER)".
Contrast operationalizes self-relevance/personal involvement (being looked at) vs observing others; aligns with self-related processing. Construct evidence span: "self-involvement (ME > OTHER)".
Primary manipulation is self-directedness rather than reasoning about others’ mental states/traits; no explicit ToM/trait inference. Missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast. Observing other-directed gaze/interaction (OTHER > ME) is social perception of interaction not involving self. Construct evidence span: "Common activations of other-related activity (OTHER > ME)".
No bonding/attachment or cooperative/competitive relationship content; just gaze direction. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Contrast isolates perception of gaze/communicative direction toward another person (receptive nonverbal communication). Construct evidence span: "OTHER > ME".
This is other-directed vs self-directed; not self-focused processing. Missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Requires representing others as interaction partners and tracking other-directed signals (third-person perspective). Construct evidence span: "other-related activity (OTHER > ME)".
This is a factorial interaction contrast (SOC × ME), which is excluded by global criteria as an interaction. Excluded per GLOBAL_E3.
Excluded because this is an interaction contrast (SOC × ME).

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast isolating ToM. Construct evidence span: "‘ToM’ - ‘Physical 1’".

No affiliation/attachment construct (bonding, cooperation/competition, kinship, loved-one vs stranger) is manipulated; contrast is intention attribution (ToM) vs physical causality. Construct evidence span: "‘ToM’ - ‘Physical 1’". Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Not a social communication task (no faces/gaze/communicative signal processing); participants infer intentions from cartoons. Construct evidence span: "‘ToM’ - ‘Physical 1’". Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

No self-referential judgment/agency/self vs other contrast; focus is on others' intentions. Construct evidence span: "‘ToM’ - ‘Physical 1’". Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Global criteria met; ToM/mentalizing requires reasoning about others' intentions/mental states. Construct evidence span: "‘ToM’ - ‘Physical 1’".
Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast isolating empathy processing. Construct evidence span: "‘Empathy’ - ‘Physical 2’".

Empathy for a protagonist is not an affiliation/attachment manipulation (no bonding/relationship closeness/kinship/cooperation vs competition). Construct evidence span: "‘Empathy’ - ‘Physical 2’". Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Task is not focused on decoding communicative signals (faces/gaze/prosody/gestures); it is empathy inference from story context. Construct evidence span: "‘Empathy’ - ‘Physical 2’". Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

No self-focused evaluation or self vs other comparison; question targets protagonist feelings. Construct evidence span: "‘Empathy’ - ‘Physical 2’". Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Global criteria met; empathy condition requires inferring another individual's emotional state. Construct evidence span: "‘Empathy’ - ‘Physical 2’".
Global criteria met; conjunction captures common whole-brain activations for ToM and empathy—both social-cognitive processes. Construct evidence span: "Conjuction analysis".

Conjunction is for ToM and empathy vs physical causality controls; no affiliation/attachment manipulation present. Construct evidence span: "Conjuction analysis". Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Conjunction does not isolate communication signal processing; it reflects mentalizing/empathy inference from cartoons. Construct evidence span: "Conjuction analysis". Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

No self-referential processing contrast; conjunction pertains to understanding others. Construct evidence span: "Conjuction analysis". Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Global criteria met; conjunction reflects shared neural correlates of understanding others' intentions and emotions. Construct evidence span: "Conjuction analysis".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast of emotional prosody. Construct evidence span: "Anger vs. Neutral (AN + NA) vs. (NN)".

No affiliation/attachment construct (bonding, loved one, cooperation/competition) is measured; task is emotion prosody perception/attention. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Task involves receptive decoding of socially relevant auditory affect (anger prosody in voices). Construct evidence span: "Anger vs. Neutral (AN + NA) vs. (NN)".
No self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge manipulation; participants judge speaker gender while hearing prosody. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Contrast indexes perceiving others' emotional state cues (anger vs neutral prosody) in voices. Construct evidence span: "Anger vs. Neutral (AN + NA) vs. (NN)".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast examining angry prosody when it is task-relevant. Construct evidence span: "Anger to-be-attended vs. Neutral".
No affiliation/attachment construct is manipulated (no bonding/kinship/cooperation). Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Receptive social communication: decoding affective prosody in voices (anger) under attention. Construct evidence span: "Anger to-be-attended vs. Neutral".
Contrast does not involve self-related evaluation; it is about stimulus prosody and attention. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Anger prosody is a cue to others' emotional state; contrast isolates perceiving anger (attended) vs neutral. Construct evidence span: "Anger to-be-attended vs. Neutral".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast examining angry prosody even when to-be-ignored. Construct evidence span: "Anger to-be-ignored vs. Neutral".
No attachment/affiliation manipulation present. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Receptive processing of vocal affective cues (anger prosody) constitutes social communication, despite being task-irrelevant. Construct evidence span: "Anger to-be-ignored vs. Neutral".
No self-referential component; contrast concerns processing others' vocal cues outside attention. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Anger prosody provides information about another individual's affect; contrast tests processing of that cue when ignored. Construct evidence span: "Anger to-be-ignored vs. Neutral".

Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast testing attention modulation of emotional prosody (socially salient cue). Construct evidence span: "Anger to-be-attended vs. to-be-ignored".
Does not assess bonding/attachment/affiliation; it is attention modulation of anger prosody. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Contrast targets differential receptive processing of affective prosody (anger) depending on attention. Construct evidence span: "Anger to-be-attended vs. to-be-ignored".
No explicit self-evaluation/agency manipulation; contrast is about attended vs ignored vocal emotion. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Indexes how strongly participants process others' anger cues when attended vs ignored. Construct evidence span: "Anger to-be-attended vs. to-be-ignored".
Healthy adults; whole-brain group contrast is the inverse attention modulation of angry prosody (ignored > attended). Construct evidence span: "Anger to-be-ignored vs. to-be-attended".
No affiliation/attachment component. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Still a social communication contrast about receptive processing of anger prosody under differing attention demands (inverse direction). Construct evidence span: "Anger to-be-ignored vs. to-be-attended".
No self-related judgment/mentalizing. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Compares processing of others' anger cues when ignored versus attended (still about others' emotion perception). Construct evidence span: "Anger to-be-ignored vs. to-be-attended".

Global criteria met, but this contrast is spatial attention (right vs left ear) independent of emotional prosody, so it does not measure social processing. Missing local criteria: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
Spatial attention contrast does not assess affiliation/attachment. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Contrast is auditory spatial attention (right vs left), not communication-specific processing of social cues. Missing local criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-referential processing; only spatial attention. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
No explicit perception/understanding of others manipulated; contrast is spatial attention irrespective of emotion. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.
Global criteria met, but contrast is spatial attention (left vs right ear) not measuring social processing per se. Missing local criteria: SOCIAL_PROCESSING_ALL_I2.
No affiliation/attachment construct. Missing local criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Spatial attention contrast does not isolate processing of vocal affect/communication cues. Missing local criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
No self-related task component. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Not about others' states/traits; just attentional orienting. Missing local criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adults and group-level whole-brain task contrast (distracter/unknown faces vs scrambled/null) assessing face perception, a core social-information processing component. Construct evidence span: "distracter minus null contrast".

Face-perception contrast does not probe bonding/attachment, cooperation/competition, or loved-one/relationship valuation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "distracter minus null contrast" indicates generic face viewing.
Whole-brain task-evoked contrast involves viewing faces vs scrambled control, matching receptive facial communication processing. Construct evidence span: "distracter minus null contrast".

No self-related manipulation; contrast is unknown/distracter faces vs scrambled baseline. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "distracter minus null contrast".

Although faces are 'others', the contrast targets perceptual face processing rather than reasoning about others' traits/mental states/empathy. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "distracter minus null contrast".
Healthy adults; whole-brain familiar vs unknown face recognition contrast probes socially relevant person/familiarity processing. Construct evidence span: "familiar minus distracter contrast".

Personally familiar (fraternity brother) vs unknown includes familiarity but is not designed to measure attachment/affiliation bonding (no relationship/attachment evaluation or loved-one vs stranger as affiliative construct). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "familiar minus distracter contrast".
Contrast involves perception/recognition of familiar faces vs unfamiliar faces, fitting receptive facial communication processing. Construct evidence span: "familiar minus distracter contrast".

No self condition in this contrast; it is familiar other vs unknown other. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "familiar minus distracter contrast".

Does not require inferring others’ mental states/traits; it is primarily face familiarity discrimination. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "familiar minus distracter contrast".
Healthy adults; whole-brain self vs unknown face contrast is a social-cognitive/self-recognition task component within social processing. Construct evidence span: "self minus distracter contrast".

Self vs unknown does not assess affiliative bonding/attachment constructs. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "self minus distracter contrast".

Includes viewing/recognizing a face stimulus (self face) vs other face category, aligning with receptive facial communication/face recognition processes. Construct evidence span: "self minus distracter contrast".
Directly contrasts viewing/identifying one’s own face vs unknown faces, measuring self-recognition/self-knowledge. Construct evidence span: "self minus distracter contrast".

Primary manipulation is self-recognition rather than understanding others’ mental states/traits. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "self minus distracter contrast".

Healthy adults; whole-brain contrast between familiar other and self engages socially relevant person identity processing (self/other discrimination). Construct evidence span: "familiar minus self contrast".

Although the familiar face is personally familiar, the contrast is not an affiliation/attachment task (no bonding/relationship appraisal; focuses on identity discrimination). Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "familiar minus self contrast".
Face recognition/discrimination (familiar face vs self face) is receptive facial communication processing. Construct evidence span: "familiar minus self contrast".
Self is explicitly part of the contrast (other > self), still indexing self-referential face processing via comparison to self-face. Construct evidence span: "familiar minus self contrast".

Does not involve inferring others’ mental states/traits; it is perceptual/identity discrimination. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "familiar minus self contrast".

Healthy adults; whole-brain self vs familiar other contrast targets self-recognition relative to a socially relevant other, within social processing. Construct evidence span: "self minus familiar contrast".

Contrast is self vs familiar identity discrimination, not attachment/affiliation bonding or close-relationship evaluation. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "self minus familiar contrast".
Involves discriminating between two face identities (self vs familiar), fitting receptive facial communication/face recognition. Construct evidence span: "self minus familiar contrast".
Direct self > other (personally familiar) contrast measures self-face recognition/self-referential processing. Construct evidence span: "self minus familiar contrast".

Task does not require reasoning about others’ internal states; focuses on self vs familiar identity recognition. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2. Construct evidence span: "self minus familiar contrast".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults and a group-level whole-brain conjunction identifying regions commonly activated during moral emotion evaluation (guilt and embarrassment). Construct evidence span: "Brain regions commonly activated by guilt and embarrassment conditions".

Global criteria are met, but this analysis does not specifically measure affiliation/attachment (bonding, kinship, loved-one/close relationship, cooperation/competition). Construct evidence span: "commonly activated by guilt and embarrassment conditions". Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Global criteria are met, but the task is not a social communication paradigm (e.g., faces/gaze/affective prosody/communicative gestures); it is evaluative judgment of moral emotions from sentences. Construct evidence span: "guilt and embarrassment conditions". Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: sentences are written in first person and involve self-conscious moral emotions requiring self-evaluation/judgment about one's own actions/feelings (guilt/embarrassment). Construct evidence span: "guilt and embarrassment".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: evaluative processing of self-conscious emotions is explicitly framed as requiring representation of others' evaluations/mental states (ToM), supporting understanding others in social context. Construct evidence span: "guilt and embarrassment conditions".
