Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast of social interaction (cooperation) vs nonsocial control (independent play). Construct evidence span: "Cooperation versus independent".
Cooperation task indexes prosocial affiliation-related interaction relative to independent play. Construct evidence span: "Cooperation versus independent".

Contrast is about cooperative interaction/stance, not communicative signal reception/production (e.g., faces, gaze, vocal). Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Cooperation versus independent".

Although discussion mentions agency, the contrast is not designed to isolate self-agency/self-judgment (e.g., Self>Other); it primarily manipulates social interaction mode. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Cooperation versus independent".

Cooperation requires monitoring/anticipating partner’s actions toward shared goal, engaging representations of the other relative to independent play. Construct evidence span: "Cooperation versus independent".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain task contrast of social interaction (competition) vs nonsocial control (independent play). Construct evidence span: "Competition versus independent".
Competition is explicitly listed as an affiliation/attachment-relevant social interaction manipulation vs independent play. Construct evidence span: "Competition versus independent".

No manipulation of communicative cue processing (faces/gaze/vocal) relative to control; focuses on competitive stance. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Competition versus independent".

Not a self-referential/agency-isolating contrast (no Self>Other/self-judgment); primarily social interaction mode. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Competition versus independent".
Competition requires reasoning about an opponent’s actions/intentions relative to independent play (mentalizing/other-monitoring). Construct evidence span: "Competition versus independent".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast directly comparing two social interaction modes (cooperation vs competition). Construct evidence span: "Cooperation versus competition".

Contrast targets differential neural response for cooperative (affiliative) vs competitive stance; cooperation is central to affiliation/attachment construct examples. Construct evidence span: "Cooperation versus competition".

Direct comparison of cooperation vs competition does not isolate communication cue processing/production. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Cooperation versus competition".

Comparison of social stances is not an explicit self-perception/agency contrast (e.g., Self>Other); any agency content is indirect. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Cooperation versus competition".

Both conditions involve representing another person; the contrast captures changes in other-oriented processing between cooperative vs competitive interaction. Construct evidence span: "Cooperation versus competition".
Healthy adults; group-level whole-brain contrast directly comparing competitive vs cooperative social interaction. Construct evidence span: "Competition versus cooperation".

Contrast isolates competitive vs cooperative interaction mode, explicitly within the affiliation/attachment task examples (cooperation and competition). Construct evidence span: "Competition versus cooperation".

No social communication (faces/gaze/vocal/gesture) processing contrast; focuses on competitive stance vs cooperative stance. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Competition versus cooperation".

Not an explicit self-focused task/contrast; although self–other distinction is discussed, it is not operationalized as self-perception contrast. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Competition versus cooperation".

Competitive stance increases mentalizing/monitoring of opponent relative to cooperation per study framing; thus the contrast indexes other-understanding differences. Construct evidence span: "Competition versus cooperation".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: healthy adults and a group-level whole-brain univariate contrast. The contrast targets processing of observed social interactions/relations vs a single person acting alone. Construct evidence span: "Relational vs. alone segment".

Global criteria are met, but this contrast is not specifically about bonding/attachment/affiliation (no loved-one/kinship/cooperation manipulation); it is broadly social interaction perception. Missing local inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1, AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "Relational vs. alone segment".

Global criteria are met, but the contrast is not designed to isolate social communication (e.g., faces>nonfaces, gaze, emotion recognition); it compares interaction vs alone and mixes many perceptual/cognitive components. Missing local inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Relational vs. alone segment".

Global criteria are met, but there is no self-referential judgment/agency/self-knowledge manipulation; participants passively watch clips. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Relational vs. alone segment".

Global and local inclusion criteria are met: participants observe and interpret others' actions/social interactions (understanding others in a relational context) contrasted with a single actor alone. Construct evidence span: "Relational vs. alone segment".

Healthy adults performing a social causal attribution task; whole-brain internal vs external attribution contrast indexes social processing (attributing responsibility for social events). Construct evidence span: "Internal > external".

Although scenarios involve friends, this contrast targets causal responsibility (internal vs external) rather than bonding/attachment per se; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Task involves reading/imaging social events and making attribution judgments, not communicative signal reception/production (e.g., faces, gaze, gestures); missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Internal (self-responsibility) vs external attributions directly engage agency/self-responsibility judgments about oneself as causal agent. Construct evidence span: "Internal > external".
This contrast emphasizes self-responsibility (internal) rather than representing others’ traits/mental states; missing PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Whole-brain contrast during a social attribution task; external > internal reflects attributing responsibility to others/situations, a core social cognition operation. Construct evidence span: "External > internal".
Contrast concerns causal attribution to others/situations, not attachment/affiliation bonding constructs; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
No explicit social communication cue processing/production; it is responsibility attribution. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
External > internal downweights self-focused agency and instead focuses on others/situation responsibility; missing PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.

External attributions require representing others (or situational factors) as causal agents in social events, aligning with understanding others’ intentions/agency. Construct evidence span: "External > internal".

Self-serving bias contrast combines internal attribution of positive events with external attribution of negative events, a motivated social attribution style; analyzed with whole-brain voxelwise map in healthy adults. Construct evidence span: "Self-serving bias".
Bias relates to self-esteem/motivated attribution, not relationship bonding/attachment; missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
No social cue communication reception/production; it is causal attribution bias. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Self-serving bias includes internal attributions for positive events (credit to self) and thus engages self-evaluative agency/self-knowledge processes. Construct evidence span: "Self-serving bias".
Self-serving bias includes external attributions for negative events (blame to others/situation), requiring representation of others as causal agents. Construct evidence span: "Self-serving bias".

Non-self-serving bias combines internal attribution of negative events and external attribution of positive events within social scenarios; whole-brain voxelwise analysis in healthy adults. Construct evidence span: "Non-self-serving bias".
Contrast reflects attributional style rather than affiliation/attachment bonding. Missing AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.
Not a social communication paradigm (faces/gaze/gestures); it is causal attribution bias. Missing SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1 and SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2.
Non-self-serving bias includes internal attributions for negative events (self-blame), directly engaging self-related agency/self-evaluation. Construct evidence span: "Non-self-serving bias".

Non-self-serving bias includes external attributions for positive events (credit to others/situation), involving representation of others as causal agents in social events. Construct evidence span: "Non-self-serving bias".

Healthy adults and group-level whole-brain univariate task contrast. Task is self vs familiar other face recognition, a social-cognitive/self-other discrimination paradigm. Construct evidence span: "self–other contrast".

Involves receptive facial communication processing (viewing and categorizing faces), with whole-brain self vs other face contrast. Construct evidence span: "self–other contrast".
Directly measures self-perception via recognizing one’s own face relative to a familiar other. Construct evidence span: "self–other contrast".

This contrast isolates self > other (reduced other component), so it is not primarily measuring understanding/representation of others. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1, PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Healthy adults and group-level whole-brain univariate task contrast. Contrast compares familiar other vs self face judgments, a social-cognitive/self-other discrimination task. Construct evidence span: "other–self contrast".

Receptive facial processing task (viewing and categorizing faces) with whole-brain contrast other vs self. Construct evidence span: "other–self contrast".
This contrast emphasizes other > self rather than self-referential processing. Missing local inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
Measures perception/representation of a familiar other relative to self (other-face recognition component). Construct evidence span: "other–self contrast".

Group-level whole-brain activation during explicit facial emotion discrimination, a core receptive facial communication task (affect recognition). Construct evidence span: "explicit emotion recognition (EMO)".

Explicitly requires inferring others' emotional states from facial expressions (emotion discrimination), fitting perception/understanding of others' affect. Construct evidence span: "explicit emotion recognition (EMO)".

Meets global criteria: healthy adults and group-level univariate whole-brain task-evoked map for implicit face processing during age discrimination (faces with emotional expressions). This constitutes social-related processing of faces. Construct evidence span: "implicit face processing (AGE)".
Implicit age discrimination of faces does not probe affiliation/attachment or relationship bonding; missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Implicit processing of facial stimuli (age discrimination) is a receptive facial-communication/face-perception task (viewing faces and extracting socially relevant cues). Construct evidence span: "implicit face processing (AGE)".
No self-referential component (not self judgments or self vs other); missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
AGE analysis is age-decade judgment and does not require representing others' mental/emotional states; missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I1 and PERCEPTION_OTHERS_I2.

Construct evidence span: "direct comparison between explicit and implicit processing (EMO-AGE)".

Contrast compares emotion recognition vs age discrimination and does not assess attachment/affiliation constructs; missing local inclusion criteria AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I1 and AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2.

Contrast isolates increased activation for explicit facial emotion recognition relative to incidental face processing, directly tied to receptive facial communication. Construct evidence span: "EMO-AGE".
No self-related judgments; missing local inclusion criteria PERCEPTION_SELF_I1 and PERCEPTION_SELF_I2.
EMO-AGE emphasizes explicit inference/labeling of others' emotions from faces vs age judgment, fitting perception/understanding of others' affect. Construct evidence span: "EMO-AGE".

Meets global criteria (healthy adults; group-level whole-brain univariate contrast) and the task is explicitly social interaction (cooperation vs competition). Construct evidence span: "Cooperation > competition".

Cooperation vs competition directly indexes affiliative/prosocial vs antagonistic exchange in a dyadic interactive task, aligning with affiliation/attachment domain examples. Construct evidence span: "Cooperation > competition".

No explicit manipulation/measurement of communicative signals (e.g., facial/gaze/verbal/nonverbal communication) in this contrast; it targets cooperation vs competition in a token-placement game. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Cooperation > competition".

Contrast is not self-referential/agency/self-knowledge (no Self > Other or self-judgment component). Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Cooperation > competition".

Participants’ brain responses are modeled as reactions to their partner’s moves (iBBD), requiring tracking/understanding of the other’s actions/intentions; cooperation vs competition modulates this other-oriented processing. Construct evidence span: "Cooperation > competition".
Meets global criteria and probes social interaction dimension (competition relative to cooperation) within dyadic exchange. Construct evidence span: "Competition > cooperation".

Competition (vs cooperation) is a core social-exchange manipulation relevant to affiliation/attachment-related social comparison/prosocial vs antisocial contexts. Construct evidence span: "Competition > cooperation".

No communicative cue processing/manipulation is specified; the contrast is about competitive vs cooperative goal structure in gameplay. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "Competition > cooperation".

No self-referential judgment/agency contrast is present; it varies interaction goal (competition vs cooperation). Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "Competition > cooperation".

iBBD models neural responses to the co-player’s actions; competitive context heightens monitoring/prediction of the opponent, fitting perception/understanding of others. Construct evidence span: "Competition > cooperation".
Meets global criteria; compares interaction structure (concurrent vs turn-based) during real-time dyadic exchange, a social interaction manipulation. Construct evidence span: "CN > TB".

This contrast targets interaction structure (concurrent vs turn-based) regardless of cooperative/competitive valence; it does not specifically measure affiliative bonding/attachment/prosociality. Missing inclusion criteria: AFFILIATION_ATTACHMENT_I2. Construct evidence span: "CN > TB".

Although it is an interaction-structure manipulation, there is no explicit communication modality (faces/gaze/verbal/gestural signaling) being measured; it is primarily action coordination/turn-taking. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "CN > TB".

No self-focused appraisal/agency/self-knowledge contrast; it varies concurrent vs turn-based interaction. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "CN > TB".

Concurrent (vs turn-based) exchange increases need to track/predict partner actions in real time; iBBD is defined as response to co-player moves, aligning with action/mental-state understanding of others. Construct evidence span: "CN > TB".

Meets global criteria; interaction examines how goal-structure effect (cooperation>competition) differs by interaction structure (TB vs CN) within a social exchange task, still indexing social processing. Construct evidence span: "TB (COO > COM) > CN (COO > COM)".

This contrast concerns cooperation-vs-competition differences (an affiliation-related manipulation) and how they depend on turn-taking vs concurrent structure. Construct evidence span: "TB (COO > COM) > CN (COO > COM)".

No explicit communicative signal processing is manipulated; contrast is about cooperation/competition effect size across interaction structures. Missing inclusion criteria: SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I1, SOCIAL_COMMUNICATION_I2. Construct evidence span: "TB (COO > COM) > CN (COO > COM)".

Does not isolate self-referential/agency processing (no Self > Other/self-judgment); it is an interaction-of-conditions within dyadic exchange. Missing inclusion criteria: PERCEPTION_SELF_I1, PERCEPTION_SELF_I2. Construct evidence span: "TB (COO > COM) > CN (COO > COM)".

Because iBBD responses are defined relative to partner’s preceding moves, and this contrast modulates that by cooperation vs competition across TB vs CN, it indexes partner-focused action/intent monitoring. Construct evidence span: "TB (COO > COM) > CN (COO > COM)".

Excluded because this is an interaction/between-condition comparison across roles (Builder vs Other), which is not a simple within-group main effect map and falls under between-group/interaction exclusion.
